questions.title: JEE Advanced 2025 Paper 1 Online
questions.question: Regarding the molecular orbital (MO) energy levels for homonuclear diatomic molecules, the INCORRECT statement(s) is(are)
options: The highest occupied molecular orbital (HOMO) of F2 is $\sigma$-type.
Bond energy of $O_2^{+}$ is smaller than the bond energy of O2.
Bond length of Li2 is larger than the bond length of B2.
questions.solution:
Molecular orbital (MO) energy levels for homonuclear diatomic molecules:
For Molecular orbital theory, all the valence atomic orbitals are included. The total number of atomic orbitals must always equal the total number of molecular orbitals. Bonding molecular orbitals are more stable than the atomic orbitals and increase the electron density between the nuclei Antibonding molecular orbitals are. less stable than the atomic orbitals and decrease the electron density between the nuclei.
(A) Bond older can be calculated using the formula:
Bond order $=\frac{1}{2}\left(N_b-N_a\right)$
$N_b$ - Number of bonding electrons
$\mathrm{N_a}^{\text { }}$ - Number of antibonding electrons
For $\mathrm{Ne}_2$, total number of electrons $=20 \quad\left\{\begin{array}{l}\text { Number of electrons } \\ \text { for } \mathrm{Ne}=10\end{array}\right.$

The total electrons are filled in the molecular orbitals as,
$${\sigma _{1{s^2}}}\,\sigma _{1{s^2}}^ * \,{\sigma _{2{s^2}}}\,\sigma _{2{s^2}}^ * \,{\sigma _{2p_z^2}}\,{\pi _{2p_x^2}}\, = \,{\pi _{2p_y^2}}\,\pi _{2p_x^2}^ * \, = \,\pi _{2p_y^2}^ * \sigma _{2{p_z^2}}^ *$$
$$ \begin{aligned} N_b=10, & N_a=10 \\ \text { Bond older } & =\frac{1}{2}\left(N_b-N_a\right) \\ & =\frac{1}{2}(10-10) \\ & =\frac{1}{2}(0) \\ & =0 \end{aligned} $$
Bond order of $\mathrm{Ne}_2$ is zero. It is correct.
B) $H O M O$ of $F_2$
The number of electrons in $F$ is 9 .
So, the total number of electrons in $F_2$ molecule is $18(9+9)$ The 18 electrons are filled in the molecular orbitals as,
$${\sigma _{1{s^2}}}\,\sigma _{1{s^2}}^ * \,{\sigma _{2{s^2}}}\,\sigma _{2{s^2}}^ * \,{\sigma _{2p_z^2}}\,{\pi _{2p_x^2}}\, = \,{\pi _{2p_y^2}}\,\pi _{2p_x^2}^ * \, = \,\pi _{2p_y^2}^ * $$

Homo is the highest occupied molecular orbital.
For $F_2$, the last electron is occupied in the $\pi^*$ orbital. So, the highest occupied molecular orbital of $F_2$ is $\pi$ - type, not $\sigma$-type.
It is not correct.
c) Bond energy is directly proportional to bond order. The strength of a chemical bond is directly proportional to the amount of energy required to break it.
To compare the bond energy of $\mathrm{O}_2^{+}$and $\mathrm{O}_2$, calculate the bond older. The molecule with higher bond order has higher bond energy
$\mathrm{O}_2$
Number of elections of oxygen $(0)=8$
number of elections of $\mathrm{O}_2$ molecule $=16$
The 16 elections are filled in the molecular orbitals as:
$${\sigma _{1{s^2}}}\,\sigma _{1{s^2}}^ * \,{\sigma _{2{s^2}}}\,\sigma _{2{s^2}}^ * \,{\sigma _{2p_z^2}}\,{\pi _{2p_x^2}}\, = \,{\pi _{2p_y^2}}\,\pi _{2p_x^2}^ * $$

$$ \begin{aligned} & N_b=10, N_a=6 \end{aligned} $$
$$ \begin{aligned} \text { Bond older } & =\frac{1}{2}\left(N_b-N_a\right) \\ & =\frac{1}{2}(10-6) \\ & =\frac{1}{2} \cdot(4) \\ & =2 \end{aligned} $$
$\mathrm{O}_2^{+}$
Total number of elections for $0_2$ molecule $=16$
For $\mathrm{O}_2^{+}$, one electron is removed from $\mathrm{O}_2$. So, the total number of electrons $=16-1=15$
The 15 electrons are filled in the molecular orbitals as,
$${\sigma _{1{s^2}}}\,\sigma _{1{s^2}}^ * \,{\sigma _{2{s^2}}}\,\sigma _{2{s^2}}^ * \,{\sigma _{2p_z^2}}\,{\pi _{2p_x^2}}\, = \,{\pi _{2p_y^2}}\,\pi _{2p_x^1}^ * $$
$$ \begin{aligned} N_b=10, & N_a=5 \\ \text { Bond order } & =\frac{1}{2}\left(N_b-N_a\right) \\ & =\frac{1}{2}(10-5) \\ & =\frac{1}{2}(5) \\ & =2.5 \end{aligned} $$
Bond order of $\mathrm{O}_2^{+}$is higher than the bond order of $\mathrm{O}_2$.
Bond order and bond energy are directly proportional so, the higher the bond oder, the higher the bond energy. Therefore, $\mathrm{O}_2{ }^{+}$molecule which has higher bond order and also has higher bond energy.
So, bond energy of $\mathrm{O}_2^{+}$is higher than the bond energy of $\mathrm{O}_2$.
$$ \text { It is not correct: } $$
D) Bond length of $Li_2$ and $B_2$
Bond length and bond older are inversely proportional to each other so; higher the bond order, shorter the bond length.
To get the bond length of $L_2$ and $B_2$, first calculate the bond order of $Li_2$ and $B_2$.
$\mathrm{Li}_2$
Number of electrons of $\mathrm{Li}=3$ :
total number of electrons in $\mathrm{Li}_2$ molecule $=6$
The total electrons are filled in the molecular orbitals as
Li2 = $${\sigma _{1{s^2}}}\,\,\sigma _{1{s^2}}^ * \,$$ $${\sigma _{2{s^2}}} \,$$
$$ N_b=4, N_a=2 $$
$$ \begin{aligned} \text { Bond loeder } & =\frac{1}{2}\left(N_b-N_a\right) \\ & =\frac{1}{2}(4-2) \\ & =\frac{1}{2}(2) \\ & =1 \end{aligned} $$
$B_2$
Number of electrons of Balon $(B)=5$
Total number of electrons of $B_2$ molecule $=10$
The total electrons are filled in the molecular orbitals as
$${\sigma _{1{s^2}}}\,\sigma _{1{s^2}}^ * \,\,{\sigma _{2{s^2}}}\,\,\sigma _{2{s^2}}^ * \,\,{\pi _{2p_x^1}} = {\pi _{2p_y^1}}$$
$$ N_b=6 \quad N_a=4 $$
$$ \begin{aligned} \text { Bond older } & =\frac{1}{2}\left(N_b+N_a\right) \\ & =\frac{1}{2}(6-4) \\ & =\frac{1}{2}(2) \\ & =1 \end{aligned} $$
Bond order is equal for $Li_{2}$ and $B_2$ so, atomic size is consider Comparing the size of lithium and boron, lithium is a larger atom than boron. Bond length generally increases with the size of atom so, bond length of $\mathrm{Li}_2$ is larger than the bond length of $\mathrm{B_2}$
Size of $Li>B$
So, Bond length of $Li_2>B_2$
This statement is correct: Answer:
The incorrect statements are (B) and (C).
(B) and (C).

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2025 Paper 2 Online
questions.question: The correct statement(s) about intermolecular forces is(are)
options: The potential energy between two point charges approaches zero more rapidly than the potential energy between a point dipole and a point charge as the distance between them approaches infinity.
The average potential energy of two rotating polar molecules that are separated by a distance r has $1/r^3$ dependence.
The dipole-induced dipole average interaction energy is independent of temperature.
Nonpolar molecules attract one another even though neither has a permanent dipole moment.
questions.solution:
Option A

A charge–charge interaction falls off as

$$U_{qq}\propto\frac1r$$

while a charge–dipole interaction goes as

$$U_{q\mu}\propto\frac1{r^2}\,. $$

So the charge–dipole potential actually decays faster.

⇒ A is false.

Option B

A fixed dipole–dipole energy is

$$U_{\mu\mu}\propto\frac1{r^3}\,, $$

but once both molecules tumble freely, the thermal (Keesom) average gives

$$\langle U\rangle_{\rm Keesom}\propto -\frac{\mu^4}{kT\,(4\pi\varepsilon_0)^2\,r^6}\,. $$

That falls off as $1/r^6$, not $1/r^3$.

⇒ B is false.

Option C

The permanent–induced dipole (Debye) interaction is

$$U_{\rm Debye}=-\tfrac12\,\alpha\,E^2\propto -\frac{\alpha\,\mu^2}{(4\pi\varepsilon_0)^2\,r^6}\,, $$

and when you average over random orientations (assuming the interaction is weak compared with $kT$), no $T$ appears in the result.

⇒ C is true.

Option D

Even totally nonpolar species experience instantaneous‐induced (London) dispersion forces that attract as

$$U_{\rm disp}\propto -\frac1{r^6}\,. $$

⇒ D is true.

Answer: C and D.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2025 Paper 2 Online
questions.question:
Consider a reaction $A+R \rightarrow$ Product. The rate of this reaction is measured to be $k[A][R]$. At the start of the reaction, the concentration of $R,[R]_0$, is 10-times the concentration of $A,[A]_0$. The reaction can be considered to be a pseudo first order reaction with assumption that $k[R]=k^{\prime}$ is constant. Due to this assumption, the relative error (in %) in the rate when this reaction is $40 \%$ complete, is ___________.

[ $k$ and $k^{\prime}$ represent corresponding rate constants]
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \text { Rate }=k[\mathrm{~A}][\mathrm{R}] \\\\ & \operatorname{Rate}_1=k\left(0.6 \mathrm{~A}_0\right) \times 9.6 \mathrm{~A}_0\end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & \text { Rate }=\mathrm{k}^{\prime}[\mathrm{A}], \mathrm{k}^{\prime}=\mathrm{k}[\mathrm{R}] \end{aligned}$

The relative error between the actual rate and the pseudo-first order rate is computed by:

$ \text{Relative Error (%)} = \frac{\text{Rate}_2 - \text{Rate}_1}{\text{Rate}_1} \times 100 $

Plugging the calculations:

$ \text{Relative Error (%)} = \frac{(0.6 \times 10 - 0.6 \times 9.6)}{0.6 \times 9.6} \times 100 = 4.1666\% $

questions.title: BITSAT 2025
questions.question:
For the reaction,

$$ 2 \mathrm{SO}_2+\mathrm{O}_2 \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{SO}_3 $$

if the rate of disappearance of $\mathrm{O}_2$ is $2 \times 10^{-4} \mathrm{~mol} \mathrm{~L}^{-1} \mathrm{~s}^{-1}$, determine the rate of appearance of $\mathrm{SO}_3$.
questions.solution:
From the balanced reaction,

$$ 2 \mathrm{SO}_2+\mathrm{O}_2 \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{SO}_3 $$

Using stoichiometry,

$$ \begin{aligned} & \text { Rate of disapperance of } \mathrm{O}_2=2 \times 10^{-1} \mathrm{molL}^{-1} \mathrm{~s}^{-1} \\ &\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\, \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\, \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\, \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\, \,\,\,\,\,\frac{-d\left[\mathrm{O}_2\right]}{d t}=\frac{1}{2} \frac{d\left[\mathrm{SO}_3\right]}{d t} \\ &\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\, \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\, \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\, \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\, \,\,\,\,\, \frac{d\left[\mathrm{SO}_3\right]}{d t}=2 \times\left[\frac{-d\left[\mathrm{O}_2\right]}{d t}\right] \end{aligned} $$

Rate of appearance of

$$ \mathrm{SO}_3=2 \times\left(2 \times 10^{-1}\right)=4 \times 10^{-1} \mathrm{molL}^{-1} \mathrm{~s}^{-1} $$

questions.title: BITSAT 2025
questions.question: If for a first-order reaction, the frequency factor $A=4 \times 10^{13} \mathrm{~s}^{-1}$ and activation energy $E_a=98.6 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$, at what temperature will the half-life be 10 minutes?
questions.solution:
Half-life for first-order reaction,

$$ t_{1 / 2}=0.693 / \mathrm{k} $$

Given, $t_{1 / 2}=600 \mathrm{~s}$

$$ k=\frac{0.693}{600} \approx 1.155 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~s}^{-1} $$

Using Arrhenius,

$$ k=A e^{\left(-E_0 / R T\right)} $$

Take $\log$ both sides and solve,

$$ \ln k=\ln (A)-\frac{E_a}{R T} $$

$$ \begin{aligned} &\text { Substituting the values, }\\ &\begin{aligned} \ln \left(1.155 \times 10^{-3}\right) & =\ln \left(4 \times 10^{13}\right)-\left(\frac{98600}{R T}\right) \\ & \approx 311.49 k \end{aligned} \end{aligned} $$

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: Which one of the following statements is NOT correct ?
options: The molecules of soap are sodium or potassium salts of long chain fatty acids
The molecules of soap contain both hydrophobic and hydrophilic ends
Detergents are more effective than soaps in hard water
In micelles the ionic-end of the molecules is towards oil droplet while the other end faces outside
questions.solution:
The statement that is NOT correct is Option 4.

Reason: In a micelle formed in water, the ionic (hydrophilic) ends face the surrounding aqueous medium, while the non-polar (hydrophobic) tails point inward toward the trapped oil or grease. Option 4 has this orientation reversed.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: Element X forms a chloride with the formula XCl₂, which is a solid with high melting point. X would most likely be in the same group of periodic table as
questions.solution:
XCl₂ being a high-melting solid tells us it’s an ionic compound with X in the +2 oxidation state. Metals that commonly form MCl₂ salts are the Group 2 (alkaline-earth) elements.

• Group 2 elements → Be, Mg, Ca, …

• From the options given, Mg is the only Group 2 metal.

Answer: Option 3 (Mg)

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: Which one of the following oxides is a neutral oxide?
questions.solution:
The neutral oxide among the given choices is carbon monoxide, CO.

• CO₂ is acidic (forms H₂CO₃ in water)

• Na₂O and MgO are basic (react with water to give OH⁻)

• CO does not react appreciably with acids or bases, so it’s considered neutral.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: Which one of the following is also known as Dry ice in its solid form?
questions.solution:
Dry ice is actually the solid form of a very common gas. The compound known as dry ice is carbon dioxide.

Its chemical formula is $$CO_2$$.

Looking at the options you gave:

Option 1: $$SiO_2$$ is Silicon dioxide, which is more commonly known as silica.

Option 2: $$CO_2$$ is Carbon dioxide.

Option 3: $$CaO$$ is Calcium oxide, or quicklime.

Option 4: $$MgO$$ is Magnesium oxide.

So, the one that is also known as dry ice in its solid form is $$CO_2$$, Carbon dioxide.

That means Option 2 is the correct one! Dry ice gets its name because it doesn't melt into a liquid; instead, it goes directly from a solid to a gas, a process called sublimation.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: Which one of the following statements is NOT correct?
options: A scale measuring hydrogen ion concentration in a solution is called pH scale
The higher the hydrogen ion concentration in a solution, higher is its pH
We can measure pH generally from 0 to 14 on a pH scale
The 'p' in pH stands for 'potenz' in German meaning power
questions.solution:
The statement that is NOT correct is Option 2.

Explanation:

By definition,

$$\mathrm{pH} = -\log_{10}[H^+].$$

If $[H^+]$ increases, $\log_{10}[H^+]$ goes up, so $\mathrm{pH} = -\log_{10}[H^+]$ goes down.

Hence, a higher hydrogen‐ion concentration actually means a lower pH, not a higher one.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: Which one of the following statements is NOT correct?
options: Half mole of nitrogen gas is measured 11·2 litre at STP
17 gram of ammonia gas contains 6·022 × 10²³ molecules at STP
22·4 litre of CO₂ gas at STP contains 44 gram of molecules
4 gram of hydrogen gas contains 6·022 × 10²³ molecules
questions.solution:
Let’s check each statement using the molar volume (1 mol gas at STP = 22.4 L) and molar masses:

½ mol N₂ → 0.5×22.4 L = 11.2 L ✅

17 g NH₃ (M = 17 g/mol) → 1 mol → 6.022×10²³ molecules ✅

22.4 L CO₂ → 1 mol → 44 g CO₂ ✅

4 g H₂ (M = 2 g/mol) →

$$n=\frac{4}{2}=2\;\text{mol}$$

$$N=2\times6.022\times10^{23}=1.2044\times10^{24}\;\text{molecules}$$

Statement says only 6.022×10²³ molecules, so it’s wrong. ❌

Answer: Option 4 is NOT correct.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: Which one of the following statements about a compound is NOT correct?
options: A compound is a substance composed of two or more elements, chemically combined in a fixed proportion
Properties of a compound are different from its constituent elements
A compound is an impure substance
The constituents of a compound can be separated only by chemical or electrochemical reactions
questions.solution:
The statement that is NOT correct is Option 3:

“A compound is an impure substance.”

• By definition, a compound is a pure substance made up of two or more elements chemically combined in fixed proportions.

• Its properties differ from those of the individual elements.

• You can only separate its constituents by chemical or electrochemical means.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question:
Match List I with List II and select the answer using the code given below the Lists:
List I (Chemical Name)  List II (Chemical Formula)
A. Washing soda  1. CaOCl₂
B. Baking soda 2. NaHCO₃
C. Bleaching powder  3. Na₂CO₃·10H₂O
D. Gypsum 4. CaSO₄·2H₂O

Code:
questions.solution:
Here’s how the chemicals match up:

Washing soda (A) is sodium carbonate decahydrate:

$$\text{Na}_2\text{CO}_3\cdot10\text{H}_2\text{O}$$ → 3

Baking soda (B) is sodium bicarbonate:

$$\text{NaHCO}_3$$ → 2

Bleaching powder (C) is calcium oxychloride:

$$\text{CaOCl}_2$$ → 1

Gypsum (D) is calcium sulfate dihydrate:

$$\text{CaSO}_4\cdot2\text{H}_2\text{O}$$ → 4

So the correct code is Option 4:

(a)–3, (b)–2, (c)–1, (d)–4

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: Which one of the following nitrogen oxides may dimerize?
questions.solution:
The oxide that dimerizes is NO₂. Here’s why:

NO₂ is a free radical (it has an unpaired electron).

Two NO₂ molecules can pair up that unpaired electron to form dinitrogen tetroxide, N₂O₄.

The equilibrium is:

$$2\,NO_2 \;\rightleftharpoons\; N_2O_4$$

At lower temperatures the equilibrium shifts toward N₂O₄ (the dimer), while at higher temperatures it shifts back to NO₂.

None of the other oxides listed (N₂O, N₂O₃, N₂O₅) form such dimers under normal conditions.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: Which one of the following statements regarding oxidation and reduction reactions is NOT correct?
options: If a substance loses hydrogen during reaction, it is reduced
If a substance loses oxygen during reaction, it is reduced
If a substance gains hydrogen during reaction, it is reduced
If a substance gains oxygen during reaction, it is oxidized
questions.solution:
The statement that’s NOT correct is Option 1.

Here's why:

Oxidation involves either gaining O₂ or losing H₂ (loss of electrons).

Reduction involves either losing O₂ or gaining H₂ (gain of electrons).

So losing hydrogen is an oxidation process, not a reduction.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: For acid rain, the pH of rain water should be less than
questions.solution:
Acid rain is defined as precipitation with a pH lower than the natural “background” value of about 5.6 (due to dissolved CO₂).

So the correct choice is:

Option 3: 5·6

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: What happens when the fat and oil containing food materials are left outside for a long time?
options: Fats and oils in the food get oxidized
Fats and oils in the food get reduced
Fats and oils in the food get ice-covered
questions.solution:
Option 1

When fats and oils in foods are exposed to air (especially light, heat or trace metals) over time, they undergo auto-oxidation, commonly called “rancidity.” Here’s what happens:

Initiation

• A hydrogen atom is abstracted from a fatty acid (usually at a double bond), forming a lipid radical (L•).

Propagation

• The lipid radical reacts with O₂ to give a peroxyl radical (LOO•).

• That peroxyl radical steals H from another fatty acid, creating a hydroperoxide (LOOH) and a new lipid radical—fueling a chain reaction.

Termination

• Two radicals combine to form non-radical products, but by then you’ve already formed volatile aldehydes, ketones and acids responsible for off-flavors and odors.

Why the other options don’t fit

• Reduction (Option 2) would require a hydrogen‐rich environment or specific enzymes—unlikely in food left out.

• Ice formation (Option 3) only occurs below 0 °C.

• “No reaction” (Option 4) isn’t true—oxidative rancidity happens at room temperature given time and oxygen.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: Which one among the following elements is known to be discovered the earliest?
questions.solution: Copper. Archaeological evidence shows native copper was in use by around 9000 BCE, well before gold (≈6000 BCE), oxygen (isolated in 1774), or uranium (identified in 1789).

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: The temperature at which “vapour pressure of the liquid in an open vessel becomes equal to the atmospheric pressure” is called
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

Option 2: Boiling point

Explanation:

According to NCERT, boiling point is the temperature at which the vapour pressure of a liquid becomes equal to the atmospheric pressure.

At this temperature, the liquid starts changing into vapour throughout the liquid.

So,

$$ \text{Boiling point} = \text{temperature where vapour pressure} = \text{atmospheric pressure} $$

Answer: Boiling point

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: The reaction that occurs on passing carbon dioxide gas through lime water is
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option 2.

The reaction is:

$$ \mathrm{Ca(OH)_2 + CO_2 \rightarrow CaCO_3 + H_2O} $$

Explanation:

  • Lime water is a solution of calcium hydroxide, that is, $\mathrm{Ca(OH)_2}$.

  • When carbon dioxide is passed through lime water, it reacts to form:

  • calcium carbonate $\mathrm{(CaCO_3)}$, which is a white precipitate

  • water $\mathrm{(H_2O)}$

So lime water turns milky due to the formation of $\mathrm{CaCO_3}$.

Therefore:

$$ \boxed{\mathrm{Ca(OH)_2 + CO_2 \rightarrow CaCO_3 + H_2O}} $$

So, Option 2 is correct.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: Which one of the following statements is NOT correct ?
options: Water of crystallization is the fixed number of water molecules present in one formula unit of a salt
The molecular formula of Plaster of Paris is CaSO<sub>4</sub>:2H<sub>2</sub>O
Washing soda is used for removing the permanent hardness of water
The atmosphere of Venus is made up of thick white and yellow clouds of sulphuric acid
questions.solution:
The incorrect statement is:

$$\text{Option 2}$$

Reason:

Plaster of Paris has the formula:

$$\text{CaSO}_4 \cdot \dfrac{1}{2}\text{H}_2\text{O}$$

It is also written as:

$$2\text{CaSO}_4 \cdot \text{H}_2\text{O}$$

But in Option 2, it is given as:

$$\text{CaSO}_4 : 2\text{H}_2\text{O}$$

This is wrong.

Checking the other options:

  1. Water of crystallization is the fixed number of water molecules present in one formula unit of a salt — correct.

  2. Plaster of Paris formula given is wrong — not correct.

  3. Washing soda is used for removing permanent hardness of water — correct.

  4. Venus has thick white and yellow clouds of sulphuric acid — correct.

So, the answer is:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 2}}$$

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: Which one of the following type of medicines is used for treating indigestion ?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

Option 4: Antacid

Explanation:

Indigestion is usually caused by excess acid in the stomach. Antacids are medicines that help to neutralise the extra acid and give relief from indigestion.

Other options:

  • Analgesic: used to relieve pain

  • Antiseptic: used to prevent infection in wounds

  • Antibiotic: used to kill or stop the growth of bacteria

So, the medicine used for treating indigestion is Antacid.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: Which one of the following is NOT present in cement ?
options: 3CaO.SiO2
4CaO.SiO2
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

$$\boxed{4CaO \cdot SiO_2}$$

So, Option 3 is NOT present in cement.

Explanation:

The main constituents of cement are:

  1. Tricalcium silicate

    $$ 3CaO \cdot SiO_2 $$

  2. Dicalcium silicate

    $$ 2CaO \cdot SiO_2 $$

  3. Tricalcium aluminate

    $$ 3CaO \cdot Al_2O_3 $$

  4. Tetracalcium aluminoferrite

    $$ 4CaO \cdot Al_2O_3 \cdot Fe_2O_3 $$

Among the given options,

$$ 4CaO \cdot SiO_2 $$

is not a normal constituent of cement.

Final Answer:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 3}}$$

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: Which one of the following is NOT a chemical change ?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option 3: Melting of ice.

Reason:

A chemical change is a change in which a new substance is formed.

Let us check each option:

  1. Burning of coal

    Coal burns and forms new substances like carbon dioxide, ash, etc.

    So, it is a chemical change.

  2. Rusting of metal

    Iron reacts with oxygen and moisture to form rust.

    Rust is a new substance.

    So, it is a chemical change.

  3. Melting of ice

    Ice changes into water only. No new substance is formed.

    It is only a physical change.

    So, this is not a chemical change.

  4. Reaction of acid with base

    Acid and base react to form salt and water.

    New substances are formed.

    So, it is a chemical change.

Final Answer:

Option 3: Melting of ice

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: Which one of the following statements is NOT correct ?
options: The interconversion of states of matter is a physical change
During burning of a candle, both chemical and physical changes take place
During a physical change, the chemical composition of miolecules changes
Rusting of iron is a chemical change
questions.solution:
The incorrect statement is:

Option 3: During a physical change, the chemical composition of molecules changes.

Explanation:

In a physical change, only the shape, size, or state of a substance may change. The chemical composition remains the same.

Let us check the other options:

  1. The interconversion of states of matter is a physical change

    This is correct.

    Example: ice changing into water, water changing into steam.

  2. During burning of a candle, both chemical and physical changes take place

    This is correct.

  • Melting of wax is a physical change

  • Burning of wax is a chemical change

  1. Rusting of iron is a chemical change

    This is correct because a new substance, iron oxide, is formed.

Final Answer:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 3}}$$

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: Which one of the following is NOT a heterogeneous mixture ?
options: Mixture of sugar 8 and salt crystals
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 1: Sulphur in carbon disulphide}}$$

Step-by-step:

A heterogeneous mixture has non-uniform composition, and its different parts can be seen separately.

Now check each option:

  1. Sulphur in carbon disulphide

    Sulphur dissolves completely in carbon disulphide, so it forms a homogeneous mixture, not a heterogeneous mixture.

  2. Mixture of sugar and salt crystals

    Sugar crystals and salt crystals remain separate, so it is a heterogeneous mixture.

  3. Sandy water

    Sand does not dissolve in water, so it is a heterogeneous mixture.

  4. Sulphur in water

    Sulphur does not dissolve in water, so it is a heterogeneous mixture.

Therefore, the one which is not a heterogeneous mixture is:

$$\boxed{\text{Sulphur in carbon disulphide}}$$

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question:
Match List I with List II and select the answer using the code given below the Lists 

List I

(Element)

List II

(Atomicity) 

A.

 Phosphorus 

1.

 2

B.

 Nitrogen

2.

 1

C.

Neon

3.

8

D.

Sulphur

4.

4
questions.solution:
We match each element with its atomicity in its elemental form:

  • Phosphorus exists as $P_4$

    So, atomicity $= 4$

  • Nitrogen exists as $N_2$

    So, atomicity $= 2$

  • Neon is a noble gas and exists as single atoms

    So, atomicity $= 1$

  • Sulphur exists as $S_8$

    So, atomicity $= 8$

Now match with List II:

  • A. Phosphorus $\rightarrow 4$ $\rightarrow$ 4

  • B. Nitrogen $\rightarrow 2$ $\rightarrow$ 1

  • C. Neon $\rightarrow 1$ $\rightarrow$ 2

  • D. Sulphur $\rightarrow 8$ $\rightarrow$ 3

So the correct matching is:

$$ A - 4,\quad B - 1,\quad C - 2,\quad D - 3 $$

Hence, the correct answer is Option 1.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question:
In the following redox reaction, which compound is acting as a reducing agent ?

N2H4 + 2H2O2 → N2 + 4H2O
questions.solution:
To find the reducing agent, we check which substance gets oxidised.

Given reaction:

$$ N_2H_4 + 2H_2O_2 \rightarrow N_2 + 4H_2O $$

Step 1: Find oxidation state of nitrogen in $N_2H_4$

Let oxidation state of nitrogen be $x$.

In $N_2H_4$:

  • Hydrogen has oxidation state $+1$

So,

$$ 2x + 4(+1) = 0 $$

$$ 2x + 4 = 0 $$

$$ 2x = -4 $$

$$ x = -2 $$

So, oxidation state of nitrogen in $N_2H_4$ is $-2$.

Step 2: Find oxidation state of nitrogen in $N_2$

In elemental form,

$$ \text{Oxidation state of } N \text{ in } N_2 = 0 $$

So nitrogen changes from:

$$ -2 \rightarrow 0 $$

This is oxidation.

Step 3: Identify reducing agent

The substance which gets oxidised acts as the reducing agent.

Here, $N_2H_4$ is oxidised to $N_2$.

Therefore, the reducing agent is:

$$ \boxed{N_2H_4} $$

Correct option: 2. $N_2H_4$

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: Which one: of the following is considered to be the purest source of water ?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

$$\text{Option 4: Rain water}$$

Reason:

Rain water is considered the purest natural source of water because it is formed by the evaporation of water from rivers, lakes, and seas. During evaporation, most impurities and salts are left behind.

So, among the given options, the purest source of water is:

$$\text{Rain water}$$

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: Which one of the following salts is responsible for temporary hardness of water ?
questions.solution:
Temporary hardness of water is caused by the bicarbonates of calcium and magnesium.

So, the correct answer is:

Option 3: Bicarbonates of magnesium

Reason:

  • Temporary hardness is due to soluble bicarbonates.

  • Permanent hardness is due to chlorides and sulphates of calcium and magnesium.

Therefore, magnesium bicarbonate is responsible for temporary hardness of water.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question:
Match List I with List II and select the answer using the code given below the Lists : 

List I

(Reaction)

List II

(Type)

A.

 CaO(s) + H2O (I) → Ca(OH)2 (aq)

1.

 Single Displacement

B.

 CaCO3 (s) → CaO(s) + CO2(g)

2.

 Double displacement

C.

Pb(s) + CuCl2(aq) → PbCl2(aq) + Cu(s)

3.

Combination

D.

Na2SO4(aq) + BaCl2 (aq) → BaSO4(s) + 2NaCl(aq)

4.

Decomposition
questions.solution:
Let us identify the type of each reaction one by one.

A. $CaO(s) + H_2O(l) \to Ca(OH)_2(aq)$

Here, two substances combine to form a single product.

So, this is a combination reaction.

$$A \to 3$$

B. $CaCO_3(s) \to CaO(s) + CO_2(g)$

Here, one compound breaks down into simpler substances.

So, this is a decomposition reaction.

$$B \to 4$$

C. $Pb(s) + CuCl_2(aq) \to PbCl_2(aq) + Cu(s)$

Here, lead displaces copper from copper chloride.

So, this is a single displacement reaction.

$$C \to 1$$

D. $Na_2SO_4(aq) + BaCl_2(aq) \to BaSO_4(s) + 2NaCl(aq)$

Here, the ions of two compounds exchange places.

So, this is a double displacement reaction.

$$D \to 2$$

Thus, the correct matching is:

$$A - 3,\; B - 4,\; C - 1,\; D - 2$$

Correct option: Option 3

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: Which one of the following is an endothermic reaction ?
questions.solution:
The endothermic reaction is:

Option 4

$$ 2Pb(NO_3)_2(s) \rightarrow 2PbO(s) + 4NO_2(g) + O_2(g) $$

Reason:

  • Endothermic reaction is a reaction in which heat is absorbed.

  • Thermal decomposition reactions usually need heat to break down a compound.

  • Lead nitrate, $Pb(NO_3)_2$, decomposes on heating, so this is an endothermic reaction.

Why others are not endothermic:

  • Option 1: Combustion of methane is exothermic.

  • Option 2: Respiration/glucose oxidation is exothermic.

  • Option 3: Formation of ammonia is also exothermic.

Correct answer:

Option 4

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: Which one of the following is known as ‘water gas’ ?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option 4: $CO + H_2$

Explanation:

Water gas is a mixture of:

$$ CO + H_2 $$

It is formed when steam passes over red hot carbon.

The reaction is:

$$ C + H_2O \rightarrow CO + H_2 $$

So, water gas contains carbon monoxide and hydrogen.

Correct option: Option 4

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: Which one of the following statements is NOT correct ?
options: Diamond is hard due to extended covalent bonding in it
Fullerene is composed of five and six membered rings
Diamond is a good conductor of electricity
Graphite is used as a lubricant
questions.solution:
The incorrect statement is:

Option 3: Diamond is a good conductor of electricity

Step-by-step explanation:

1. Diamond

In diamond, each carbon atom is bonded to four other carbon atoms by strong covalent bonds, forming a rigid three-dimensional network.

So:

  • Diamond is very hard

  • This is due to extended covalent bonding

  • But diamond does not conduct electricity, because it has no free electrons

So Option 1 is correct.

2. Fullerene

Fullerene is a form of carbon made up of five-membered and six-membered rings.

So Option 2 is correct.

3. Graphite

Graphite has layers of carbon atoms. These layers can slide over one another easily.

So:

  • Graphite is used as a lubricant

  • It also conducts electricity due to the presence of free electrons

So Option 4 is correct.

Final Answer:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 3}}$$

questions.title: BITSAT 2025
questions.question: The locus of the middle points of chords of the circle $x^2+y^2=25$ which are parallel to the line $x-2 y+3=0$ is
questions.solution:
Let $(h, k)$ be the mid-point of a chord of the circle $x^2+y^2=25$.

Then, equation of the chord is $h x+k y-25$

$$ =h^2+k^2-25 \Rightarrow h x+k y=h^2+k^2 $$

If this is parallel to $x-2 y+3=0$, then $-\frac{h}{k}=\frac{1}{2} \Rightarrow 2 h+k=0$

So, the locus of $(h, k)$ is $2 x+y=0$.

questions.title: BITSAT 2025
questions.question: What is the set of values of a for which the point ( $2 a, a+1$ ) is an interior point of the larger segment of the circle $x^2+y^2-2 x-2 y-8=0$ made by the chord $x-y+1=0$.
questions.solution:
The point $(2 a, a+1)$ will be an interior point of the larger segment of the circle.

$$ x^2+y^2-2 x-2 y-8=0 $$

if the point $(2 a, a+1)$ is an interior point and the point $(2 a, a+1)$ and the centre $(1,1)$ are on the same side of the chord $x-y+1=0$.

$$ \begin{aligned} & \therefore(2 a)^2+(a+1)^2-2(2 a)-2(a+1)-8 < 0 \\ & \text { and }(2 a-a-1+1)(1-1+1) > 0 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad 5 a^2-4 a-9 < 0 \text { and } a > 0 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad(5 a-9)(a+1)<0 \text { and } a > 0 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad-1 < a <\frac{9}{5} \text { and } a > 0 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad a \in\left(0, \frac{9}{5}\right) \end{aligned} $$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question: What is the equation of the circle whose diameter is 10 cm and the equations of two of its diameters are x+y=0 and x−y=0?
options: x2+y2=1
x2+y2=25
x2+y2=100
x2+y2−2x−2y−23=0
questions.solution:
1. Centre of the circle lies at the intersection of its diameters.

    Solve

    $$\begin{cases}x+y=0\\x-y=0\end{cases}\;\implies\;x=0,\;y=0.$$

    So centre $(h,k)=(0,0)$.

  2. Given diameter $=10$ cm, so radius $r=\tfrac{10}{2}=5$ cm.

  3. Standard equation of a circle with centre $(0,0)$ and radius $5$ is

    $$x^2+y^2=r^2\;=\;5^2\;=\;25.$$

Answer: Option B, $$x^2+y^2=25\,.$$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question: A square is inscribed in a circle x <sup>2</sup> + y<sup> 2 </sup>+ 2x + 2y + 1 = 0 and its sides are parallel to coordinate axes. Which one of the following is a vertex of the square?
questions.solution:
1. Equation of the circle

    Given

    $$x^2 + y^2 + 2x + 2y + 1 = 0$$

    Complete the squares in $x$ and $y$:

    $ (x^2 + 2x + 1) + (y^2 + 2y + 1) + 1 - 1 - 1 = 0 $

    $ (x + 1)^2 + (y + 1)^2 - 1 = 0 $

    Therefore the circle has center $C(-1,-1)$ and radius $r = 1$.

  2. Square inscribed with sides parallel to axes

    Let the side of the square be $s$.

    Its centre must coincide with the circle’s centre $C(-1,-1)$.

    Vertices of the square are then

    $ \bigl(-1\pm \tfrac{s}{2},\,-1\pm \tfrac{s}{2}\bigr). $

  3. Distance from centre to a vertex

    For any vertex $\bigl(-1 + \tfrac{s}{2}, -1 + \tfrac{s}{2}\bigr)$,

    distance$^2$ from $C(-1,-1)$ is

    $ \Bigl(\tfrac{s}{2}\Bigr)^2 + \Bigl(\tfrac{s}{2}\Bigr)^2 = 2\cdot \Bigl(\tfrac{s}{2}\Bigr)^2 = \frac{s^2}{2}. $

    This must equal the square of the radius, $1^2 = 1$.

    So

    $ \frac{s^2}{2} = 1 \quad\Longrightarrow\quad s^2 = 2 \quad\Longrightarrow\quad s = \sqrt{2}. $

  4. Coordinates of vertices

    Since $s/2 = \tfrac{\sqrt2}{2} = \tfrac{1}{\sqrt2}$, the four vertices are

    $ \bigl(-1 \pm \tfrac{1}{\sqrt2},\, -1 \pm \tfrac{1}{\sqrt2}\bigr). $

    One such vertex is

    $ \bigl(-1 + \tfrac{1}{\sqrt2},\, -1 - \tfrac{1}{\sqrt2}\bigr). $

  5. Match with options

    Option C is $\bigl(-1+\tfrac{1}{\sqrt{2}},\,-1-\tfrac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\bigr)$, which indeed is a vertex.

Answer: Option C.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14th September 2025
questions.question: The circle $ x ^ 2 + y ^ 2 - 2kx - 2ky + k ^ 2 = 0$ touches the x-axis at P and y-axis at Q. What is PQ equal to?
questions.solution:
Given circle:

$$ x^2 + y^2 - 2kx - 2ky + k^2 = 0 $$

We compare it with the standard form:

$$ x^2 + y^2 - 2gx - 2fy + c = 0 $$

So,

$$ g = k, \quad f = k, \quad c = k^2 $$

Hence, the centre of the circle is

$$ (k, k) $$

and the radius is

$$ r = \sqrt{g^2 + f^2 - c} = \sqrt{k^2 + k^2 - k^2} = \sqrt{k^2} = k $$

Now, the circle touches the $x$-axis and $y$-axis.

Since it touches the $x$-axis, the point of contact $P$ lies vertically below the centre.

So,

$$ P = (k, 0) $$

Since it touches the $y$-axis, the point of contact $Q$ lies horizontally left of the centre.

So,

$$ Q = (0, k) $$

Now find $PQ$ using distance formula:

$$ PQ = \sqrt{(k-0)^2 + (0-k)^2} $$

$$ PQ = \sqrt{k^2 + k^2} = \sqrt{2k^2} = k\sqrt{2} $$

Therefore,

$$ \boxed{PQ = \sqrt{2}k} $$

So, the correct option is:

$$ \boxed{\text{Option 1}} $$

questions.title: BITSAT 2025
questions.question: Four letter-clusters have been given, out of which three are alike in some manner and one is different. Select the odd letter-cluster.
questions.solution:
As per the options,

(a) $S \xrightarrow{-1} R \xrightarrow{+5} W \xrightarrow{+1} X$

(b) $\mathrm{H}^{-1} \mathrm{G} \xrightarrow{+5} \mathrm{~L} \xrightarrow{+1} \mathrm{M}$

(c) $\mathrm{P} \xrightarrow{-1} \mathrm{O} \xrightarrow{+5} \mathrm{~T} \xrightarrow{+1} \mathrm{U}$

questions.title: BITSAT 2025
questions.question: If ' $a$ ' is a complex number such that $|a|=1$. Find the value of $a$, so that the equation $a z^2+z+1=0$ has one purely imaginary root.
questions.solution:
Let $\alpha$ be a purely imaginary root of $a z^2+z+1=0$

Then,   $$ a \alpha^2+\alpha+1=0 \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,...(i)$$

Taking conjugate on both sides, we get

$$ \begin{array}{ll} & \bar{a}(\bar{\alpha})^2+\bar{\alpha}+1=0 \\ \Rightarrow & \bar{a} \alpha^2-\alpha+1=0\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,...(ii) \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,[\because \bar{\alpha}=-\alpha]\end{array} $$

Solving, Eqs. (i) and (ii) by cross-multiplication, we get

$$ \begin{array}{rlrl} \frac{\alpha^2}{1+1} & =\frac{\alpha}{\bar{a}-a}=\frac{1}{-a-\bar{a}} \\ \Rightarrow & \alpha^2 =\frac{-2}{a+\bar{a}} \text { and } \alpha=\frac{\bar{a}-a}{-a-\bar{a}} \end{array} $$

Now, $\alpha^2=(\alpha)^2$

$$ \begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \frac{-2}{a+\bar{a}}=\left(\frac{\bar{a}-a}{-a-\bar{a}}\right)^2=\frac{(\bar{a}-a)^2}{(a+\bar{a})^2} \\ & \Rightarrow-2(a+\bar{a})=(\bar{a}-a)^2 \\ & \Rightarrow(\bar{a}-a)^2+2(\bar{a}+a)=0 \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,...(iii)\end{aligned} $$

Now, $|a|=1 \Rightarrow a=\cos \theta+i \sin \theta$

Put the value of $a$ in Eq. (iii), we get

$$ \begin{aligned} & (-2 i \sin \theta)^2+4 \cos \theta=0 \\ \Rightarrow & -4 \sin ^2 \theta+4 \cos \theta=0 \\ \Rightarrow & \cos ^2 \theta+\cos \theta-1=0 \\ \Rightarrow & \cos \theta=\frac{\sqrt{5}-1}{2}, \frac{-\sqrt{5}-1}{2} \quad[\because \cos \theta \in[-1,1]] \\ \Rightarrow & \quad \theta=\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{5}-1}{2}\right) \end{aligned} $$

Hence, $a=\cos \left\{\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{5}-1}{2}\right)\right\}$$$ +i \sin \left\{\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{5}-1}{2}\right)\right\} $$

questions.title: BITSAT 2025
questions.question: Let $z$ be a complex number for which $\left|2 z \cos \theta+z^2\right|>1$, if $|z|
questions.solution:
$$ \begin{aligned} &\text { }\\ &\begin{aligned} We\,\,\, have, & \left|2 z \cos \theta+z^2\right| < |2 z \cos \theta|+\left|z^2\right| \\ & \quad\left(\because\left|z_1+z_2\right| \leq\left|z_1\right|+\left|z_2\right|\right) \\ & < 2|z||\cos \theta|+|z|^2 \\ & < 2|z|+|z|^2 \\ & < 2 k+k^2 \quad \quad[\because|z| < k] \end{aligned} \end{aligned} $$

$$ \begin{aligned} \text { But } & 2 z \cos \theta+z^2 \mid >1 \\ & \therefore \,\,2 k+k^2 >1 \\ \Rightarrow &\,\, k^2+2 k-1 >0 \\ \Rightarrow \,\,& k >\sqrt{2}-1 \end{aligned} $$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2025 Paper 2 Online
questions.question:
For a non-zero complex number $z$, let $\arg (z)$ denote the principal argument of $z$, with $-\pi<\arg (z) \leq \pi$. Let $\omega$ be the cube root of unity for which $0<\arg (\omega)<\pi$. Let

$$ \alpha=\arg \left(\sum\limits_{n=1}^{2025}(-\omega)^n\right) $$

Then the value of $\frac{3 \alpha}{\pi}$ is ________________.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \alpha=\arg \left(-\omega+\omega^2-\omega^3+\ldots \ldots \ldots+(-\omega)^{2025}\right) \\ & \alpha=\arg \left(\frac{-\omega\left((-\omega)^{2025}-1\right)}{-\omega-1}\right) \\ & \alpha=\arg \left(\frac{-\omega}{-\omega-1}(-2)\right) \\ & \alpha=\arg \left(\frac{-2 \omega}{\omega+1}\right) \\ & \alpha=\arg \left(\frac{-2 \omega}{-\omega^2}\right) \\ & \alpha=\arg \left(\frac{2}{\omega}\right) \\ & \alpha=\arg \left(2 \omega^2\right) \\ & \alpha=\frac{-2 \pi}{3} \\ & \frac{3 \alpha}{\pi}=-2\end{aligned}$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question: What is $ \left( \frac{\sqrt{3}+i}{\sqrt{3}-i} \right)^3 $ equal to?
questions.solution:
We need to simplify

$ \left( \frac{\sqrt{3}+i}{\sqrt{3}-i} \right)^3 $

Step 1: Simplify the fraction inside.

$ \frac{\sqrt{3}+i}{\sqrt{3}-i} \times \frac{\sqrt{3}+i}{\sqrt{3}+i} = \frac{(\sqrt{3}+i)^2}{(\sqrt{3})^2 - (i)^2} $

Step 2: Simplify the denominator.

$ (\sqrt{3})^2 - (i)^2 = 3 - (-1) = 4 $

Step 3: Expand the numerator.

$ (\sqrt{3}+i)^2 = (\sqrt{3})^2 + 2\sqrt{3}i + i^2 = 3 + 2\sqrt{3}i - 1 = 2 + 2\sqrt{3}i $

So,

$ \frac{(\sqrt{3}+i)^2}{4} = \frac{2 + 2\sqrt{3}i}{4} = \frac{1}{2} + \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}i $

Step 4: Recognize the form.

$ \frac{1}{2} + \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} i = \cos 60^\circ + i \sin 60^\circ $

So the fraction equals $e^{i\pi/3}$.

Step 5: Raise to the power 3.

$ \left(e^{i\pi/3}\right)^3 = e^{i\pi} = \cos \pi + i\sin \pi = -1 $

Final Answer:

Option A: -1

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question: If $z\ne0$ is a complex number, then what is amp(z)+amp(zˉ) equal to?
questions.solution:
1. Let $z\neq0$ and write in polar form

    $ z = r\,e^{i\theta},\quad r>0,\ \theta=\arg(z). $

  2. Then its conjugate is

    $ \bar z = r\,e^{-i\theta},\quad \arg(\bar z) = -\theta. $

  3. Hence

    $ \arg(z) + \arg(\bar z) = \theta + (-\theta) = 0 \quad(\text{mod }2\pi), $

    and on the principal branch $\arg\in(-\pi,\pi]$ it equals $0$.

Answer: 0.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14th September 2025
questions.question: If  $(\frac{1-i}{1+i})^{2m}(\frac{1+i}{1-i})^{2n}=1$where $i=\sqrt{-1}$ then what is the smallest positive value of (m - n)?
questions.solution:
$ \left(\frac{1-i}{1+i}\right)^{2m}\left(\frac{1+i}{1-i}\right)^{2n}=1 $

We will simplify each fraction first.

Step 1: Simplify $\dfrac{1-i}{1+i}$

Multiply numerator and denominator by the conjugate of the denominator, that is, $(1-i)$:

$ \frac{1-i}{1+i}=\frac{(1-i)^2}{(1+i)(1-i)} $

Now,

$ (1-i)^2=1-2i+i^2=1-2i-1=-2i $

and

$ (1+i)(1-i)=1-i^2=1-(-1)=2 $

So,

$ \frac{1-i}{1+i}=\frac{-2i}{2}=-i $

Similarly,

$ \frac{1+i}{1-i}=i $

Step 2: Substitute in the given expression

$ (-i)^{2m}(i)^{2n}=1 $

Now,

$ (-i)^2=-1 \quad \text{and} \quad i^2=-1 $

Therefore,

$ (-i)^{2m}=\left((-i)^2\right)^m=(-1)^m $

and

$ i^{2n}=(i^2)^n=(-1)^n $

So the equation becomes

$ (-1)^m(-1)^n=1 $

$ (-1)^{m+n}=1 $

This is possible when $m+n$ is even.

Step 3: Rewrite in a better way

Notice that

$ \left(\frac{1+i}{1-i}\right)=\left(\frac{1-i}{1+i}\right)^{-1} $

So the given expression can also be written as

$ \left(\frac{1-i}{1+i}\right)^{2m-2n}=1 $

$ \left(\frac{1-i}{1+i}\right)^{2(m-n)}=1 $

Since

$ \frac{1-i}{1+i}=-i $

we get

$ (-i)^{2(m-n)}=1 $

But

$ (-i)^2=-1 $

So,

$ (-1)^{m-n}=1 $

This means $m-n$ must be even.

Hence the smallest positive value of $m-n$ is

$ \boxed{2} $

So, the correct option is:

$ \boxed{\text{Option 2}} $

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14th September 2025
questions.question: Let 1, ω, $\omega ^ 2$ be three cube roots of unity. If x = a + b $y=a\omega +b\omega ^{2}, z=a\omega ^{2}+b\omega$ then what is $ x ^ 2 + y ^ 2 + z ^ 2$ equal to?
questions.solution:
Given

$$ x=a+b,\qquad y=a\omega+b\omega^2,\qquad z=a\omega^2+b\omega $$

We have to find

$$ x^2+y^2+z^2 $$

Now,

$$ x^2=(a+b)^2=a^2+b^2+2ab $$

Also,

$$ y^2=(a\omega+b\omega^2)^2 $$

Using $(p+q)^2=p^2+q^2+2pq$,

$$ y^2=a^2\omega^2+b^2\omega^4+2ab\omega^3 $$

Since for cube roots of unity,

$$ \omega^3=1,\qquad \omega^4=\omega $$

So,

$$ y^2=a^2\omega^2+b^2\omega+2ab $$

Similarly,

$$ z^2=(a\omega^2+b\omega)^2 $$

$$ z^2=a^2\omega^4+b^2\omega^2+2ab\omega^3 $$

Again using $\omega^4=\omega$ and $\omega^3=1$,

$$ z^2=a^2\omega+b^2\omega^2+2ab $$

Now add all three:

$$ x^2+y^2+z^2 $$

$$ =(a^2+b^2+2ab)+(a^2\omega^2+b^2\omega+2ab)+(a^2\omega+b^2\omega^2+2ab) $$

Group like terms:

$$ = a^2(1+\omega+\omega^2)+b^2(1+\omega+\omega^2)+6ab $$

For cube roots of unity,

$$ 1+\omega+\omega^2=0 $$

Therefore,

$$ x^2+y^2+z^2=6ab $$

So the correct answer is:

$$ \boxed{6ab} $$

Hence, Option 1.

questions.title: BITSAT 2025
questions.question: $$ \text { The product } Z \text { formed in the given reaction is } $$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2025 Paper 1 Online
questions.question:
The major products obtained from the reactions in List-II are the reactants for the named reactions mentioned in List-I. Match each entry in List-I with the appropriate entry in List-II and choose the correct option.

List–I List–II
(P) Stephen reaction (1) $$ \text { Toluene } \xrightarrow{\begin{array}{l} \text { (i) } \mathrm{CrO}_2 \mathrm{Cl}_2 / \mathrm{CS}_2 \\ \text { (ii) } \mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{O}^{+} \end{array}} $$
(Q) Sandmeyer reaction (2) $$ \text { Benzoic acid } \xrightarrow{\substack{\text { (i) } \mathrm{PCl}_5 \\ \text { (ii) } \mathrm{NH}_3 \\ \text { (iii) } \mathrm{P}_4 \mathrm{O}_{10}, \Delta}} $$
(R) Hoffmann bromamide degradation reaction (3) $$ \text { Nitrobenzene } \xrightarrow{\begin{array}{l} \text { (i) } \mathrm{Fe}, \mathrm{HCl} \\ \text { (ii) } \mathrm{HCl}, \mathrm{NaNO}_2 \\ (273-278 \mathrm{~K}), \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \end{array}} $$
(S) Cannizzaro reaction (4) $$ \text { Toluene } \xrightarrow{\begin{array}{ll} \text { (i) } \mathrm{Cl}_2 / \mathrm{h\nu}, \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \\ \text { (ii) Tollen's reagent } \\ \text { (iii) } \mathrm{SO}_2 \mathrm{Cl}_2 \\ \text { (iv) } \mathrm{NH}_3 \end{array}} $$
(5) $$ \text { Aniline } \xrightarrow{\begin{array}{l} \text { (i) }\left(\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CO}\right)_2 \mathrm{O}, \text { Pyridine } \\ \text { (ii) } \mathrm{HNO}_3, \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4, 288 \mathrm{~K} \\ \text { (iii) aq. } \mathrm{NaOH} \end{array}} $$
questions.solution:
First, determine the major products of the reactions given in list - II
(1)
\text { Toluene } \frac{1) \mathrm{CrO}_2 \mathrm{Cl}_2 / \mathrm{Cs}_2}{2)_{\mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{O}^{+}}} \text {Benzaldehyde } $$
This reaction is known as Etard reaction $-\mathrm{CH}_3$ group in toluene is oxidized by oxidizing agent chromylchloside $\left(\mathrm{CrO}_2 \mathrm{Cl}_2\right)$ in carbon disulfide $\left(C S_2\right)$ as solvent, to form a chromium
complex. This complex on arid hydrolysis gives the major product benzaldehyde.
$$ \text { (2) } $$
$$ \text { Benzoic acid } \xrightarrow[\substack{\text { 2) } \mathrm{NH}_3 \\ \text { 3) } \mathrm{P}_4 \mathrm{O}_{10}, \Delta}]{\text { 1) } \mathrm{Pcl}_5} \text { Benzaldehyde } $$
Benzoic acid reacts with $\mathrm{PCl}_5$ to form benzoyl chloride. This step converts the - COOH group to. -COCl gorup.
Then, benzoyl chloride reacts with ammonia. This reaction converts the avid chloride to an amide, -COCl to $-\mathrm{CONH}_2$. It is the nucleophilic acyl substitution reaction where amonia acts as the nucleophile. So, this step gives benzamide.
In the next step, the amide is treated with $P_4 O_{10}$ and heat $P_4 O_{10}$ is a dehydrating agent, which removes water from the amide molecule, resulting in the formation of a nitrile. So, $-\mathrm{CONH}_2$ convert to -CN group.
The overall reaction converts benzoic acid to benzonitrile
$$ \text { (3) } $$
$$ \text { Nitrobenzene } \xrightarrow[\substack{2) \mathrm{Hcl}, \mathrm{NaNO}_2 \\(273-278 \mathrm{~K}), \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}}]{\text { 1) } \mathrm{Fe}, \mathrm{Hcl}} \text { Phenol } $$
$$ \text { In the first step, nitrobenzene convert to aniline } $$
In the next step, aniline reacts with HCl and $\mathrm{NaNO}_2$ gives benzenediazonium chloride. The reaction condition is low temperature $273-278 \mathrm{~K}$ $\left(\mathrm{O}^{\circ} \mathrm{C}-5^{\circ} \mathrm{C}\right)$. It is called diazotization. $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ works as proton donoe
Diazonium salt formation occurs at low temperature only.
So, the temperature condition $273-278 k$ is the low temperature and it is the required temperature for the, diazotization reaction.
For diazotization reaction, aniline (aromatic amine) is the reactant. $\mathrm{HCl}+\mathrm{NaNO}_2$ is the reagent. Or $\mathrm{HNO}_2$ can also be used for the diazotization reaction
The reaction involves the formation of nitrosonium ion ( $\mathrm{NO}^{+}$) which attacks the aromatic ring, leading to the formation of diazonium salt. The final product is diazonium salt
$$ \text { (4) } $$
First, toluene reacts with $\mathrm{Cl}_2$ in the presence of light. Side chain chlorination occurs in toluene, results in benzol chloride. It is then hydrolysis
$$ \text { (reaction with water) gives benzaldehyde. } $$
In the next step benzaldehyde reacts with Tollen's reagent and forms benzoic acid - CHO convert to -COOH
In the third step, benzoic acid reacts with $\mathrm{SO}_2 \mathrm{Cl}_2$ and gives benzoyl chloride.
In the next step, benzoyl chloride reaction with ammonia gives benzamide.
The fined major product of the overall reaction is benzamide.
$$ \text { (5) } $$
$$ \text { Aniline } \xrightarrow[\text { 3)aq, } \mathrm{NaOH}]{\substack{\text { 1) }\left(\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CO}\right)_2 \mathrm{O}, \text { Pyridine } \\ \text { 2) } \mathrm{HNO}_3, \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4, 288 \mathrm{~K}}} $$
Aniline reacts with acetic anhydride $\left(\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CO}\right)_2 \mathrm{O}$ in the presence of pyridine gives actylation of amingroup of aniline, forming a cetanilide.
Pyridine acts as a base to neutralize the acid produced during the reaction
Then, acetanilide undergoes nitration by reacting with a mixture of $\mathrm{HNO}_3$ and $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ at 288 K gives p-nitroacetanilide. $-\mathrm{NO}_2$ group is substituted at the para position. $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ acts as catalyst.
In the third step, para-nitroacetanilide reaction with aqueous NaOH give para-nitroaniline. The amide bond is hydrolyzed, removing the acetyl group and regenerating the amino group
$$ \text { Final product is p-nitroaniline } $$
$$ \text { Analyzing the list-I reactions, } $$
$$ \text { (P) Stephen, reaction } $$
In this reaction nitriles are converted into aldehydes.
The reactant of stephen reaction is nitriles. In List - II, reaction (2) product is benzonitrile So, P matches with reaction (2)
$$ P-2 $$
(Q) Standmeyer reaction
In this reaction an aromatic amine converts to an aryl halide, cyanide, or hydroxilated compound by using diazonium salts and copper (1) salts as catalysts.
Aromatic amine to diazonium salt reaction is known as Dicizotization.
Diazonium salt reaction with Cu(1) salt is the Sandmeyer reaction.
The reactant of Sandmeyer reaction is diazonium salt.
In List- II, reaction (3) gives diazonium salt product.
$$ \text { So, Q matches with reaction (3) } $$
$$ Q-3 $$
(R) Hoffman Bromamide degradation reaction. This reaction converts amides to primary amines
$$ \mathrm{R}-\mathrm{CONH} \xrightarrow{\mathrm{Br}_2, \mathrm{NaOH}} \mathrm{R}-\mathrm{NH}_2 $$
The reactant fol this reaction is an amide. List -II, reaction (4) product is an amide ( benzamide).
So, $R$ matches reaction (4)
$$ R-4 $$
$$ \text { (5) Cannizzaro reaction } $$
It is the reaction of base -catalyzed disproportionation of aldehydes that have no $\alpha$-hydrogens.
$$ 2 \,\,\mathrm{R}-\mathrm{CHO}\frac{\mathrm{conc} \cdot \mathrm{NaOH}}{\Delta} \mathrm{R}-\mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{OH}+\mathrm{R}-\mathrm{COONa} $$
$$ (R=H \text {, Ar or Alkyl group without } \alpha-H) $$
Reaction (1) product benzaldehyde is an aldehyde that lacks $\alpha$-hydrogen. So, $S$ matches with reaction (1).
$$ S-1 $$
Answer: Correct matching is
$$ P \rightarrow 2, Q \rightarrow 3, R \rightarrow 4, S \rightarrow 1 $$
Option (B)
