questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question:
Condensation of water vapour into water is influenced by which of the following factor/factors?
1Volume of air
2.Humidity
3.Temperature
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
Condensation occurs when the partial pressure of water vapour reaches its saturation value. That process can be driven by

changing the air volume (hence its pressure),

the existing humidity (vapour content), and

the temperature (which sets the saturation vapour pressure).

Hence all three factors are involved.

Answer: Option 4 (1, 2 and 3)

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: An object is placed between infinity and the pole (P) of a convex mirror. The position of the image is
options: between pole (P) and the focus (F), behind the mirror
between the focus (F) and infinity, behind the mirror
between the pole (P) and the infinity, in front of the mirror
at the focus (F), behind the mirror
questions.solution:
For a convex mirror the mirror‐formula

$$\frac1u + \frac1v = \frac1f$$

applies, with

$u>0$ (object in front of mirror),

$f<0$ (convex mirror focal length),

$v<0$ (image behind mirror).

If $u\to\infty$, then $1/v=1/f$ ⇒ $v=f$ (image at the focus, behind).

As $u$ decreases from ∞ toward 0, $\;1/v=1/f-1/u$ becomes more negative ⇒ $|v|$ decreases from $|f|$ toward 0.

So for any finite object distance the image lies between the pole $P$ and the focus $F$, behind the mirror.

Answer: Option 1.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: The work done in moving a charge of 2 coulomb (C) from point A to point B is 24 J. What is the potential difference between A and B?
questions.solution:
The potential difference between A and B is given by

$$ \Delta V \;=\;\frac{W}{q} $$

where

• $W = 24\text{ J}$ is the work done, and

• $q = 2\text{ C}$ is the charge.

Plugging in,

$$ \Delta V = \frac{24}{2} = 12\text{ V}. $$

So the correct choice is 12 V.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: Two conducting wires of the same material and of equal lengths and equal diameters are first connected in parallel and then in series in a circuit across the same potential difference. The ratio of heat produced in parallel and series combinations is
questions.solution:
Let the resistance of each wire be $R$.

Since both wires are identical:

  • In series, equivalent resistance:

$$ R_s = R + R = 2R $$

  • In parallel, equivalent resistance:

$$ R_p = \frac{R \cdot R}{R + R} = \frac{R}{2} $$

Now, heat produced in a circuit is given by Joule’s law:

$$ H = \frac{V^2 t}{R} $$

(where $V$ and $t$ are same in both cases)

So,

For parallel combination:

$$ H_p = \frac{V^2 t}{R_p} = \frac{V^2 t}{R/2} = \frac{2V^2 t}{R} $$

For series combination:

$$ H_s = \frac{V^2 t}{R_s} = \frac{V^2 t}{2R} $$

Now, ratio:

$$ \frac{H_p}{H_s} = \frac{2V^2 t / R}{V^2 t / (2R)} = 4 $$

So, the ratio is:

$$ 4:1 $$

Correct option: Option 2

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: The magnetic field inside a long straight solenoid-carrying current
options: decreases as we move towards its end
increases as we move towards its end
questions.solution:
The magnetic field in an ideal, infinitely long, tightly‐wound solenoid is the same everywhere inside. In fact, by Ampère’s law one finds

$$B \,=\, \mu_0\,n\,I$$

where

• μ₀ is the permeability of free space,

• n is the number of turns per unit length, and

• I is the current through the winding.

Because this expression has no dependence on the position along the axis, the field is uniform inside. (Near the very ends of a real, finite solenoid the field does fall off, but for a “long” solenoid those edge effects are negligible.)

Answer: Option 4.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: A car has an initial velocity of 12 m/s and is brought to rest over a distance of 45 m. The acceleration of the car is
questions.solution:
We can use the kinematic relation

$$v^2 = u^2 + 2as$$

where

• $u = 12$ m/s (initial speed)

• $v = 0$ m/s (final speed)

• $s = 45$ m (distance)

• $a$ is the (constant) acceleration

Plugging in:

$$0^2 = 12^2 + 2\,a\,(45)$$

$$0 = 144 + 90\,a$$

$$a = -\frac{144}{90} = -1.6\text{ m/s}^2$$

So the acceleration is $-1.6$ m/s² (Option 3).

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: The universal constant of gravitation G has the unit
questions.solution:
The correct choice is Option 4: N·m²/kg².

From Newton’s law of universal gravitation,

$$F = G \frac{m_1\,m_2}{r^2}$$

Solving for G gives

$$G = \frac{F\,r^2}{m_1\,m_2}$$

– Force F has units of newtons (N)

– Distance r has units of meters (m)

– Mass m₁, m₂ have units of kilograms (kg)

So

$$[G] = \frac{\text{N}\times\text{m}^2}{\text{kg}^2}.$$

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: Two bodies of unequal masses are dropped from a tower. At any instant, they have equal
questions.solution:
Concept Explanation:

When two objects are in free fall, ignoring air resistance, gravity is the primary force affecting their motion. Here are the key points to consider:

Acceleration: Every object in free fall experiences the same acceleration due to gravity, which is approximately 9.8 m/s², regardless of their mass.

Momentum: Momentum is defined as the product of mass and velocity (momentum = mass × velocity). Since the two bodies have different masses, their momenta will not be equal.

Kinetic Energy: This is calculated using the formula KE = (1/2) × mass × velocity². Given that the masses differ, the kinetic energies of the two bodies will also be different.

Potential Energy: The potential energy formula is PE = mass × g × height. Different masses equate to different potential energies, even if the height is the same.

Therefore, the only physical quantity that remains equal for both bodies is acceleration.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: The frequency (f), wavelength (λ) and speed (v) of a sound wave are related as
options: f=vλ
λ=vf
$f = \frac{\lambda}{v}$​
v=λf
questions.solution:
The correct relation is

$$v = \lambda\,f$$

so the answer is Option 4.

(You can also write it in the equivalent forms

• $$f = \frac{v}{\lambda}$$

• $$\lambda = \frac{v}{f}\,$$.)

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: The length of a simple pendulum is increased four times to its previous value while the mass is doubled. What is the ratio of the new and previous time period of the pendulum?
questions.solution:
Calculation:

The time period of a simple pendulum is given by the formula:

$ T_{\text{old}} = 2\pi\sqrt{\frac{L}{g}} $

If the length $ L $ of the pendulum is increased four times and the mass is doubled, we note that the time period is independent of the pendulum's mass. Therefore, the time period depends only on the length and gravitational acceleration.

For the new pendulum:

$ T_{\text{new}} = 2\pi\sqrt{\frac{4L}{g}} $

Simplifying this expression:

$ T_{\text{new}} = 2\pi\sqrt{4}\sqrt{\frac{L}{g}} $

$ T_{\text{new}} = 2 \times 2\pi\sqrt{\frac{L}{g}} $

Thus, comparing the initial and new time periods:

$ T_{\text{new}} = 2T_{\text{old}} $

The ratio of the new to the old time period is:

$ T_{\text{new}} : T_{\text{old}} = 2 : 1 $

Therefore, the ratio of the new and previous time periods is 2:1.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: Which one of the following is NOT a basic property of electric charge?
options: Charge on a body is always an integral multiple of an electron or a proton charge
Charges can be created and destroyed in an isolated system
questions.solution:
The statement that is NOT a basic property of electric charge is

Option 4: “Charges can be created and destroyed in an isolated system.”

Here’s why the others are basic properties:

Charges can be added: electric charges obey superposition, so net charge is the algebraic sum of individual charges.

Charge is conserved: in any process, total charge remains constant.

Charge quantization: any observable charge is an integer multiple of the elementary charge $e$.

By contrast, creation or destruction of net charge in isolation would violate charge conservation.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: What happens when the sunlight travels through the Earth’s atmosphere?
options: The blue colour is scattered more compared to the red colour
The red colour is scattered more compared to the blue colour
Both the blue and the red colours are scattered equally
The blue colour is not scattered but the red colour is scattered the most
questions.solution:
Concept:

Scattering of Light: This is a phenomenon where light rays are redirected in various directions when they encounter particles of a size similar to the wavelength of the light.

Rayleigh Scattering: When light scatters off molecules in the air or particles up to about a tenth of the wavelength of the light, it is known as Rayleigh scattering.

According to Rayleigh scattering:

$ \text{Scattering} \propto \frac{1}{{\lambda^4}} $

Where $\lambda$ is the wavelength of light.

For white light, the order of wavelength is:

Violet < Indigo < Blue < Green < Yellow < Orange < Red

Explanation:

Scattering is inversely proportional to the fourth power of the wavelength of light.

Among primary colors (blue, green, yellow, orange, and red), blue light has the shortest wavelength and is therefore scattered the most.

The scattered light in the sky is rich in blue light, which is why the sky appears blue. Hence, the correct conclusion aligns with this explanation.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question:
When a solid body is partially or completely immersed in a fluid, the fluid exerts an upward force on the body. The magnitude of the force is equal to
1.the mass of the body
2.the weight of the displaced fluid by the body
Which of the above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
The correct choice is 2 only.

By Archimedes’ principle, the buoyant force equals the weight of the fluid displaced:

$$F_{\rm b} = \rho_{\rm fluid}\,V_{\rm disp}\,g$$

where

• ρ₍fluid₎ is the fluid’s density

• V₍disp₎ is the volume of fluid displaced

• g is the acceleration due to gravity.

It is not equal to the mass of the body.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: If the length of a copper wire is increased by twice, then its resistivity will be
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option 3: Same.

• Resistivity (ρ) is an intrinsic property of the material—it doesn’t depend on the wire’s length or cross-section.

• Only the resistance

$$R = \rho\,\frac{L}{A}$$

changes when you alter length (L) or area (A).

• Doubling the length doubles the resistance, but ρ itself remains unchanged.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: Which one of the following is dimensionless quantity?
questions.solution:
Strain is the only dimensionless quantity among the four.

• Stress has units of force per area:

$$[{\rm Stress}] = \frac{F}{A} = \frac{MLT^{-2}}{L^2} = M\,L^{-1}\,T^{-2}.$$

• Pressure is the same dimensionally as stress:

$$[{\rm Pressure}] = M\,L^{-1}\,T^{-2}.$$

• Force has units:

$$[F] = M\,L\,T^{-2}.$$

• Strain is a ratio of lengths:

$$\text{Strain} = \frac{\Delta L}{L}$$

so all the length dimensions cancel, making it dimensionless.

Answer: Option 2 (Strain).

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: A system that does NOT allow exchange of heat with its surrounding is called
questions.solution:
Explanation:

Adiabatic Process:

An adiabatic process occurs in a thermodynamic system when there is no exchange of heat between the system and its surroundings. This means that throughout the adiabatic process, the system insulates itself against heat transfer.

Mathematically, this is represented by $ ΔQ = 0 $, indicating no heat transfer.

Additional Information:

Isothermal Process: In this process, the system undergoes changes such that its temperature remains constant.

Isobaric Process: This involves changes where the pressure remains constant.

Isochoric Process: Here, the system's volume remains constant during the change.

Isentropic Process:

In an isentropic process, the entropy of the system remains constant. It is also referred to as a constant-entropy process.

This process is both reversible and adiabatic, meaning no heat or matter is transferred.

It is an idealized concept in thermodynamics, often serving as a model to aspire to.

Entropy:

Entropy measures the amount of thermal energy in a system that cannot be used to perform work, per unit of temperature.

Over time, the entropy of the world tends to increase as systems move towards less equilibrium.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: If the speed of light in carbon disulfide and vacuum is XXXXX X X and YYYYY Y Y respectively, then
options: X < Y
X > Y
X ≥ Y
X = Y
questions.solution:
Light travels slower in a medium than in vacuum because its refractive index $n>1$. For carbon disulfide ($n_{\rm CS_2}\approx1.63$) we have

speed in CS₂: $X = \dfrac{c}{n_{\rm CS_2}}$

speed in vacuum: $Y = c$

Since $n_{\rm CS_2}>1$,

$$X = \frac{c}{n_{\rm CS_2}} < c = Y\,. $$

Answer: Option 1, $X < Y$.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: A current through a horizontal power line flows in east to west direction. What will be the direction of magnetic field at a point directly below it when viewed from east end?
options: Clockwise in a plane perpendicular to the wire
Anti-clockwise in a plane perpendicular to the wire
Clockwise in a plane of parallel to the wire
Anti-clockwise in a plane of parallel to the wire
questions.solution:
CONCEPT:

Right-hand thumb rule:

According to this rule, if you imagine holding the linear wire conductor in the grip of your right hand so that your thumb points in the direction of the current, the curvature of your fingers around the conductor will represent the direction of the magnetic field lines.

EXPLANATION:

If the current through a horizontal power line flows from east to west, the direction of the magnetic field at a point directly below it will be from north to south. Therefore, the correct option is anti-clockwise in a plane perpendicular to the wire.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: When a light ray passes through from air to water with a non-zero angle the ray will be
questions.solution:
When light goes from air (n₁≈1.00) into water (n₂≈1.33) at some angle θ₁ to the normal, Snell’s law tells us:

$$n_1\sin\theta_1 \;=\; n_2\sin\theta_2\,. $$

Because n₂>n₁, we have sin θ₂<sin θ₁ ⇒ θ₂<θ₁.

That means the refracted ray bends toward the normal.

Answer: Option 1 – bending towards the normal.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: Which one of the following equations related to the motion of an object is NOT correct? (Symbols carry their usual meanings)
options: u = v − at
u2 − v2 = 2as
Distance traveled during the nth second = u + (1/2)a(2n - 1)
questions.solution:
The equation that’s NOT correct is Option 3. The standard “third” equation of motion is

$$v^2 = u^2 + 2as$$

which can be rearranged as

$$u^2 - v^2 = -\,2as$$

—not $$u^2 - v^2 = 2as\,. $$

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: Which one of the following statements is NOT correct?
options: An ammeter is always connected in series in the circuit to measure the current
A Voltmeter is always connected to parallel in a circuit to measure the voltage
A voltmeter has a high resistance and an ammeter has a low resistance
A voltmeter has a low resistance and an ammeter has a high resistance
questions.solution:
The incorrect statement is Option 4.

Explanation:

An ammeter must have a low internal resistance and be connected in series so it doesn’t appreciably change the circuit current.

A voltmeter must have a high internal resistance and be connected in parallel so it doesn’t appreciably draw current.

Option 4 reverses these resistances and is therefore not correct.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: Which one of the following is a non-conventional source of energy?
questions.solution:
Non-conventional (renewable or alternative) sources are things like solar, wind, tidal, geothermal, etc.

• Petroleum, natural gas and coal are all conventional (fossil) fuels.

• Tidal energy harnesses the rise and fall of ocean tides, making it a renewable—hence non-conventional—source.

Answer: Option 3 (Tidal energy).

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: If three resistors of 1 Ohm each connect in parallel to each other the resultant resistance is
questions.solution:
For resistors in parallel, the reciprocal of the total resistance is the sum of reciprocals:

$$ \frac1{R_{\rm eq}} = \frac1{R_1} + \frac1{R_2} + \frac1{R_3} = \frac1{1} + \frac1{1} + \frac1{1} = 3 $$

So

$$ R_{\rm eq} = \frac1{3}\,\Omega. $$

That corresponds to Option 2: 1/3 Ohm.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: We hear an echo due to
questions.solution:
Concept:

Echo: An echo occurs when a sound wave reflects off a surface and is heard a short time after the original sound. This delay is caused by the time it takes for the sound wave to travel to the surface and back.

Reflection of sound: When sound waves encounter a hard surface, they bounce back, producing a reflected sound wave.

Conditions for Echo Formation:

The distance between the sound source and the reflecting surface should be at least 17.2 meters, assuming the speed of sound is approximately 344 m/s.

The reflecting surface should be large and hard to effectively reflect the sound.

Therefore, the phenomenon of hearing an echo is explained by the reflection of sound waves.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: At uniform speed the acceleration is
questions.solution:
Option 3: Zero

Since acceleration is the rate of change of velocity, for uniform speed (and straight-line motion) we have

$$a = \frac{dv}{dt} = 0\,. $$

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: The focal length of a concave lens is 0.5 m. The power of the lens is
questions.solution:
For a lens, power is given by

$$ P = \frac{1}{f} $$

where $f$ is in metre.

Since it is a concave lens, its focal length is negative:

$$ f = -0.5 \, \text{m} $$

Now,

$$ P = \frac{1}{-0.5} = -2 \, \text{D} $$

So, the correct answer is:

$$ \boxed{-2.0 \, \text{D}} $$

Option 4

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: The rule to determine the direction of a force experienced by a straight current carrying conductor placed in a magnetic field which is perpendicular to it is
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

Option 2: Fleming’s left-hand rule

Explanation:

When a straight current-carrying conductor is placed in a magnetic field perpendicular to it, it experiences a force.

To find the direction of this force, we use Fleming’s left-hand rule.

Fleming’s left-hand rule:

Stretch the thumb, forefinger, and middle finger of your left hand so that they are mutually perpendicular.

Then:

  • Forefinger shows the direction of magnetic field

  • Middle finger shows the direction of current

  • Thumb shows the direction of force or motion

Why other options are wrong:

  • Right-hand thumb rule: Used to find the direction of magnetic field around a current-carrying conductor.

  • Fleming’s right-hand rule: Used for direction of induced current in electromagnetic induction.

  • Hund’s rule: Related to filling of electrons in atomic orbitals.

So, the answer is:

$$\boxed{\text{Fleming’s left-hand rule}}$$

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: One parsec in astronomical units (A.U.) is about
questions.solution:
We know:

  • $1 \, \text{parsec} = 3.26 \, \text{light years}$

  • $1 \, \text{light year} \approx 6.3 \times 10^4 \, \text{A.U.}$

So,

$$ 1 \, \text{parsec} = 3.26 \times 6.3 \times 10^4 \, \text{A.U.} $$

$$ \approx 20.5 \times 10^4 \, \text{A.U.} $$

$$ = 2.05 \times 10^5 \, \text{A.U.} $$

So, one parsec is about:

$$ 2 \times 10^5 \, \text{A.U.} $$

Correct option: Option 3

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: Which one of the following statements for second law of motion is NOT correct ?
options: The net force on a body is proportional to that body’s acceleration
The net force on a body is in the same direction as the acceleration
The time rate of change of momentum is equal to force
The net force on a body is proportional to that body’s momentum
questions.solution:
The statement which is NOT correct is:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 4: The net force on a body is proportional to that body’s momentum}}$$

Step-by-step:

According to Newton’s Second Law of Motion:

$$F = \frac{dp}{dt}$$

This means:

  • Force is equal to the time rate of change of momentum

  • If mass is constant, then:

$$F = ma$$

So:

  1. Option 1 is correct because force is proportional to acceleration.

  2. Option 2 is correct because acceleration is in the same direction as net force.

  3. Option 3 is correct because it is the actual statement of the second law.

  4. Option 4 is incorrect because force is not proportional to momentum, but to the rate of change of momentum.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 4}}$$

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: The temperature of a body increases  from 310 K to 340 K. The temperature increase in degree Celsius is
questions.solution:
Temperature difference in kelvin and degree Celsius is the same.

Given:

$$ T_1 = 310\ \text{K}, \quad T_2 = 340\ \text{K} $$

Increase in temperature:

$$ \Delta T = T_2 - T_1 = 340 - 310 = 30\ \text{K} $$

So, the increase in degree Celsius is also:

$$ 30^\circ \text{C} $$

Correct option: 2. 30°C

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: Which one of the following statements for the work done by gravity on a body is NOT correct ?
options: Work done is independent of the path followed by the body
Work done depends only on the vertical distance separating the initial and the final position of the body
Work done depends on the path followed by the body
Work done is zero if the body is displaced horizontally to the force of gravity
questions.solution:
The incorrect statement is:

Option 3: Work done depends on the path followed by the body

Step-by-step explanation

Gravity is a conservative force. For a conservative force:

  • Work done does not depend on the path

  • It depends only on the initial and final positions

For gravity, work done is given by:

$$ W = mg(h_1 - h_2) $$

where:

  • $m$ = mass of the body

  • $g$ = acceleration due to gravity

  • $h_1$ = initial height

  • $h_2$ = final height

So:

  • If only the vertical height changes, gravity does work.

  • If the displacement is purely horizontal, then there is no change in height, so work done by gravity is zero.

Checking each option

  1. Work done is independent of the path followed by the body

    This is correct.

  2. Work done depends only on the vertical distance separating the initial and the final position of the body

    This is correct.

  3. Work done depends on the path followed by the body

    This is not correct.

  4. Work done is zero if the body is displaced horizontally to the force of gravity

    This is correct.

Final answer

$$ \boxed{\text{Option 3}} $$

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: Which one among the following statements is correct ?
options: Pressure has magnitude as well as direction:
As we go deeper in a liquid, the pressure decreases
Action and reaction forces act on the same object
Pascal and N/m<sup>2</sup> represent the same unit
questions.solution:
The correct statement is:

$$\text{Option 4: Pascal and } N/m^2 \text{ represent the same unit}$$

Now let us check each option one by one:

  1. Pressure has magnitude as well as direction

    This is wrong because pressure is a scalar quantity.

    It has only magnitude, not direction.

  2. As we go deeper in a liquid, the pressure decreases

    This is wrong.

    In a liquid, pressure increases with depth.

  3. Action and reaction forces act on the same object

    This is wrong.

    According to Newton’s third law, action and reaction act on different objects.

  4. Pascal and $N/m^2$ represent the same unit

    This is correct because

$$1 \, \text{pascal} = 1 \, N/m^2$$

So, the correct answer is Option 4.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: Which one of the following wavelength radiations may correspond to an X-ray ?
questions.solution:
X-rays have very short wavelengths, usually in the range of about

$$ 0.01 \text{ nm to } 10 \text{ nm} $$

Now check the options:

  • $1 \text{ nm}$: lies in the X-ray range

  • $10 \text{ nm}$: at the extreme end, but usually not taken as the standard answer here

  • $100 \text{ nm}$: ultraviolet region

  • $300 \text{ nm}$: ultraviolet region

So, the correct answer is:

$$ \boxed{1 \text{ nm}} $$

Option 1.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: In a Telescope, compared to the eyepiece lens the objective lens has
options: larger focal length and larger aperture
larger focal length and smaller aperture
smaller focal length and larger aperture
smaller focal length and smaller aperture
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

$$\text{Option 1: larger focal length and larger aperture}$$

Step-by-step:

  1. In a telescope, the objective lens is the lens facing the distant object.

  2. Its job is to collect as much light as possible from faraway objects.

  3. So, it must have a large aperture.

Also:

  1. The objective lens forms a real, inverted, and diminished image of the distant object.

  2. For better magnification in a telescope, the objective lens should have a large focal length.

  3. The eyepiece acts like a magnifying glass, so it has a small focal length.

Therefore, compared to the eyepiece, the objective lens has:

  • larger focal length

  • larger aperture

Hence, the answer is:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 1}}$$

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: The energy, E, of a photon can be expressed as E = hf where f is the frequency and h is Planck’s constant. The dimensions of h are the same as that of
questions.solution:
Given,

$$ E = hf $$

We need the dimensions of $h$.

Step 1: Write dimensions of energy

Energy has the dimensions:

$$ [E] = ML^2T^{-2} $$

Step 2: Write dimensions of frequency

Frequency is the number of oscillations per second, so:

$$ [f] = T^{-1} $$

Step 3: Find dimensions of $h$

From

$$ h = \frac{E}{f} $$

So,

$$ [h] = \frac{ML^2T^{-2}}{T^{-1}} = ML^2T^{-1} $$

Step 4: Compare with given options

  • Linear momentum:

$$ p = mv $$

Dimensions:

$$ [p] = MLT^{-1} $$

Not same.

  • Angular momentum:

$$ L = mvr $$

Dimensions:

$$ [L] = MLT^{-1} \cdot L = ML^2T^{-1} $$

Same.

  • Displacement:

$$ [L] $$

Not same.

  • Torque:

$$ \tau = \text{force} \times \text{distance} $$

Dimensions:

$$ [\tau] = (MLT^{-2})(L) = ML^2T^{-2} $$

Not same.

Correct option:

$$ \boxed{\text{Option 2: angular momentum}} $$

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: The work done by the force acting on an object is zero if the displacement of the object
options: is in the opposite direction of the direction of force
is in the same direction of the direction of force
is in perpendicular direction of the direction of force
questions.solution:
Work done is given by

$$ W = F s \cos \theta $$

where:

  • $F$ = force

  • $s$ = displacement

  • $\theta$ = angle between force and displacement

For work done to be zero,

$$ \cos \theta = 0 $$

This happens when

$$ \theta = 90^\circ $$

That means the displacement is perpendicular to the direction of force.

So, the correct answer is:

Option 3: is in perpendicular direction of the direction of force

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: The magnetic field inside a current carrying very long solenoid is
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

Option 1: Uniform

For a very long current-carrying solenoid, the magnetic field inside is nearly the same at all points inside it.

According to NCERT, the magnetic field lines inside a long solenoid are:

  • parallel

  • equally spaced

  • directed along the axis of the solenoid

This shows that the magnetic field inside is uniform.

So, the answer is:

$$\boxed{\text{Uniform}}$$

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: Which one of the following instruments can be used to measure —250°C temperature ?
options: By using a mercury based thermometer
By using an alcohol based thermometer
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

$$\text{Option 4: By using thermocouple based thermometer}$$

Step by step:

  1. $-250^\circ \text{C}$ is an extremely low temperature.

  2. A mercury thermometer cannot be used because mercury freezes at about

$$-39^\circ \text{C}$$

  1. An alcohol thermometer can measure lower temperatures than mercury, but not as low as $-250^\circ \text{C}$.

  2. A clinical thermometer is used only for measuring human body temperature, usually in the range

$$35^\circ \text{C} \text{ to } 42^\circ \text{C}$$

  1. A thermocouple thermometer is used for very high as well as very low temperatures.

Therefore, to measure $-250^\circ \text{C}$, we use

$$\boxed{\text{thermocouple based thermometer}}$$

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: The quantity of heat needed to change unit mass of a substance from liquid to vapour without changing the temperature, is called
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option 2: specific latent heat.

Explanation:

The quantity of heat needed to change unit mass of a substance from liquid to vapour without any change in temperature is called specific latent heat of vaporisation.

In NCERT terms:

  • Latent heat: Heat absorbed or released during change of state.

  • Specific latent heat: Heat required for unit mass of a substance to change its state without change in temperature.

So here, since the change is from liquid to vapour, it is specific latent heat.

Therefore,

$$ \text{Correct option} = \text{Option 2} $$

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: Which one of the following statements about surface tension is correct ?
options: It will remain same with increase or decrease of temperature
It will always decrease with increase or decrease of temperature
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

Option 2: It decreases when temperature increases.

Explanation:

Surface tension is the property of a liquid surface due to which it behaves like a stretched membrane.

When temperature increases, the molecules of the liquid gain more kinetic energy and move more freely. Because of this, the intermolecular force of attraction becomes less effective at the surface, so the surface tension decreases.

Therefore:

$$ \text{Surface tension decreases with increase in temperature} $$

So, the correct option is Option 2.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: A body sinks in water when
options: density of water is greater than density of body
density of water is less than density of body
density of water is equal to density of body
questions.solution:
A body sinks in water when its density is greater than the density of water.

So, the correct answer is:

Option 2: density of water is less than density of body

Reason:

According to the NCERT concept of flotation:

  • If density of body $>$ density of liquid, the body sinks

  • If density of body $<$ density of liquid, the body floats

  • If density of body $=$ density of liquid, the body may remain suspended

Here, for sinking in water:

$$ \text{Density of body} > \text{Density of water} $$

which means

$$ \text{Density of water} < \text{Density of body} $$

Hence, Option 2 is correct.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: Why a fielder in the ground gradually pulls his hands backward while catching a fast moving cricket ball ?
options: To reduce the acceleration of the ball
To increase the acceleration of the ball
To increase the velocity of the ball
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option 1: To reduce the acceleration of the ball.

Explanation:

When a fielder catches a fast-moving cricket ball, the ball has a large momentum. To stop it, the fielder has to bring its momentum to zero.

We know:

$$ \text{Force} = \frac{\Delta p}{\Delta t} $$

where:

  • $\Delta p$ = change in momentum

  • $\Delta t$ = time taken

The change in momentum is fixed because the ball has to be stopped. So, if the fielder pulls his hands backward, the time of stopping $\Delta t$ increases.

When time increases, the force decreases.

Also, since acceleration is related to force by

$$ F = ma $$

less force means less acceleration (actually less retardation).

So, by moving his hands backward, the fielder reduces the acceleration of the ball, and therefore the force on his hands becomes less. This prevents injury.

Final Answer:

$$ \boxed{\text{Option 1: To reduce the acceleration of the ball}} $$

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: Bottom of a tank containing water appears to be raised. It is due to
questions.solution:
It is due to Option 2: Refraction of light.

Explanation:

When light rays come from the bottom of the tank and pass from water to air, they bend at the surface because of refraction.

Since the rays bend away from the normal on coming from a denser medium (water) to a rarer medium (air), the bottom appears to be at a smaller depth than its real depth.

So, the bottom of the tank appears raised.

Correct answer:

$$\text{Refraction of light}$$

questions.question: Which one of the following radiations has longer wavelength ?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

Option 3: Micro Wave

Reason:

In the electromagnetic spectrum, wavelength increases in this order:

$$ \text{X-rays} < \text{Ultraviolet} < \text{Infra-red} < \text{Microwaves} $$

So, among the given options, microwaves have the longest wavelength.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: For a material under static conditions, which one among the following statements is correct ?
options: Both perfect conductor and insulator can be charged
Both perfect conductor and insulator cannot be charged
questions.solution:
Static conditions mean that electric charges are not moving. They stay still.

In an insulator, charges cannot move freely. They stay in one place where they are put.

This means an insulator can keep an electric charge when conditions are static.

In a conductor, charges can move around easily. This makes it hard to keep the charges in one spot.

⇒ Under static conditions, an insulator can be charged.

Therefore, the correct answer is Option 1.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: The energy is always conserved for a system which is
questions.solution:
Energy stays the same, or is "conserved," in a system when it doesn’t exchange energy or matter with anything outside of it.

An "isolated system" is a system where this no-exchange condition is met.

In an isolated system, the total energy doesn’t change over time, which means energy conservation is always present.

For systems that are "non-isolated," energy can move in and out, so energy conservation isn't guaranteed for just that system.

Therefore, the correct answer is Option 1: Isolated only.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: Suppose, a ball of mass M is thrown upwards from a point A and it reaches up to the highest point B and returns back to point A, which one among the following is correct ?
options: Kinetic Energy at A = Potential Energy at B
Kinetic Energy at A = Potential Energy at A
Kinetic Energy at B = Potential Energy at B
Kinetic Energy at B = Kinetic Energy at A
questions.solution:
The correct option is:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 1: Kinetic Energy at A = Potential Energy at B}}$$

Step-by-step explanation:

Let the ball be thrown upward from point $A$ with initial speed $u$.

At point $A$:

  • The ball has kinetic energy

$$KE_A = \frac{1}{2}Mu^2$$

  • If we take point $A$ as the reference level, then

$$PE_A = 0$$

At highest point $B$:

  • The velocity becomes zero, so

$$KE_B = 0$$

  • Let the height reached be $h$, then potential energy at $B$ is

$$PE_B = Mgh$$

Now, by conservation of mechanical energy:

$$\frac{1}{2}Mu^2 = Mgh$$

So,

$$KE_A = PE_B$$

Hence, Option 1 is correct.

Why other options are wrong:

  • Option 2: At point $A$, potential energy is usually taken as zero, so it is not equal to kinetic energy.

  • Option 3: At point $B$, kinetic energy is zero but potential energy is not zero.

  • Option 4: At point $B$, kinetic energy is zero, but at $A$ it is maximum.

$$\boxed{\text{Correct Answer: Option 1}}$$

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: The Constitution (Seventy-third Amendment) Act provides for
options: an elaborate system of establishing municipal self-government
an elaborate system of establishing panchayats as units of self-government
establishing a Commission for Linguistic Minorities
the creation of the Jharkhand State
questions.solution:
The correct choice is Option 2.

Key points of the 73rd Amendment (1992):

It inserted Part IX (Articles 243–243O) and the Eleventh Schedule into the Constitution.

It mandates a three-tier Panchayati Raj system (village, intermediate, district levels).

It provides for regular elections, reservation of seats (SC/ST and women), State Finance Commissions, and Gram Sabhas.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: Which one of the following statements about the Parliament of India is correct ?
options: A majority of members of Rajya Sabha are elected by a system of proportional representation by means of single transferable vote
The Deputy Chairperson of the Rajya Sabha is nominated by the President from among the members of the Rajya Sabha
The Lok Sabha cannot be dissolved before the completion of its five year term
The members of Lok Sabha also vote in the election of the members of Rajya Sabha
questions.solution:
The correct statement is Option 1.

• Rajya Sabha members (except the President’s nominees) are chosen by elected members of State Legislative Assemblies (and certain Union Territory legislators)

• The voting method is proportional representation by means of the single transferable vote

Why the others are wrong:

The Deputy Chairman of Rajya Sabha is elected by its members, not nominated by the President.

Lok Sabha can be dissolved early by the President on the Prime Minister’s advice.

Only State/UT legislators (not Lok Sabha MPs) vote for Rajya Sabha elections.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question:
Consider the following features :
1.Consent of the governed
2.Political equality
3.Accountability of the ruled to the ruler

Which of the above feature/features outline democratic rule?
questions.solution:
The correct choice is Option 1: 1 and 2 only.

Why?

Consent of the governed is fundamental to democracy.

Political equality (one person, one vote) is another core democratic principle.

Statement 3 (“accountability of the ruled to the ruler”) is the reverse of how democracy works—rulers are accountable to the people, not vice versa.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question:
Placing the earliest first, arrange the following countries in the chronological order in which they granted universal adult franchise :
1.USA
2.Sri Lanka
3.Japan
4.India
Select the answer using the code given below :
questions.solution:
The chronological order (earliest first) in which these countries enacted universal adult franchise is:

USA – 1920 (19th Amendment extended voting to women; 15th Amendment of 1870 had already enfranchised Black men in principle)

Sri Lanka – 1931 (Ceylon Constitution introduced full adult franchise)

Japan – 1945 (post-war reforms granted women the vote; first election under this was in 1946)

India – 1950 (Constitution came into force on 26 Jan 1950 with universal adult franchise)

So the correct sequence is 1, 2, 3, 4 (Option 1).

Amide ( $-\mathrm{CO}-\mathrm{NH}-$ ) bond is present. It is known as peptide bond It is a dipeptide.
This gives ninhydrin test. The result is a purple color.
Ninhydrin test is used to detect the presence of amines in a sample.

The compound also reacts with $\mathrm{FeCl}_3$ to give violet color. It is due to the free - OH group. It is a characteristic reaction of phenols
So, The compound $P$ reaction with ninhydrin gives purple color and it also reacts with $\mathrm{FeCl}_3$ to give violet color.

But the compound does not give phthalein dye test.
Phthalein dye test - It is used to identify the presence of phenolic functional groups. The compound has no free -OH group and hence it does not give phthalein dye test. For phthalein dye test, $-O H$ group must be attached directly to the momatic ring.
So, The compound Q after complete hydrolysis give ninhydrin test and it does not give positive phthalein dye test.

Aniline hydrochloride reacts with phenyl diazonium salt (benzene diazonium salt) and forms a dye ( $p$-aminoarobenzene) with yellow color.
So, compound $R$ reaction with phenyl diazonium salt gives yellow dye.
$$ R-1 $$
It is a hydrazine. It reacts with glucose and give corresponding hydrazone.
$$ \text { It can be simply written as. } $$
So, S reaction with glucose give corresponding hydrazone.
$S-3$
