questions.title: BITSAT 2025
questions.question: What is the probability of getting a sum of 9 in a single throw of three fair dice?
questions.solution:
To get a sum of 9 ,possible dice combinations are

$$ \begin{array}{ll} (1,3,6) & -6 \text { ways } \\ (1,3,5) & -5 \text { ways } \\ (1,4,4) & -3 \text { ways } \\ (2,3,5) & -3 \text { ways } \\ (2,3,4) & -6 \text { ways } \\ (3,3,3) & -1 \text { way } \end{array} $$

Total ways $=6+6+3+3+6+1=25$

Total possible outcomes when rolling three dice $=6 \times 6 \times 6=216$.

Number of favourable outcomes $=25$

∴ Probability of getting a sum of 9 in a single throw of three fair dice $=\frac{25}{216}$

questions.title: BITSAT 2025
questions.question: In a binomial distribution, the mean is 10 and the variance is 6 . Then, its median is
questions.solution:
We know that

Mean, $\mu=n p=10\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,...(i)$

Variance, $\sigma^2=n p(1-p)=6\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,...(ii)$

Dividing Eq. (ii) by Eq. (i),

$$ \begin{gathered} (1-p)=\frac{6}{10}=0.6 \\ p=0.4 \end{gathered} $$

Substituting $p=0.4 \mathrm{in}$ Eq. (i),

$$ \begin{aligned} n \times 0.4 & =10 \\ n & =25 \end{aligned} $$

The median of a binomial distribution is typically approximated by

$$ M \approx[n p] $$

Substituting values

$$ M=[25 \times 0.4]=[10] $$

Thus, the median is 10 .

questions.title: BITSAT 2025
questions.question: A four digit number is formed with digits $1,3,4$, 5 with no repetition. What is the probability that the number is divisible by 5 ?
questions.solution:
Total number of ways in which four digit number can be found $=4!=24$

So, exhaustive number of cases $=24$

Now, the number is divisible by 5 if at units place we have 5.

∴ Unit place can be filled in one way the remaining 3 places can be filled with other digits in 3! ways.

So, the number of ways of forming the number divisible by 5 is $3!=6$

So, the required probability $=\frac{6}{24}=\frac{1}{4}$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2025 Paper 1 Online
questions.question:
Three students $S_1, S_2,$ and $S_3$ are given a problem to solve. Consider the following events:

U: At least one of $S_1, S_2,$ and $S_3$ can solve the problem,

V: $S_1$ can solve the problem, given that neither $S_2$ nor $S_3$ can solve the problem,

W: $S_2$ can solve the problem and $S_3$ cannot solve the problem,

T: $S_3$ can solve the problem.

For any event $E$, let $P(E)$ denote the probability of $E$. If

$P(U) = \dfrac{1}{2}$ , $P(V) = \dfrac{1}{10}$ , and $P(W) = \dfrac{1}{12}$,

then $P(T)$ is equal to
questions.solution:
We start with the given probabilities:

$ P(U) = \frac{1}{2} $

$ P(V) = \frac{1}{10} $

$ P(W) = \frac{1}{12} $

The event $ U $ indicates that at least one of the students can solve the problem. Thus, its complement $ U' $ (none of the students can solve the problem) is expressed as:

$ \mathrm{P}(U') = \mathrm{P}(S_1' \cap S_2' \cap S_3') = 1 - P(U) = \frac{1}{2} $

The expression for the probability of the complement is:

$ \mathrm{P}(S_1') \cdot \mathrm{P}(S_2') \cdot \mathrm{P}(S_3') = \frac{1}{2} $

From this, we understand:

$ (1 - \mathrm{P}(S_1))(1 - \mathrm{P}(S_2))(1 - \mathrm{P}(S_3)) = \frac{1}{2} \quad \text{(Equation 1)} $

Given $ P(V) $, which is the probability that $ S_1 $ solves the problem given that neither $ S_2 $ nor $ S_3 $ can, we have:

$ \mathrm{P}(V) = \frac{\mathrm{P}(S_1 \cap S_2' \cap S_3')}{\mathrm{P}(S_2' \cap S_3')} = \frac{1}{10} $

$ \Rightarrow \mathrm{P}(S_1) \cdot \mathrm{P}(S_2') \cdot \mathrm{P}(S_3') = \frac{1}{10} \cdot \mathrm{P}(S_2' \cap S_3') $

Now, calculate $ \mathrm{P}(S_2' \cap S_3') $:

From $ P(W) $:

$ \mathrm{P}(W) = \mathrm{P}(S_2 \cap S_3') = \frac{1}{12} $

Combining Equations from conditions of complementary events:

$ \mathbf{\frac{P(S_1)}{1 - \mathrm{P}(S_1)} = \frac{1}{10}} $

Next, let's solve:

$ \begin{aligned} & (1 - \mathrm{P}(S_2)) (1 - \mathrm{P}(S_3)) = \frac{5}{9} \\ & \text{Given, using } \mathrm{P}(W), \; \mathrm{P}(S_2) \cdot \mathrm{P}(S_3') = \frac{1}{12} \\ & \text{Therefore, from equivalence} \left(\frac{\mathrm{P}(S_2)}{1 - \mathrm{P}(S_2)} = \frac{1}{12} \times \frac{9}{5}\right) \end{aligned} $

From simplification:

$ \begin{aligned} & \mathrm{P}(S_2) = \frac{3}{23} \\ & \frac{3}{23} \cdot (1 - \mathrm{P}(S_3)) = \frac{1}{12} \\ & 1 - \mathrm{P}(S_3) = \frac{23}{36} \\ & \mathrm{P}(S_3) = \frac{13}{36} \end{aligned} $

Thus, the probability that student $ S_3 $ can solve the problem, denoted by $ P(T) $, is:

$ P(T) = 1 - \mathrm{P}(S_3') = \frac{13}{36} $

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2025 Paper 2 Online
questions.question:
A factory has a total of three manufacturing units, $M_1, M_2$, and $M_3$, which produce bulbs independent of each other. The units $M_1, M_2$, and $M_3$ produce bulbs in the proportions of $2: 2: 1$, respectively. It is known that $20 \%$ of the bulbs produced in the factory are defective. It is also known that, of all the bulbs produced by $M_1, 15 \%$ are defective. Suppose that, if a randomly chosen bulb produced in the factory is found to be defective, the probability that it was produced by $M_2$ is $\frac{2}{5}$.

If a bulb is chosen randomly from the bulbs produced by $M_3$, then the probability that it is defective is __________.
questions.solution:
$H_1$: The bulb is produced by unit $M_1$.

$H_2$: The bulb is produced by unit $M_2$.

$H_3$: The bulb is produced by unit $M_3$.

$E$: The bulb is defective.

The unit production proportions and known probabilities are:

$P(H_1) = \frac{2}{5}$, $P(H_2) = \frac{2}{5}$, $P(H_3) = \frac{1}{5}$

$P(E | H_1) = \frac{15}{100} = \frac{3}{20}$

The total probability of a defective bulb is given by:

$ P(E) = P(E | H_1) \cdot P(H_1) + P(E | H_2) \cdot P(H_2) + P(E | H_3) \cdot P(H_3) $

Given:

$ P(E) = 0.20 = \frac{3}{20} \cdot \frac{2}{5} + P(E | H_2) \cdot \frac{2}{5} + P(E | H_3) \cdot \frac{1}{5} $

This simplifies to:

$ 2P(E | H_2) + P(E | H_3) = \frac{7}{10} \quad \text{...(i)} $

Additionally, the probability that a defective bulb is from $M_2$ is given as:

$ P(H_2 | E) = \frac{2}{5} = \frac{P(E | H_2) \cdot \frac{2}{5}}{\frac{1}{5}} $

Solving the equation above gives:

$ P(E | H_2) = \frac{1}{5} \quad \text{...(ii)} $

Using the equations (i) and (ii), we substitute $P(E | H_2)$ into (i):

$ 2 \cdot \frac{1}{5} + P(E | H_3) = \frac{7}{10} $

Solving for $P(E | H_3)$:

$ \frac{2}{5} + P(E | H_3) = \frac{7}{10} $

Converting $\frac{2}{5}$ to $\frac{4}{10}$ gives:

$ \frac{4}{10} + P(E | H_3) = \frac{7}{10} $

$ P(E | H_3) = \frac{7}{10} - \frac{4}{10} = \frac{3}{10} $

Therefore, the probability that a randomly chosen bulb from $M_3$ is defective is 0.30.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question: What is the value of k?
questions.solution:
Let $X\sim \mathrm{Bin}(n=6,p=k)$. Recall the binomial probability mass function:

$ P(X=r)=\binom{6}{r}\,k^r\,(1-k)^{6-r}. $

We are given

$ 9\,P(X=4)=P(X=2). $

Write out $P(X=4)$ and $P(X=2)$:

$ P(X=4)=\binom{6}{4}\,k^4\,(1-k)^2, \quad P(X=2)=\binom{6}{2}\,k^2\,(1-k)^4. $

Substitute into the given relation:

$ 9\;\binom{6}{4}\,k^4\,(1-k)^2 \;=\; \binom{6}{2}\,k^2\,(1-k)^4. $

Note that $\binom{6}{4}=\binom{6}{2}=15$. Cancel these common factors:

$ 9\,k^4\,(1-k)^2 =k^2\,(1-k)^4. $

Divide both sides by $k^2\,(1-k)^2$ (assuming $0

$ 9\,k^{4-2} = (1-k)^{4-2} \quad\Longrightarrow\quad 9\,k^2=(1-k)^2. $

Take square roots (we take the positive root since $k>0$):

$ 3k = 1-k \quad\Longrightarrow\quad 4k=1 \quad\Longrightarrow\quad k=\tfrac14. $

Thus the required value of $k$ is $\displaystyle \frac14$.

Answer: Option C.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question: What is the value of P * (X = 3) ^ 0
questions.solution:
Write the given probabilities.

For $X\sim \mathrm{Bin}(6,p)$,

$ P(X=4) = \binom{6}{4}p^4(1-p)^2 = 15\,p^4(1-p)^2, $

$ P(X=2) = \binom{6}{2}p^2(1-p)^4 = 15\,p^2(1-p)^4. $

Use the condition $9\,P(X=4)=P(X=2)$.

$ 9\cdot 15\,p^4(1-p)^2 \;=\; 15\,p^2(1-p)^4 $

Divide both sides by $15\,p^2(1-p)^2$ (assuming $0

$ 9\,p^2 = (1-p)^2. $

Taking square roots (and choosing the positive root since $p>0$):

$ 3p = 1-p \quad\Longrightarrow\quad 4p = 1 \quad\Longrightarrow\quad p = \tfrac14. $

Compute $P(X=3)$.

$ P(X=3) = \binom{6}{3}p^3(1-p)^3 = 20\bigl(\tfrac14\bigr)^3\bigl(\tfrac34\bigr)^3 = 20\cdot \frac{1}{64}\cdot \frac{27}{64} = \frac{20\cdot27}{4096} = \frac{540}{4096} = \frac{135}{1024}. $

Answer: $\displaystyle \frac{135}{1024}$. (Option A)

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question: What is the probability that the committee includes exactly 3 gentlemen?
questions.solution:
Total number of ways to form a committee of 6 from 7 gentlemen and 4 ladies (11 persons) is

$$\binom{11}{6} = \frac{11!}{6!\,5!} = 462.$$

We want exactly 3 gentlemen (and hence 3 ladies).

Number of ways to choose 3 gentlemen out of 7:

$$\binom{7}{3} = \frac{7!}{3!\,4!} = 35.$$

Number of ways to choose 3 ladies out of 4:

$$\binom{4}{3} = \frac{4!}{3!\,1!} = 4.$$

Total favourable ways =

$$\binom{7}{3}\times\binom{4}{3} = 35\times4 = 140.$$

Therefore, the required probability is

$$ \frac{\text{favourable ways}}{\text{total ways}} = \frac{140}{462} = \frac{140\div14}{462\div14} = \frac{10}{33}. $$

Answer: Option A (10/33).

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question: What is the probability that the committee includes at least 2 ladies?
questions.solution:
Total number of ways to form a committee of 6 from 7 gentlemen and 4 ladies

$$\text{Total}=\binom{11}{6}$$

Calculate total ways:

$$\binom{11}{6}=\frac{11!}{6!\,5!}=462$$

Use the complement (“fewer than 2 ladies”)

• Case 1: 0 ladies (all men)

$$\binom{4}{0}\times\binom{7}{6}=1\times7=7$$

• Case 2: 1 lady

$$\binom{4}{1}\times\binom{7}{5}=4\times21=84$$

Total unwanted = $7+84=91$

Desired number of committees (at least 2 ladies)

$$462-91=371$$

Probability

$$P=\frac{371}{462}$$

Simplify the fraction by 7:

$$\frac{371\div7}{462\div7}=\frac{53}{66}$$

Answer: Option D, $\displaystyle \tfrac{53}{66}$.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question: What is the probability that the bonus scheme will be introduced?
questions.solution:
Let $A,B,C$ be the events “A becomes manager”, “B becomes manager”, “C becomes manager”, and let $D$ be the event “bonus scheme is introduced”. By the law of total probability,

Write down the given probabilities:

• $P(A)=\tfrac{3}{10},\;P(B)=\tfrac12,\;P(C)=\tfrac{4}{5}.$

• $P(D\mid A)=\tfrac{4}{9},\;P(D\mid B)=\tfrac{2}{9},\;P(D\mid C)=\tfrac{1}{3}.$

Apply the law of total probability:

$ P(D) =P(A)\,P(D\mid A)+P(B)\,P(D\mid B)+P(C)\,P(D\mid C). $

Substitute and simplify:

$ P(D) =\frac{3}{10}\times\frac{4}{9} +\frac12\times\frac{2}{9} +\frac{4}{5}\times\frac{1}{3} =\frac{12}{90}+\frac{2}{18}+\frac{4}{15}. $

Convert to a common denominator $90$:

$ \frac{12}{90}+\frac{10}{90}+\frac{24}{90} =\frac{46}{90} =\frac{23}{45}. $

Answer: $\displaystyle \frac{23}{45}$. (Option C)

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question: If the bonus scheme has been introduced, then what is the probability that the manager appointed was B?
questions.solution:
Let us denote by

$M_A,M_B,M_C$ the events “A is appointed manager”, “B is appointed manager”, “C is appointed manager”

and by $B$ the event “bonus scheme is introduced”.

According to the (intended) data,

• $P(M_A)=\tfrac{4}{9},\;P(M_B)=\tfrac{2}{9},\;P(M_C)=\tfrac{1}{3}.$

• $P(B\mid M_A)=\tfrac{3}{10},\;P(B\mid M_B)=\tfrac{1}{2},\;P(B\mid M_C)=\tfrac{4}{5}.$

(These choices make $P(M_A)+P(M_B)+P(M_C)=1$.)

Compute the total probability of introducing the bonus scheme:

$ P(B) =P(M_A)\,P(B\mid M_A)\;+\;P(M_B)\,P(B\mid M_B)\;+\;P(M_C)\,P(B\mid M_C). $

Substituting,

$ P(B) =\frac{4}{9}\cdot\frac{3}{10} +\frac{2}{9}\cdot\frac{1}{2} +\frac{1}{3}\cdot\frac{4}{5} =\frac{12}{90}+\frac{1}{9}+\frac{4}{15} =\frac{12}{90}+\frac{10}{90}+\frac{24}{90} =\frac{46}{90} =\frac{23}{45}. $

By Bayes’ theorem, the probability that B was appointed given that the bonus is introduced is

$ P(M_B\mid B) =\frac{P(M_B)\,P(B\mid M_B)}{P(B)} =\frac{\displaystyle \frac{2}{9}\times\frac{1}{2}} {\displaystyle \frac{23}{45}} =\frac{\displaystyle \frac{1}{9}} {\displaystyle \frac{23}{45}} =\frac{1}{9}\;\times\;\frac{45}{23} =\frac{5}{23}. $

Answer : Option A, $\displaystyle \frac{5}{23}.$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question: If P(A)=1/3, P(B)=1/2 and P(A∩B)=1/4, then what is the value of P(A|BC)?
questions.solution:
We know these probabilities:

$ P(A) = \frac{1}{3} $

$ P(B) = \frac{1}{2} $

$ P(A \cap B) = \frac{1}{4} $

We want to find the probability $ P(B \mid A^{C}) $.

By the conditional probability formula:

$ P(B \mid A^C) = \frac{P(B) - P(A \cap B)}{1 - P(A)} $

Substitute the given values:

$ P(B \mid A^C) = \frac{\frac{1}{2} - \frac{1}{4}}{1 - \frac{1}{3}} $

Compute the numerator:

$ \frac{1}{2} - \frac{1}{4} = \frac{2}{4} - \frac{1}{4} = \frac{1}{4} $

Compute the denominator:

$ 1 - \frac{1}{3} = \frac{3}{3} - \frac{1}{3} = \frac{2}{3} $

Now divide:

$ P(B \mid A^C) = \frac{\frac{1}{4}}{\frac{2}{3}} = \frac{1}{4} \times \frac{3}{2} = \frac{3}{8} $

Hence, the correct answer is Option B.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question: If P(A)=1/3, P(B)=1/2 and P(A∩B)=1/4, then what is the value of P(AC∩BC)?
questions.solution:
Let's find $P(A^C \cap B^C)$, which is the probability that neither $A$ nor $B$ occurs.

We'll use these facts:

$P(A) = \dfrac{1}{3}$

$P(B) = \dfrac{1}{2}$

$P(A \cap B) = \dfrac{1}{4}$

Step 1: Find $P(A \cup B)$

Recall that:

$$ P(A \cup B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A \cap B) $$

Plug in the given values:

$$ P(A \cup B) = \dfrac{1}{3} + \dfrac{1}{2} - \dfrac{1}{4} $$

First, find a common denominator (12):

$\dfrac{1}{3} = \dfrac{4}{12}$

$\dfrac{1}{2} = \dfrac{6}{12}$

$\dfrac{1}{4} = \dfrac{3}{12}$

So,

$$ P(A \cup B) = \dfrac{4}{12} + \dfrac{6}{12} - \dfrac{3}{12} = \dfrac{7}{12} $$

Step 2: Find $P(A^C \cap B^C)$

The set $A^C \cap B^C$ means "not $A$ and not $B$", which is the complement of $A \cup B$.

So,

$$ P(A^C \cap B^C) = 1 - P(A \cup B) $$

Plug in $P(A \cup B)$:

$$ P(A^C \cap B^C) = 1 - \dfrac{7}{12} = \dfrac{12}{12} - \dfrac{7}{12} = \dfrac{5}{12} $$

Final Answer:

$$ \boxed{\dfrac{5}{12}} $$

So, the correct option is Option B: $5/12$.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question: If two fair dice are tossed, then what is the probability that the sum of the numbers on the faces of the dice is strictly greater than 7?
questions.solution:
Total possible outcomes when two fair dice are tossed:

$$6\times6=36$$

Outcomes with sum strictly greater than 7 (sums 8, 9, 10, 11, 12):

Sum = 8: 5 outcomes

Sum = 9: 4 outcomes

Sum = 10: 3 outcomes

Sum = 11: 2 outcomes

Sum = 12: 1 outcome

Total favorable outcomes:

$$5+4+3+2+1=15$$

Probability = (favorable outcomes) / (total outcomes):

$$\frac{15}{36}=\frac{5}{12}$$

Answer: Option B (5/12).

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question: The probability of a man hitting a target is 1/5. If the man fires 7 times, then what is the probability that he hits the target at least twice?
questions.solution:
Let $p=\tfrac15$ (hit) and $q=\tfrac45$ (miss). For 7 shots, the number of hits $X$ is binomial:

$ P(X=k)=\binom{7}{k}p^kq^{7-k}. $

We want

$ P(X\ge2)=1 - \bigl[P(X=0)+P(X=1)\bigr]. $

Compute

$ P(X=0)=q^7=\Bigl(\tfrac45\Bigr)^7, \quad P(X=1)=\binom{7}{1}p\,q^6=7\cdot\tfrac15\Bigl(\tfrac45\Bigr)^6 =\frac{7}{5}\Bigl(\tfrac45\Bigr)^6. $

Sum of these:

$ P(X=0)+P(X=1) =\Bigl(\tfrac45\Bigr)^7 + \frac{7}{5}\Bigl(\tfrac45\Bigr)^6 =\Bigl(\tfrac45\Bigr)^6\Bigl(\tfrac45 + \tfrac{7}{5}\Bigr) =\Bigl(\tfrac45\Bigr)^6\cdot\frac{11}{5}. $

Therefore

$ P(X\ge2) =1 - \frac{11}{5}\Bigl(\tfrac45\Bigr)^6. $

This matches Option C.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question: Let X be a random variable following binomial distribution whose mean and variance are 200 and 160 respectively. What is the value of the number of trials (n)?
questions.solution:
For a binomial distribution with parameters $n$ (number of trials) and $p$ (probability of success):

The mean is $np$.

The variance is $np(1-p)$.

We know:

Mean $= np = 200$

Variance $= np(1-p) = 160$

Let's solve for $n$ step by step.

Express $p$ using the mean:

$$ np = 200 \implies p = \frac{200}{n} $$

Substitute $p$ into the variance formula:

$$ np(1-p) = 160 $$

$$ n \cdot \frac{200}{n} \left(1 - \frac{200}{n}\right) = 160 $$

$$ 200 \left(1 - \frac{200}{n}\right) = 160 $$

Solve for $n$:

$$ 200 - \frac{200 \times 200}{n} = 160 $$

$$ 200 - 160 = \frac{40000}{n} $$

$$ 40 = \frac{40000}{n} $$

$$ n = \frac{40000}{40} $$

$$ n = 1000 $$

Answer:

$\boxed{1000}$

So, the correct option is Option B (1000).

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14th September 2025
questions.question: In a sample survey of a village, the probability that a farmer is in debt is 0.60. What is the probability that three randomly selected farmers are all in debt (assume independence of events)?
questions.solution:
Given:

  • Probability that a farmer is in debt $= 0.60$

We need the probability that $3$ randomly selected farmers are all in debt.

Since the events are independent, we multiply the probabilities:

$$ P(\text{all 3 in debt}) = 0.60 \times 0.60 \times 0.60 $$

$$ = (0.60)^3 $$

$$ = 0.216 $$

So, the correct answer is:

$$ \boxed{0.216} $$

Therefore, the correct option is Option 3.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14th September 2025
questions.question: The probability that a family owns a laptop is 0.68; that it also owns a desktop is 0.56. If the probability that it owns both is 0.48, then what is the probability that a randomly selected family owns a laptop or a desktop?
questions.solution:
Let

  • $P(L) = 0.68$ be the probability that a family owns a laptop

  • $P(D) = 0.56$ be the probability that a family owns a desktop

  • $P(L \cap D) = 0.48$ be the probability that a family owns both

We have to find the probability that a family owns a laptop or a desktop, that is $P(L \cup D)$.

Using the formula:

$$ P(L \cup D) = P(L) + P(D) - P(L \cap D) $$

Substitute the values:

$$ P(L \cup D) = 0.68 + 0.56 - 0.48 $$

$$ P(L \cup D) = 1.24 - 0.48 = 0.76 $$

So, the required probability is:

$$ \boxed{0.76} $$

Hence, the correct option is Option 2.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14th September 2025
questions.question: An urn contains 10 white and 5 red balls. If two balls are drawn at random, then what is the probability that both the balls are red?
questions.solution:
Total balls $= 10+5=15$

Red balls $=5$

We want the probability that both balls drawn are red.

Using combinations:

  • Total number of ways to draw $2$ balls from $15$ balls is

$$ {}^{15}C_2 $$

  • Number of ways to draw $2$ red balls from $5$ red balls is

$$ {}^{5}C_2 $$

So, required probability is

$$ \frac{{}^{5}C_2}{{}^{15}C_2} = \frac{\frac{5 \cdot 4}{2}}{\frac{15 \cdot 14}{2}} = \frac{10}{105} = \frac{2}{21} $$

Hence, the correct answer is:

$$ \boxed{\frac{2}{21}} $$

So, Option 1 is correct.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14th September 2025
questions.question: An urn contains 5 white, 6 red and 4 blue balls. Three balls are drawn at random. What is the probability that a white ball, a red ball and a blue ball are drawn?
questions.solution:
Total number of balls in the urn:

$$5+6+4=15$$

Three balls are drawn at random, so the total number of ways of drawing 3 balls from 15 is

$$^{15}C_3=\frac{15 \cdot 14 \cdot 13}{3 \cdot 2 \cdot 1}=455$$

Now we want exactly one white, one red and one blue ball.

Number of ways to choose:

  • 1 white ball from 5 white balls = $^{5}C_1=5$

  • 1 red ball from 6 red balls = $^{6}C_1=6$

  • 1 blue ball from 4 blue balls = $^{4}C_1=4$

So favourable number of ways is

$$^{5}C_1 \cdot {}^{6}C_1 \cdot {}^{4}C_1 = 5 \cdot 6 \cdot 4 = 120$$

Therefore, the required probability is

$$\frac{120}{455}$$

Now simplify:

$$\frac{120}{455}=\frac{24}{91}$$

Hence, the correct answer is

$$\boxed{\frac{24}{91}}$$

So, the correct option is Option 3.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14th September 2025
questions.question:
Under which of the following conditions may binomial distribution be used?

I. The number of trials is infinite and not fixed.

II. The trials are independent.

III. Each trial has two possible outcomes.

Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.solution:
For a binomial distribution, the required conditions are:

  1. The number of trials must be fixed.

  2. The trials must be independent.

  3. Each trial must have exactly two possible outcomes.

  4. The probability of success must remain the same in every trial.

Now check the statements:

  • I. The number of trials is infinite and not fixed.

    This is false, because in binomial distribution the number of trials must be fixed.

  • II. The trials are independent.

    This is true.

  • III. Each trial has two possible outcomes.

    This is true.

So, the correct statements are II and III.

Hence, the correct answer is:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 4: II and III}}$$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14th September 2025
questions.question: A person X speaks the truth 4 out of 5 times and person Y speaks the truth 5 out of 6 times. What is the probability that they will contradict each other in stating the fact?
questions.solution:
To contradict each other, one person must speak the truth and the other must speak a lie.

Given:

  • Probability that $X$ speaks truth $= \dfrac{4}{5}$

  • Therefore, probability that $X$ lies $= 1 - \dfrac{4}{5} = \dfrac{1}{5}$

  • Probability that $Y$ speaks truth $= \dfrac{5}{6}$

  • Therefore, probability that $Y$ lies $= 1 - \dfrac{5}{6} = \dfrac{1}{6}$

Now, they will contradict each other in two cases:

  1. $X$ speaks truth and $Y$ lies

  2. $X$ lies and $Y$ speaks truth

So, required probability

$$ = P(X \text{ truth and } Y \text{ lie}) + P(X \text{ lie and } Y \text{ truth}) $$

$$ = \left(\dfrac{4}{5} \times \dfrac{1}{6}\right) + \left(\dfrac{1}{5} \times \dfrac{5}{6}\right) $$

$$ = \dfrac{4}{30} + \dfrac{5}{30} = \dfrac{9}{30} = \dfrac{3}{10} $$

Therefore, the correct answer is:

$$ \boxed{\dfrac{3}{10}} $$

So, Option 1 is correct.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14th September 2025
questions.question: The probability that a student passes Physics test is 2/3 and the probability that he passes both Physics test and English test is 11/15. The probability that he passes at least one test is 4/5 What is the probability that he passes English test?
questions.solution:
Let

  • $P(P)$ = probability of passing Physics $= \dfrac{2}{3}$

  • $P(P \cap E)$ = probability of passing both Physics and English $= \dfrac{11}{15}$

  • $P(P \cup E)$ = probability of passing at least one test $= \dfrac{4}{5}$

We use the formula:

$$ P(P \cup E) = P(P) + P(E) - P(P \cap E) $$

Substitute the given values:

$$ \frac{4}{5} = \frac{2}{3} + P(E) - \frac{11}{15} $$

Now simplify:

$$ \frac{2}{3} = \frac{10}{15} $$

So,

$$ \frac{4}{5} = \frac{10}{15} + P(E) - \frac{11}{15} $$

$$ \frac{4}{5} = P(E) - \frac{1}{15} $$

Now add $\frac{1}{15}$ to both sides:

$$ P(E) = \frac{4}{5} + \frac{1}{15} $$

$$ \frac{4}{5} = \frac{12}{15} $$

Therefore,

$$ P(E) = \frac{12}{15} + \frac{1}{15} = \frac{13}{15} $$

So, the probability that he passes English test is

$$ \boxed{\frac{13}{15}} $$

Correct option: Option 2

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14th September 2025
questions.question:
An event X can happen with probability pand event Y can happen with probability q. Further, X and Y are independent events. Which of the following statements is/are correct?

1. The probability that exactly one of the events happens is p + q - pq

II. The probability that at least one of the events happens is p + q - 2pq

Select the answer using the code given below.
questions.solution:
For independent events $X$ and $Y$,

$$ P(X) = p,\qquad P(Y) = q $$

and since they are independent,

$$ P(X \cap Y) = pq $$

Now let us check both statements.

Statement I: Probability that exactly one event happens

Exactly one of the events happens means:

  • $X$ happens and $Y$ does not happen, or

  • $Y$ happens and $X$ does not happen

So,

$$ P(\text{exactly one}) = P(X \cap Y') + P(Y \cap X') $$

Using independence,

$$ P(X \cap Y') = p(1-q) $$

and

$$ P(Y \cap X') = q(1-p) $$

Therefore,

$$ P(\text{exactly one}) = p(1-q) + q(1-p) $$

$$ = p - pq + q - pq $$

$$ = p + q - 2pq $$

So Statement I says $p+q-pq$, which is incorrect.

Statement II: Probability that at least one event happens

At least one of the events happens means

$$ P(X \cup Y) $$

Using the addition rule,

$$ P(X \cup Y) = P(X) + P(Y) - P(X \cap Y) $$

$$ = p + q - pq $$

So Statement II says $p+q-2pq$, which is incorrect.

Conclusion

Both statements are incorrect.

$$ \boxed{\text{Option 4: Neither I nor II}} $$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14th September 2025
questions.question: Three faces of a die are black, two faces are white and one face is red. The die is tossed three times. What is the probability that the colours black, white and red appear in the first, second and third tosses respectively?
questions.solution:
For one toss of the die:

  • Probability of getting black $= \dfrac{3}{6} = \dfrac{1}{2}$

  • Probability of getting white $= \dfrac{2}{6} = \dfrac{1}{3}$

  • Probability of getting red $= \dfrac{1}{6}$

We need the colours to appear in this exact order:

  • first toss: black

  • second toss: white

  • third toss: red

Since the tosses are independent, we multiply the probabilities:

$$ P(B \text{ then } W \text{ then } R) = \frac{1}{2} \times \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{6} $$

$$ = \frac{1}{36} $$

So, the required probability is

$$ \boxed{\frac{1}{36}} $$

Hence, Option 1 is correct.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14th September 2025
questions.question: A fair coin is tossed 4 times. What is the probability that two heads do not occur consecutively?
questions.solution:
For 4 tosses of a fair coin, total number of possible outcomes is

$$ 2^4 = 16 $$

We need the probability that two heads do not occur consecutively, that is, the sequence should not contain $HH$.

Let us list all outcomes with no consecutive heads:

  • $TTTT$

  • $TTTH$

  • $TTHT$

  • $THTT$

  • $HTTT$

  • $THTH$

  • $HTHT$

  • $HTTH$

So, number of favourable outcomes $= 8$.

Therefore, the required probability is

$$ \frac{8}{16} = \frac{1}{2} $$

So, the correct answer is

$$ \boxed{\frac{1}{2}} $$

Hence, Option 4 is correct.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14th September 2025
questions.question: In a throw of three dice, what is the probability of getting one prime number, one composite number and one number which is neither prime nor composite?
questions.solution:
Based on the numbers available on a standard six-sided die, we categorize them as follows:

  • Prime numbers: {2, 3, 5} (3 possible outcomes)
  • Composite numbers: {4, 6} (2 possible outcomes)
  • Neither prime nor composite: {1} (1 possible outcome)

To find the probability of selecting exactly one number from each category in three trials, we apply the following formula:

$$ P = \frac{3}{6} \cdot \frac{2}{6} \cdot \frac{1}{6} \cdot 3! $$

Performing the multiplication:

$$ = \frac{6}{216} \cdot 6 $$

$$ = \frac{36}{216} $$

$$ = \frac{1}{6} $$

The correct result is Option 4.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14th September 2025
questions.question: An integer is chosen at random from the first 50 integers. What is the probability that the integer is neither divisible by 5 nor 9?
questions.solution:
From the first $50$ integers, we take the numbers $1$ to $50$.

We want the probability that the chosen integer is neither divisible by $5$ nor by $9$.

So first, let us count how many numbers are divisible by $5$ or $9$.

Numbers divisible by $5$

$$ \left\lfloor \frac{50}{5} \right\rfloor = 10 $$

Numbers divisible by $9$

$$ \left\lfloor \frac{50}{9} \right\rfloor = 5 $$

Numbers divisible by both $5$ and $9$

A number divisible by both must be divisible by $\operatorname{LCM}(5,9)=45$.

$$ \left\lfloor \frac{50}{45} \right\rfloor = 1 $$

Using inclusion-exclusion

Numbers divisible by $5$ or $9$:

$$ 10+5-1=14 $$

So numbers divisible by neither $5$ nor $9$:

$$ 50-14=36 $$

Therefore, the required probability is

$$ \frac{36}{50}=\frac{18}{25} $$

So the correct answer is:

$$ \boxed{\frac{18}{25}} $$

Hence, Option 2.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14th September 2025
questions.question: Out of 50 consecutive natural numbers, two integers are chosen at random. What is the probability that their sum is odd?
questions.solution:
For the sum of two integers to be odd, one number must be even and the other must be odd.

Now, among $50$ consecutive natural numbers:

  • There are $25$ even numbers

  • There are $25$ odd numbers

Total ways to choose any $2$ numbers from $50$:

$$ \binom{50}{2} = \frac{50 \cdot 49}{2} = 1225 $$

Favourable ways:

Choose $1$ even out of $25$ and $1$ odd out of $25$:

$$ 25 \times 25 = 625 $$

So, the required probability is

$$ \frac{625}{1225} $$

Simplify:

$$ \frac{625}{1225} = \frac{25}{49} $$

Hence, the correct answer is

$$ \boxed{\frac{25}{49}} $$

So, Option 4 is correct.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14th September 2025
questions.question: Let X be a random variable following binomial distribution with parameters n = 5 and p = k Further, P(X = 1) = 0.4096 and P(X = 2) = 0.2048 . What is the value of k?
questions.solution:
For a binomial random variable $X \sim B(5,k)$, we know:

$$ P(X=r)=\binom{5}{r}k^r(1-k)^{5-r} $$

Given:

$$ P(X=1)=0.4096,\qquad P(X=2)=0.2048 $$

Now write both probabilities using the binomial formula.

For $X=1$:

$$ P(X=1)=\binom{5}{1}k(1-k)^4=5k(1-k)^4 $$

For $X=2$:

$$ P(X=2)=\binom{5}{2}k^2(1-k)^3=10k^2(1-k)^3 $$

Now take the ratio:

$$ \frac{P(X=2)}{P(X=1)}=\frac{10k^2(1-k)^3}{5k(1-k)^4} $$

Simplify:

$$ \frac{0.2048}{0.4096}=\frac{10k^2(1-k)^3}{5k(1-k)^4} $$

$$ \frac{1}{2}=\frac{2k}{1-k} $$

Now solve:

$$ \frac{2k}{1-k}=\frac{1}{2} $$

Cross-multiplying:

$$ 4k=1-k $$

$$ 5k=1 $$

$$ k=\frac{1}{5}=0.2 $$

So, the correct answer is:

$$ \boxed{0.2} $$

Hence, Option 1 is correct.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14th September 2025
questions.question: If P(A) = 0.3 , P(B) = 0.4 and P( A |B)=0.5, then what is the value P( B |A) ?
questions.solution:
Use the conditional probability formula:

$$ P(A|B) = \frac{P(A \cap B)}{P(B)} $$

Given:

$$ P(A) = 0.3,\quad P(B) = 0.4,\quad P(A|B) = 0.5 $$

First find $P(A \cap B)$:

$$ P(A \cap B) = P(A|B)\cdot P(B) = 0.5 \times 0.4 = 0.2 $$

Now use:

$$ P(B|A) = \frac{P(A \cap B)}{P(A)} $$

So,

$$ P(B|A) = \frac{0.2}{0.3} = \frac{2}{3} = 0.667 $$

Hence, the correct answer is:

$$ \boxed{0.667} $$

Option 4

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14th September 2025
questions.question: If P(A) = 1/3 , P(B) = 1/2 and $ P(A\cap B)=1/4$ , then what is the value of $P(\overline{A}\cup B)$ ?
questions.solution:
We need to find $P(\overline{A}\cup B)$.

Use the identity:

$$ P(\overline{A}\cup B)=1-P(A\cap \overline{B}) $$

So first find $P(A\cap \overline{B})$.

Since

$$ A=(A\cap B)\cup (A\cap \overline{B}) $$

and these two parts are disjoint,

$$ P(A)=P(A\cap B)+P(A\cap \overline{B}) $$

Therefore,

$$ P(A\cap \overline{B})=P(A)-P(A\cap B) $$

Substitute the given values:

$$ P(A\cap \overline{B})=\frac{1}{3}-\frac{1}{4} =\frac{4-3}{12} =\frac{1}{12} $$

Now,

$$ P(\overline{A}\cup B)=1-\frac{1}{12} =\frac{11}{12} $$

So the correct answer is:

$$ \boxed{\frac{11}{12}} $$

Option 4

questions.title: BITSAT 2025
questions.question: The angles of a triangle are in the ratio $1: 2: 7$. The ratio of the greatest side to the least side is $(k+1):(k-1)$. The value of $k$ is
questions.solution:
Let the angles of a triangle be $x, 2 x$ and $7 x$.

Then, $x+2 x+7 x=180^{\circ} \Rightarrow x=18^{\circ}$

So, angles are $A=18^{\circ}, B=36^{\circ}$ and $C=126^{\circ}$

The least side is $a$ and the greatest side is $c$.

$$ \begin{array}{rlrl}&\frac{a}{\sin A} =\frac{c}{\sin C} \\ \Rightarrow \quad & \frac{c}{a} =\frac{\sin C}{\sin A}=\frac{\sin 126^{\circ}}{\sin 18^{\circ}} \\ & =\frac{\sin \left(180^{\circ}-54^{\circ}\right)}{\sin 18^{\circ}}=\frac{\sin 54^{\circ}}{\sin 18^{\circ}} \\ & =\frac{\cos 36^{\circ}}{\sin 18^{\circ}}=\frac{\frac{(\sqrt{5}+1)}{4}}{\frac{(\sqrt{5}-1)}{4}}=\frac{\sqrt{5}+1}{\sqrt{5}-1}=\frac{k+1}{k-1} \\ \therefore \quad k & =\sqrt{5} \end{array} $$

questions.title: BITSAT 2025
questions.question: Let $A, B$ and $C$ be the angles of a triangle and $\tan \frac{A}{2}=\frac{2}{5}, \tan \frac{B}{2}=\frac{3}{7}$. Then, $\tan \frac{C}{2}$ is equal to
questions.solution:
In a triangle, $A+B+C=180^{\circ}$

Let $\tan \frac{A}{2}=x, \tan \frac{B}{2}=y$ and $\tan \frac{C}{2}=z$

Using the identity:

$$ \begin{aligned} & \tan \frac{A}{2} \cdot \tan \frac{B}{2}+\tan \frac{B}{2} \cdot \tan \frac{C}{2}+\tan \frac{C}{2} \cdot \tan \frac{A}{2}=1 \\ \Rightarrow & \frac{2}{5} \cdot \frac{3}{7}+\frac{3}{7} \cdot z+z \cdot \frac{2}{5}=1 \\ \Rightarrow & \frac{6}{35}+\frac{15}{35} z+\frac{14}{35} z=1 \Rightarrow \frac{29}{35} z=\frac{29}{35} \\ \Rightarrow & z=1 \\ \Rightarrow & \text { Thus, } \tan \frac{C}{2}=1 \end{aligned} $$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question:
Consider the following statements:

I. The triangle is obtuse-angled triangle.

II. The sum of acute angles of the triangle is also acute.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
1. Verify triangle type using the converse of the Pythagorean theorem

    Let the sides be $a=3\,$cm, $b=5\,$cm, $c=7\,$cm (where $c$ is the largest).

    Compute

    $$c^2 = 7^2 = 49,\quad a^2 + b^2 = 3^2 + 5^2 = 9 + 25 = 34.$$

    Since

    $$c^2 > a^2 + b^2\;(49 > 34),$$

    the triangle is obtuse-angled.

    ⇒ Statement I is correct.

  2. Sum of the two acute angles

    In any triangle, the sum of all angles is $180^\circ$. Let the obtuse angle be $\theta_O>90^\circ$ and the other two acute angles be $\alpha$ and $\beta$. Then

    $$\alpha + \beta = 180^\circ - \theta_O < 180^\circ - 90^\circ = 90^\circ.$$

    Thus $\alpha + \beta$ is less than $90^\circ$, so it is an acute angle.

    ⇒ Statement II is also correct.

Answer: Both I and II (Option C).

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question: What is ∠B equal to?
options: 60∘
105∘
120∘
150∘
questions.solution:
1. In ΔABC, by the Law of Cosines on ∠B:

    $$AC^2 \;=\; AB^2 + BC^2 \;-\;2\cdot AB\cdot BC\cos\angle B$$

  2. Substitute $AB=3\,$cm, $BC=5\,$cm, $AC=7\,$cm:

    $$7^2 \;=\;3^2 + 5^2 \;-\;2\cdot3\cdot5\cos B$$

    $$49 \;=\;9 + 25 \;-\;30\cos B$$

    $$49 =34 -30\cos B$$

  3. Solve for $\cos B$:

    $$30\cos B =34-49 = -15$$

    $$\cos B = -\tfrac{15}{30} = -\tfrac12$$

  4. Therefore

    $$\angle B = \cos^{-1}\bigl(-\tfrac12\bigr) =120^\circ$$

Answer: ∠B = 120° (Option C).

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question: What is the area of the triangle?
questions.solution:
Step 1: Information Given

The triangle has side $a = 5$ cm, side $c = 3$ cm, and the angle between them, $B = 120^\circ$.

Step 2: Area Formula

To find the area of a triangle when you know two sides and the angle between them, use this formula:

$ \text{Area} = \frac{1}{2} \times a \times c \times \sin B $

Step 3: Substitute the values

Put in the values for $a$, $c$, and $\sin(120^\circ)$:

$ \text{Area} = \frac{1}{2} \times 5 \times 3 \times \sin(120^\circ) $

Step 4: Find $\sin(120^\circ)$

We know that $\sin(120^\circ) = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$.

Step 5: Calculate the Area

Now put this value into the formula:

$ \text{Area} = \frac{1}{2} \times 5 \times 3 \times \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} $

Multiply $5 \times 3 = 15$:

$ \text{Area} = \frac{1}{2} \times 15 \times \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} $

This can be written as:

$ \text{Area} = \frac{15 \sqrt{3}}{4} $

So, the area of the triangle is $\frac{15\sqrt{3}}{4}$ square cm.

Therefore, the correct answer is Option A.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question: One of the angles of the triangle is
options: 15∘
30∘
45∘
75∘
questions.solution:
1. Let’s use common names for the sides and angles in the triangle ΔABC:

    – Side BC is called $a$, side CA is called $b$, and side AB is called $c$.

    – Angle A is opposite to side BC, angle B is opposite to side CA, and angle C is opposite to side AB.

  2. It's given that the sides are in the ratio $a : b = 2 : 1$.

    The angles are in the ratio $\angle A : \angle B = 3 : 1$.

  3. Let’s use a variable $x$ for the smaller angle:

    Let $\angle A = 3x$ and $\angle B = x$.

    The third angle, $\angle C$, is what’s left from 180°, so: $\angle C = 180^\circ - (3x + x) = 180^\circ - 4x$.

  4. We use the Law of Sines for triangles:

    $$\frac{a}{\sin A} = \frac{b}{\sin B} \implies \frac{a}{b} = \frac{\sin A}{\sin B}.$$

    We know $\frac{a}{b} = 2$, so:

    $$\frac{\sin(3x)}{\sin x} = 2.$$

  5. Use the formula for $\sin 3x$: $\sin 3x = 3\sin x - 4\sin^3 x$

    Substitute it in and solve:

    $$\frac{3\sin x - 4\sin^3 x}{\sin x} = 2$$

    This means: $3 - 4\sin^2 x = 2$

    So: $3 - 4\sin^2 x = 2 \implies 4\sin^2 x = 1 \implies \sin^2 x = \frac{1}{4}$

    This gives $\sin x = \frac{1}{2}$.

    The positive answer in a triangle is $x = 30^\circ$.

  6. Now, let’s find all the angles:

    $\angle B = x = 30^\circ$

    $\angle A = 3x = 3 \times 30^\circ = 90^\circ$

    $\angle C = 180^\circ - (90^\circ + 30^\circ) = 60^\circ$

So, one of the angles in the triangle is $30^\circ$.

Answer: Option B.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question:
Consider the following statements:

I. The triangle is right-angled.

II. One of the sides of the triangle is 3 times the other.

III. The angles A, C and B of the triangle are in AP.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
1. Let BC : CA = a : b = 2 : 1 and ∠A : ∠B = 3 : 1.

  2. Write ∠A = 3x, ∠B = x. By the sine rule,

    $$\frac{a}{\sin A} \;=\;\frac{b}{\sin B} \;\implies\;\frac{a}{b} \;=\;\frac{\sin3x}{\sin x}\;=\;2.$$

  3. Use $\sin3x=3\sin x-4\sin^3x$:

    $$\frac{\sin3x}{\sin x}=3-4\sin^2x=2 \;\implies\;4\sin^2x=1\;\implies\;\sin x=\tfrac12 \;\implies\;x=30^\circ.$$

  4. Hence $\angle B=30^\circ,\;\angle A=90^\circ,\;\angle C=180^\circ-120^\circ=60^\circ.$

Verification of statements:

I. ∠A=90° ⇒ triangle is right-angled. ✓

II. The only given side ratio is 2 : 1; no side is 3 times another. ✗

III. Angles A, C, B = 90°, 60°, 30° form an AP (common difference –30°). ✓

Correct statements: I and III only.

Answer: Option C.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question: The vertices of a triangle are A(1, 1),B(0, 0) and C(2, 0). The angular bisectors of the triangle meet at P. What are the coordinates of P?
questions.solution:
1. Denote the side-lengths opposite A, B, C by

    $a=BC,\;b=CA,\;c=AB$.

    $BC=\sqrt{(2-0)^2+(0-0)^2}=2,\quad CA=\sqrt{(2-1)^2+(0-1)^2}=\sqrt2,\quad AB=\sqrt{(1-0)^2+(1-0)^2}=\sqrt2.$

  2. Incentre $P=(x,y)$ has coordinates

    $$x=\frac{a x_A+b x_B+c x_C}{a+b+c},\quad y=\frac{a y_A+b y_B+c y_C}{a+b+c}.$$

  3. Here $a=2,b=\sqrt2,c=\sqrt2,\;x_A=1,y_A=1,\;x_B=0,y_B=0,\;x_C=2,y_C=0$, so

    $$x=\frac{2\cdot1+\sqrt2\cdot0+\sqrt2\cdot2}{2+2\sqrt2} =\frac{2+2\sqrt2}{2+2\sqrt2}=1,$$

    $$y=\frac{2\cdot1+\sqrt2\cdot0+\sqrt2\cdot0}{2+2\sqrt2} =\frac{2}{2(1+\sqrt2)} =\frac1{1+\sqrt2} =\sqrt2-1.$$

Answer: $\displaystyle P=(1,\;\sqrt2-1)$. Option A.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2025 Paper 1 Online
questions.question:
Let $\mathbb{R}$ denote the set of all real numbers. Let $a_i, b_i \in \mathbb{R}$ for $i \in \{1, 2, 3\}$.

Define the functions $f: \mathbb{R} \to \mathbb{R}$, $g: \mathbb{R} \to \mathbb{R}$, and $h: \mathbb{R} \to \mathbb{R}$ by

$f(x) = a_1 + 10x + a_2 x^2 + a_3 x^3 + x^4$

$g(x) = b_1 + 3x + b_2 x^2 + b_3 x^3 + x^4$

$h(x) = f(x + 1) - g(x + 2)$

If $f(x) \neq g(x)$ for every $x \in \mathbb{R}$, then the coefficient of $x^3$ in $h(x)$ is
questions.solution:
To solve for the coefficient of $ x^3 $ in the function $ h(x) = f(x + 1) - g(x + 2) $, we start by expanding both $ f(x + 1) $ and $ g(x + 2) $.

First, expand $ f(x + 1) $:

$ f(x+1) = a_1 + 10(x+1) + a_2 (x+1)^2 + a_3 (x+1)^3 + (x+1)^4 $

From this expansion, the coefficient of $ x^3 $ in $ f(x+1) $ is $ a_3 + 4 $.

Next, expand $ g(x + 2) $:

$ g(x+2) = b_1 + 3(x+2) + b_2 (x+2)^2 + b_3 (x+2)^3 + (x+2)^4 $

Here, the coefficient of $ x^3 $ in $ g(x+2) $ is $ b_3 + 8 $.

Now, calculate the coefficient of $ x^3 $ in $ h(x) = f(x+1) - g(x+2) $:

$ \text{Coefficient of } x^3 \text{ in } h(x) = (a_3 + 4) - (b_3 + 8) = a_3 - b_3 - 4 $

Given that $ f(x) \neq g(x) $ for every $ x \in \mathbb{R} $, it follows that $ f(x) - g(x) \neq 0 $ for any $ x $. This means the equation:

$ (a_1 - b_1) + 7x + (a_2 - b_2)x^2 + (a_3 - b_3)x^3 = 0 $

cannot have any real roots. For this polynomial to have no real roots, the degree of the polynomial must be less than or equal to zero, implying that the coefficients of the highest degree terms must equate to zero. Thus, $ a_3 - b_3 = 0 $.

Substituting $ a_3 - b_3 = 0 $ into our earlier expression for the coefficient of $ x^3 $ in $ h(x) $ gives:

$ a_3 - b_3 - 4 = -4 $

Thus, the coefficient of $ x^3 $ in $ h(x) $ is $-4$.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question: If one root of the equation x2−kx+k=0 exceeds the other by $2\sqrt{3}$ then which one of the following is a value of k?
questions.solution:
1. Let the roots be $\alpha$ and $\beta$.

    By Viète’s relations for the equation $x^2 - kx + k = 0$:

    • $\alpha + \beta = k$

    • $\alpha\beta = k$

  2. Given that one root exceeds the other by $2\sqrt{3}$, so

    $$(\alpha - \beta)^2 = (2\sqrt{3})^2 = 12\,. $$

  3. But

    $$(\alpha - \beta)^2 = (\alpha + \beta)^2 - 4\alpha\beta = k^2 - 4k\,. $$

  4. Equate and simplify:

    $$k^2 - 4k = 12 \quad\Longrightarrow\quad k^2 - 4k - 12 = 0 \quad\Longrightarrow\quad k = \frac{4 \pm \sqrt{16 + 48}}{2} = \frac{4 \pm 8}{2} = 6 \text{ or } -2.$$

  5. From the given options (3, 6, 9, 12), the only admissible value is

    $\boxed{6}.$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question: If $x+\frac{5}{y}=4$, and then $y+\frac{5}{x}= -4$  what is (x + y) equal to?
questions.solution:
1. Write the two equations and eliminate denominators by multiplying:

    (i) $x + \frac{5}{y} = 4\;\;\Longrightarrow\;\;xy + 5 = 4y$

    (ii) $y + \frac{5}{x} = -4\;\;\Longrightarrow\;\;xy + 5 = -4x$

  2. From (i) and (ii), the left-hand sides are equal:

    $xy + 5 = 4y$

    $xy + 5 = -4x$

    $\displaystyle\Longrightarrow 4y = -4x$

  3. Hence

    $y = -x$

  4. Therefore

    $$x + y = x + (-x) = 0.$$

Answer: 0.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question: If x2− x +1 = 0, then what is $(x-\frac{1}{x})^2+(x-\frac{1}{x})^4+(x-\frac{1}{x})^8$
questions.solution:
1. Roots of the given equation

    Solve $$x^2 - x + 1 = 0$$

    Discriminant $D = (-1)^2 - 4\cdot1\cdot1 = -3$.

    So $$x = \frac{1\pm i\sqrt{3}}{2} = e^{\pm i\pi/3}\,. $$

  2. Compute $x - \dfrac1x$

    For $x=e^{i\pi/3}$,

    $$x - \frac1x = e^{i\pi/3} - e^{-i\pi/3} = 2i\sin\frac{\pi}{3} = i\sqrt{3}.$$

    (For the other root $e^{-i\pi/3}$ one gets $-\,i\sqrt{3}$, whose even powers coincide.)

  3. Compute the required powers

    Let $A = x - \dfrac1x = i\sqrt{3}$. Then

    $$A^2 = (i\sqrt{3})^2 = -3,$$

    $$A^4 = (-3)^2 = 9,$$

    $$A^8 = 9^2 = 81.$$

  4. Sum them up

    $$(x-\tfrac1x)^2 + (x-\tfrac1x)^4 + (x-\tfrac1x)^8 = A^2 + A^4 + A^8 = -3 + 9 + 81 = 87.$$

Answer: 87.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14th September 2025
questions.question: What is the relation between α and β
questions.solution:
Take $ L = \log_{0.5}(\alpha^2) $. The quadratic equation then becomes

$ x^2 + Lx + L^4 = 0 $

If its roots are $ \alpha $ and $ \beta $, then

⇒ $ \alpha + \beta = -L $

⇒ $ \alpha \beta = L^4 \Rightarrow \beta = \frac{L^4}{\alpha} $

It is also given that $ \beta^2 = \alpha \cdot \log_{\alpha^2}(0.5) = \frac{\alpha}{L} $

Now evaluate $ \beta^2 $ using the second relation:

⇒ $ \left( \frac{L^4}{\alpha} \right)^2 = \frac{L^8}{\alpha^2} $

Equating the two expressions for $ \beta^2 $, we get

⇒ $ \frac{L^8}{\alpha^2} = \frac{\alpha}{L} \Rightarrow L^9 = \alpha^3 \Rightarrow \alpha = L^3 $

Therefore,

⇒ $ \beta = \frac{L^4}{\alpha} = \frac{L^4}{L^3} = L \Rightarrow \alpha = L^3 = (L)^3 = (\beta)^3 \Rightarrow \alpha = \beta^3 $

Next, check the proposed answer $ \alpha = 2\beta $.

If $ \alpha = 2\beta $, then

⇒ $ \beta = \frac{L^4}{\alpha} = \frac{L^4}{2\beta} \Rightarrow \beta^2 = \frac{L^4}{2} $

Also, $ \beta^2 = \frac{\alpha}{L} = \frac{2\beta}{L} $

Equating these gives

$ \frac{L^4}{2} = \frac{2\beta}{L} \Rightarrow L^5 = 4\beta \Rightarrow \beta = \frac{L^5}{4} $

Then $ \alpha = 2\beta = \frac{L^5}{2} $, which can be compared with $ \alpha = L^3 $.

⇒ $ L^3 = \frac{L^5}{2} \Rightarrow L^2 = 2 \Rightarrow L = \sqrt{2} $

So $ \alpha = L^3 = 2\sqrt{2} $ and $ \beta = \sqrt{2} \Rightarrow \alpha = 2\beta $

Hence, the correct answer is Option 1.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14th September 2025
questions.question: If the roots of the equation are equal, then which one of the following is correct?
questions.solution:
Let us solve this problem step-by-step using the standard concepts of quadratic equations.

First, we will write the given equation and rearrange it into the standard form of a quadratic equation, which is $Ax^2 + Bx + C = 0$.

The given equation is:

$$abx^2 + bcx + ca = cax^2 + abx + bc$$

Bring all the terms to the left-hand side to group them:

$$abx^2 - cax^2 + bcx - abx + ca - bc = 0$$

Now, take the common factors out for $x^2$, $x$, and the constant terms:

$$(ab - ca)x^2 + (bc - ab)x + (ca - bc) = 0$$

Taking out the common variables from each bracket, we get:

$$a(b - c)x^2 + b(c - a)x + c(a - b) = 0$$

Comparing this with the standard quadratic equation $Ax^2 + Bx + C = 0$, we can identify our coefficients:

$A = a(b - c)$

$B = b(c - a)$

$C = c(a - b)$

According to the question, the roots of the equation are equal. We know that for a quadratic equation to have equal roots, its discriminant ($D$) must be equal to zero.

The condition is:

$$D = B^2 - 4AC = 0$$

Let us substitute our values of $A$, $B$, and $C$ into this condition:

$$[b(c - a)]^2 - 4[a(b - c)][c(a - b)] = 0$$

Now, carefully expand the terms:

$$b^2(c^2 + a^2 - 2ac) - 4ac(ab - b^2 - ac + bc) = 0$$

Multiply the terms inside the brackets:

$$b^2c^2 + a^2b^2 - 2ab^2c - (4a^2bc - 4ab^2c - 4a^2c^2 + 4abc^2) = 0$$

$$b^2c^2 + a^2b^2 - 2ab^2c - 4a^2bc + 4ab^2c + 4a^2c^2 - 4abc^2 = 0$$

Group and simplify the like terms (notice that $-2ab^2c + 4ab^2c = +2ab^2c$):

$$a^2b^2 + b^2c^2 + 4a^2c^2 + 2ab^2c - 4abc^2 - 4a^2bc = 0$$

At this stage, you may recognize that this expression resembles the expansion of the standard algebraic identity:

$$(X + Y + Z)^2 = X^2 + Y^2 + Z^2 + 2XY + 2YZ + 2ZX$$

By observing our simplified equation, let us set:

$X = ab$

$Y = bc$

$Z = -2ac$

Let us check if this assumption holds true:

$X^2 = a^2b^2$

$Y^2 = b^2c^2$

$Z^2 = 4a^2c^2$

$2XY = 2(ab)(bc) = 2ab^2c$

$2YZ = 2(bc)(-2ac) = -4abc^2$

$2ZX = 2(-2ac)(ab) = -4a^2bc$

Our terms match the expanded equation perfectly. Therefore, we can write the entire long expression as a perfect square:

$$(ab + bc - 2ac)^2 = 0$$

Taking the square root on both sides simply gives zero:

$$ab + bc - 2ac = 0$$

Rearranging the terms, we get:

$$ab + bc = 2ac$$

To find a relation that matches our given options, we will divide the entire equation by $abc$:

$$\frac{ab}{abc} + \frac{bc}{abc} = \frac{2ac}{abc}$$

Simplify the fractions by canceling out the common terms in the numerator and denominator:

$$\frac{1}{c} + \frac{1}{a} = \frac{2}{b}$$

This relation indicates that Option 4 is the correct answer.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14th September 2025
questions.question: If the roots of the equation are equal, then a, b, c are in
questions.solution:
Let us solve this step-by-step by simplifying the given equation and using the properties of quadratic equations.

The given equation is:

$$abx^2 + bcx + ca = cax^2 + abx + bc$$

Step 1: Rewrite the equation in the standard quadratic form

Bring all the terms to the left-hand side to format it as $Ax^2 + Bx + C = 0$:

$$abx^2 - cax^2 + bcx - abx + ca - bc = 0$$

Taking the common factors out for each power of $x$:

$$(ab - ca)x^2 + (bc - ab)x + (ca - bc) = 0$$

$$a(b - c)x^2 + b(c - a)x + c(a - b) = 0$$

Step 2: Observe the sum of the coefficients

Let us find the sum of the coefficients $A$, $B$, and $C$:

$$A + B + C = a(b - c) + b(c - a) + c(a - b)$$

$$A + B + C = (ab - ac) + (bc - ab) + (ca - cb) = 0$$

In any quadratic equation, if the sum of the coefficients is $0$, it implies that $x = 1$ is a root of the equation.

Step 3: Apply the condition of equal roots

The problem states that the roots of the equation are equal. Since we have found that one root is $1$, the second root must also be $1$.

Step 4: Use the product of the roots

For a quadratic equation $Ax^2 + Bx + C = 0$, the product of the roots is given by $\frac{C}{A}$.

Since both roots are $1$, their product is:

$$1 \times 1 = 1$$

Substitute $C$ and $A$ into the product formula:

$$\frac{c(a - b)}{a(b - c)} = 1$$

Step 5: Simplify to find the relationship between a, b, and c

Multiply both sides by $a(b - c)$:

$$c(a - b) = a(b - c)$$

$$ac - bc = ab - ac$$

Rearrange the terms by bringing $ac$ to the left side and $bc$ to the right side:

$$ac + ac = ab + bc$$

$$2ac = b(a + c)$$

Now, divide the entire equation by $abc$:

$$\frac{2ac}{abc} = \frac{ab}{abc} + \frac{bc}{abc}$$

$$\frac{2}{b} = \frac{1}{c} + \frac{1}{a}$$

This result, $\frac{2}{b} = \frac{1}{a} + \frac{1}{c}$, implies that $\frac{1}{a}$, $\frac{1}{b}$, and $\frac{1}{c}$ are in an Arithmetic Progression (AP).

By definition, if the reciprocals of numbers are in an AP, the numbers themselves are in a Harmonic Progression (HP).

Therefore, $a$, $b$, and $c$ are in HP.

Correct Answer:

Option 3

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14th September 2025
questions.question: What is the other root of f(x) = 0
questions.solution:
Given

$$ f(x)=ax^2+bx+c $$

and

$$ f(1)=2,\quad f(4)=2 $$

Also, $2$ is a root of $f(x)=0$, so

$$ f(2)=0 $$

We need to find the other root.

Since $f(1)=f(4)$, the quadratic takes equal values at $x=1$ and $x=4$.

For a quadratic polynomial, equal values at two points mean these points are equally distant from the axis of symmetry. So the axis of symmetry is the midpoint of $1$ and $4$:

$$ x=\frac{1+4}{2}=\frac{5}{2} $$

Now one root is $2$. In a quadratic, the axis of symmetry is also the midpoint of the two roots.

Let the other root be $r$. Then

$$ \frac{2+r}{2}=\frac{5}{2} $$

So,

$$ 2+r=5 $$

$$ r=3 $$

Hence, the other root is

$$ \boxed{3} $$

So the correct option is:

Option 3: $3$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14th September 2025
questions.question: What is (a + b + c) equal to?
questions.solution:
Given,

$$ f(x)=ax^2+bx+c $$

and

  • $f(1)=2$

  • $f(4)=2$

  • $2$ is a root of $f(x)=0$, so $f(2)=0$

We have to find $a+b+c$.

Now,

$$ a+b+c=f(1) $$

This is because

$$ f(1)=a(1)^2+b(1)+c=a+b+c $$

But it is already given that

$$ f(1)=2 $$

Therefore,

$$ a+b+c=2 $$

So the correct option is:

$$ \boxed{2} $$

Hence, the answer is Option 3.

questions.title: BITSAT 2025
questions.question: For what value of $a, 6$ lies between the roots of the equation $x^2+2(a-3) x+9=0$.
questions.solution:
Let $f(x)=x^2+2(a-3) x+9$.

If 6 lies between the roots of $f(x)=0$, then we must have the following conditions:

(i) Disc $>0$ and (ii) $f(6)<0$

[ $\because$ coefficient of $x^2$ is positive]

Now (i) Disc $>0$

$$ \begin{array}{lc} \Rightarrow & 4(a-3)^2-36>0 \\ \Rightarrow & (a-3)^2-9>0 \\ \Rightarrow & a^2-6 a>0 \\ \Rightarrow & a<0 \text { or } a>6\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,...(i) \\ \text { and Eq. (ii) } & f(6)<0 \\ \Rightarrow & 36+12(a-3)+9<0 \\ \Rightarrow & 12 a+9<0 \\ \Rightarrow & a<-\frac{3}{4} \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,...(ii)\end{array} $$

From Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get

                               $$ a<-\frac{3}{4} $$

i.e.                                $$ a \in\left(-\infty, \frac{-3}{4}\right) $$

questions.title: BITSAT 2025
questions.question: Roots of the equation $a x^2+b x+c=0(a, b, c>0)$ are
options:
Both positive

Both negative

Of opposite sign

depends on the values of $a, b$ and $c$
questions.solution:
For the quadratic equation, $a x^2+b x+c=0$, using the quadratic formula

$$ x=\frac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2-4 a c}}{2 a} $$

Sum of roots $=-\frac{b}{a}$

Since, $a, b>0$, so $-\frac{b}{a}$ is negative, meaning the sum of the roots in negative.

Products of roots $=c / a$

Since, $a, c>0$, this means the product is positive we know that if the product of roots is positive and the sum of roots is negative, both roots must be negative.

questions.title: BITSAT 2025
questions.question: The frequency of a light wave in a material is $2 \times 10^{14} \mathrm{~Hz}$ and wavelength is $5000 \mathop {\rm{A}}\limits^{\rm{o}} $. The refractive index of material will be
questions.solution:
Velocity of light waves in material is

$$ v=\nu \lambda \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,...(i)$$

Refractive index of material is

$$ \mu=\frac{c}{v} \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,...(ii)$$

where, $c$ is speed of light in vacuum or air.

or   $$ \mu=\frac{c}{v \lambda} $$

$$ \begin{aligned} &\text { }\\ &\begin{aligned} Given,\,\, \nu & =2 \times 10^{14} \mathrm{~Hz} \\ \lambda & =5000 \mathop {\rm{A}}\limits^{\rm{o}} =5000 \times 10^{-10} \mathrm{~m} \\ c & =3 \times 10^8 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1} \\ \mu & =\frac{3 \times 10^8}{2 \times 10^{14} \times 5 \times 10^{-7}}=3 \end{aligned} \end{aligned} $$

questions.question:
In given lens combination, each lens is of focal length 10 cm .

Correct ray diagram for this combination is
questions.solution:
