questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: Which one of the following is NOT the reason for the difficulty in estimating the number of languages of the world ?
options: New languages continue to be discovered
New languages are considered a dialect of a known language
People who speak the language do not claim their language
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option 4.

Key Points

The question asks which option is NOT a reason for the difficulty in estimating the number of languages according to the passage.

Let's examine each option against the text:

New languages continue to be discovered: The passage states, "A few new languages do continue to be discovered, even these days…" This is a reason for the difficulty in estimation.

There are still unexplored regions: The passage mentions, "…as unexplored regions of the world begin to be opened up." This contributes directly to the discovery of new languages, making estimation challenging. This is a reason.

New languages are considered a dialect of a known language: The passage explicitly states, "Often there are similarities with an already known language which make the investigators assume that what they have found is just a dialect of that language. Only after a considerable period of contact does it transpire that the speech is so different that it has to be considered a different language." This is a reason for difficulty in initial identification.

People who speak the language do not claim their language: The passage indicates, "Because many such peoples are bilingual or multilingual, and converse with outsiders in lingua franca, it may take a while before linguists come to realize that there is an ethnic language there at all." This implies that linguists may not realize a language exists due to multilingualism and the use of a lingua franca, but it does not suggest that speakers do not claim their language. The passage does not mention speakers actively refusing to claim their language, hence this is NOT explicitly stated as a reason for difficulty.

Thus, the correct answer is Option 4.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: Which one of the following is a way to establish the discovery of a new language ?
options: By establishing contact over a period of time with the speakers of the language
By finding the similarities with the language with other languages
By comparing with the lingua franca of the region
By conducting a survey of all languages of the region
questions.solution:
The passage explains: "Only after a considerable period of contact does it transpire that the speech is so different that it has to be considered a different language." This means that it takes a while, after interacting with the people, for the significant differences to become clear enough to classify it as a distinct language.

So, looking at the options, Option 1 aligns perfectly with this description.

Let's briefly consider the others:

Option 2 is actually what might lead investigators to think it's not a new language initially (because of similarities).

Option 3 isn't mentioned as the way to establish a new language, but rather the use of lingua franca can sometimes hide the existence of an ethnic language.

Option 4, conducting a survey, is mentioned as a way "to establish the facts" which would certainly be part of the formal process, but the text specifically points to the "period of contact" as the point where the distinction becomes clear enough to consider it a different language.

Therefore, the text highlights Option 1: By establishing contact over a period of time with the speakers of the language as a key way to establish the discovery of a new language by realizing its significant difference.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: Which one of the following statements is correct ?
options: Language surveys have been conducted by all nations
There is hardly any language to be discovered in the world
All the languages are either complete languages or dialects of other major languages
No new language needs to be discovered since all languages are known to the world
questions.solution:
The text says:

New languages are still being discovered ("A few new languages do continue to be discovered, even these days").

It's hard to distinguish new languages from dialects initially ("Often there are similarities… assume that what they have found is just a dialect… speech is so different that it has to be considered a different language").

Language surveys are needed, but many countries have incomplete or no surveys ("there are still many countries where such surveys are incomplete or have not even begun").

Languages can be hidden by people being bilingual and using lingua francas ("it may take a while before linguists come to realize that there is an ethnic language there at all").

Now let's check the options against these points:

Option 1: Language surveys have been conducted by all nations.

The text says "many countries where such surveys are incomplete or have not even begun." This directly contradicts Option 1.

Option 2: There is hardly any language to be discovered in the world.

The text says "A few new languages do continue to be discovered, even these days". "A few" continuing to be discovered suggests there are still some, perhaps not a massive number, but definitely more than "hardly any," especially considering the incomplete surveys mentioned. This statement seems to contradict the text.

Option 3: All the languages are either complete languages or dialects of other major languages.

The text describes the process of trying to figure out if newly encountered speech is a "dialect of that language" or "so different that it has to be considered a different language." This implies that the distinction investigators are grappling with is between something being a separate "language" and being a "dialect" of another. While the text doesn't explicitly state this is a universal truth about all linguistic varieties, it frames the challenge of discovery and identification using this exact dichotomy. It reflects the operational categories being used in the scenario described.

Option 4: No new language needs to be discovered since all languages are known to the world.

The text explicitly says "A few new languages do continue to be discovered" and mentions incomplete surveys and hidden languages. This statement is clearly contradicted by the text.

Based on the text provided, Options 1 and 4 are definitely incorrect. Option 2 is also unlikely to be correct given that new languages are still being discovered. Option 3 best reflects the core distinction that the text describes investigators struggling with when trying to identify and count languages: determining if something is a distinct language or a dialect.

So, the statement that is correct based on the text is Option 3.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: ‘The people may be known, but the identity of their language may not be,’ means
options: People are recognised as different ethnic groups but not necessarily their language
People are recognised as different ethnic groups and their language is recognised
People are not recognised as different ethnic groups, and so are their languages
Because the people are not from different ethnic groups and their language is recognised
questions.solution:
The sentence means that we can identify the ethnic group, but we haven’t yet identified (or classified) their language. That corresponds to:

Option 1: People are recognised as different ethnic groups but not necessarily their language.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: Which one of the following words from passage means ‘come to be known’ ?
questions.solution:
Based on how the words are used in the passage, the word that means 'come to be known' is transpire.

Here's why:

In the sentence, "Only after a considerable period of contact does it transpire that the speech is so different…", 'transpire' means that it becomes known or it turns out to be the case after a while.

Let's quickly look at the other options:

lingua franca refers to a common language used by people who speak different native languages.

straight in this context means immediately or directly, as in "straight away".

So, 'transpire' is the word that carries the meaning of 'come to be known' in the text.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: <!-- x-tinymce/html -->Why do twentieth century advances not seem sustainable?
options: They do not meet the human needs
Due to the increased cost of agricultural production
Because they are outdated for the current times
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option 2: Due to the increased cost of agricultural production.

Step-by-step:

  • The passage says: “Since twentieth century advances, given increasing costs, do not seem sustainable…”

  • This clearly tells us that these advances are not sustainable because of increasing costs.

  • So the reason is the increased cost of agricultural production.

Therefore, the answer is:

$$\text{Option 2}$$

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: What pushes farmers into places unsuited for agriculture?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option 1: Population growth.

Step-by-step from the passage:

  • The passage says: “Global population growth… has pushed farmers and herders into areas unsuited to intensive agriculture.”

  • So, the reason is clearly population growth.

Hence, the answer is Population growth.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: <!-- x-tinymce/html -->Which one of the following is NOT true, according to the passage, for ecological degradation?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option 4: World War II.

Step-by-step:

  1. The passage gives causes of ecological degradation such as:

  • Global population growth

  • Excess use of chemicals through chemical runoffs

  • Desertification

  1. About World War II, the passage only says:

  • Tropical forests have shrunk by one-half since World War II

  • This gives a time reference, not a cause of ecological degradation.

Therefore, World War II is NOT stated as a cause of ecological degradation.

So, the answer is:

$$\text{Option 4: World War II}$$

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question: <!-- x-tinymce/html -->What is the conclusion of the author in the passage about the prospects of agriculture in the world?
options: The author is both hopeful and sceptical of the prospects of agriculture
The author is very hopeful of the prospects of agriculture
The author is sceptical of the prospects of agriculture
<!-- x-tinymce/html -->The author is neither hopeful nor sceptical of the prospects of agriculture
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option 1: The author is both hopeful and sceptical of the prospects of agriculture.

Step-by-step:

  1. The passage lists many serious problems:

  • loss of arable land and fresh water

  • pollution from chemical runoffs

  • destruction of natural habitats

  • desertification

  • shrinking tropical forests

  • harmful government intervention

  1. These points show that the author is sceptical about the future of agriculture.

  2. But in the last line, the author says:

  • “we may remain hopeful about humankind's agricultural prospects”

    This shows some hope.

  1. At the same time, the author also says:

  • “we can scarcely look forward optimistically”

    This shows doubt and lack of full confidence.

So, the author is partly hopeful but also doubtful.

Therefore, Option 1 is correct.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question:
Directions: The following items have a sentence in direct or indirect speech, followed by four options. One of the options correctly converts the direct speech into indirect speech. Select the most appropriate option and mark your answer on the Answer Sheet.

Convert from direct into indirect speech.

Ayushi said to the group, "We have had enough of suffering today. It is time to march forward."
options: Ayushi told the group that they had had enough of suffering that day and it was time to march forward
Ayushi asked the group that they had had enough of suffering this day and it was time to march forward
Ayushi told the group that they had enough of suffering and that it is time to march forward
Ayushi told the group that we had had enough of suffering this day and it was time to march forward
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option 1.

$$ \text{Ayushi told the group that they had had enough of suffering that day and it was time to march forward.} $$

Step-by-step explanation:

The direct speech is:

$$ \text{Ayushi said to the group, "We have had enough of suffering today. It is time to march forward."} $$

Rules used:

  1. "said to" changes to "told" when an object is given.

    $$ \text{said to the group} \rightarrow \text{told the group} $$

  2. Pronoun changes according to the reporting speech.

    $$ \text{We} \rightarrow \text{they} $$

    because Ayushi is speaking to the group.

  3. Present perfect changes to past perfect in indirect speech.

    $$ \text{have had} \rightarrow \text{had had} $$

  4. "today" changes to "that day".

    $$ \text{today} \rightarrow \text{that day} $$

  5. The second sentence also becomes part of the reported speech:

    $$ \text{It is time} \rightarrow \text{it was time} $$

Why the other options are wrong:

  • Option 2: uses asked, which is incorrect because this is not a question.

  • Option 3: does not correctly change the tense and keeps is instead of was.

  • Option 4: keeps we and this day, which are incorrect in indirect speech.

So, the most appropriate option is Option 1.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question:
Directions: The following items have a sentence in direct or indirect speech, followed by four options. One of the options correctly converts the direct speech into indirect speech. Select the most appropriate option and mark your answer on the Answer Sheet.

Convert from direct speech into indirect speech.

"Alas! We lost the match by one point," said the Captain
options: <!-- x-tinymce/html -->The Captain regretted that they had lost the match by one point
The Captain regretted that they lost the match by one point
The Captain exclaimed that they had lost the match by one point
The Captain told the team that they had lost the match by one point
questions.solution:
The correct option is Option 1.

Correct indirect speech:

The Captain regretted that they had lost the match by one point.

Step-by-step:

  • The word Alas! shows sorrow or regret.

  • In indirect speech, said is changed to regretted.

  • The pronoun We changes to they.

  • The reporting verb is in the past, so the past tense lost changes to past perfect had lost.

So, the correct answer is:

Option 1

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question:
Directions: The following items have a sentence in direct or indirect speech, followed by four options. One of the options correctly converts the direct speech into indirect speech. Select the most appropriate option and mark your answer on the Answer Sheet.

Convert from direct speech into indirect speech.

"Where were you last week?" Alok asked Sheela
options: Alok told Sheela where she had been the week before
Alok asked Sheela where she had been the week before
Alok asked Sheela where she was the week before
Alok asked Sheela where she had been the last week
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

Option 2: Alok asked Sheela where she had been the week before.

Step-by-step:

The direct speech is:

$$ \text{"Where were you last week?" Alok asked Sheela.} $$

Rules used:

  1. Since it is a question, asked is the correct reporting verb.

  2. The question word where remains the same.

  3. In indirect speech, you changes to she because Alok is asking Sheela.

  4. The past tense were changes to had been.

  5. last week changes to the week before.

So the correct indirect speech is:

$$ \text{Alok asked Sheela where she had been the week before.} $$

Why others are incorrect:

  • Option 1: Uses told, but this is a question, so asked should be used.

  • Option 3: was should change to had been.

  • Option 4: last week should change to the week before.

Answer: Option 2

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question:
Directions : Following items have sentences in active voice with four options. One of the options converts the active voice into passive voice correctly. Select the most appropriate option and mark your answer on the Answer Sheet. 

The king who the people loved built the castle five centuries ago
options: The castle was built five centuries ago by the king who was loved by the people
The castle was built five centuries ago by the king who loved the people
The castle built five centuries ago by the king loved by the people
The castle was built five centuries ago by the people who were loved by the king
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option 1.

Step-by-step:

Active sentence:

$$\text{The king who the people loved built the castle five centuries ago.}$$

  1. The object in the active voice is:

$$\text{the castle}$$

  1. In passive voice, the object becomes the subject:

$$\text{The castle}$$

  1. The verb is:

$$\text{built}$$

This is in the simple past tense, so in passive voice we use:

$$\text{was built}$$

  1. The doer is:

$$\text{the king who the people loved}$$

This becomes:

$$\text{by the king who was loved by the people}$$

So the correct passive form is:

$$\text{The castle was built five centuries ago by the king who was loved by the people.}$$

Why others are wrong:

  • Option 2 changes the meaning.

    It says the king loved the people, but the original says the people loved the king.

  • Option 3 is grammatically incomplete because was is missing.

  • Option 4 changes the subject completely and gives the wrong meaning.

Therefore, the correct option is:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 1}}$$

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question:
Directions : Following items have sentences in active voice with four options. One of the options converts the active voice into passive voice correctly. Select the most appropriate option and mark your answer on the Answer Sheet. 

The company has recruited four hundred engineers who will have to complete an internship for three weeks
options: Four hundred engineers have been recruited by the company and an internship of three weeks will have to be completed by them
Four hundred engineers have been recruited by the company and they will have to complete an internship of three weeks
The company has been recruited by four hundred engineers and an internship of three week will have to be completed by them
Four hundred engineers have been recruited by the company. Internship of three weeks will have had to be completed by them
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option 1.

Step-by-step:

The active sentence is:

The company has recruited four hundred engineers who will have to complete an internship for three weeks.

To change into passive voice:

  1. The object “four hundred engineers” becomes the subject.

  2. “has recruited” becomes “have been recruited”.

  3. “by the company” is added.

  4. The clause “who will have to complete an internship for three weeks” changes into passive as

    “an internship of three weeks will have to be completed by them.”

So the correct passive form is:

Four hundred engineers have been recruited by the company and an internship of three weeks will have to be completed by them.

Why others are wrong:

  • Option 2: Second part is still in active voice.

  • Option 3: Meaning becomes incorrect; the company cannot be recruited by engineers.

  • Option 4: “will have had to be completed” is grammatically incorrect here.

Final Answer:

Option 1

questions.title: BITSAT 2025
questions.question:
Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph.

A. Two astronomers - Johannes Kepler from Germany and Galileo from Italy-supported the Copernicus theory after nearly a century passed by.

B. Copernicus' view was that the Sun was stationary and the Earth and the planets moved in orbits around the Sun.

C. Nicholas Copernicus, a Polish priest, proposed a model in 1514.

D. There are many popular theories about the solar system. Ancient people believed that the Sun moved around the Earth and the Earth was static.
questions.solution: $$ \text { DCBA } $$

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question:
S1: Although all sources of energy commonly come from natural processes, non-renewable resources, are being rapidly depleted and at the rate they are being used.
S6: The sustainable use of natural resources in a manner that provides for resource needs for current resources for humans over a period of time can be termed as con- servation.
P: This will increase the time and cost of mining and once these resources are used up they cannot be replaced.
Q: Hence, we must remember that though our country is rich in natural deposits these resources are not.
R: Extraction of these ores through the process of mining will soon become difficult and also expensive because these minerals are often located deep from greater depths over time.
S: Mineral resources can be said to be finite and non-renewable.
questions.solution:
The sentences slot in as follows:

S1 → S → Q → R → P → S6

That corresponds to option 3, i.e. the order SQRP.

Briefly:

• S defines mineral resources as finite/non-renewable right after the depletion warning in S1.

• Q then draws the conclusion (“these resources are not [infinite]”).

• R explains how that finiteness makes mining difficult and costly as ores lie deeper.

• P adds that time and cost of mining will rise and once gone they can’t be replaced.

• Finally, S6 offers “sustainable use …” (conservation) as the way forward.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question:
S1: India, at present has one of the largest road networks in the world.
S6: It passes through Peshawar, Delhi and Amritsar and terminates in Kabul (Afghanistan).
P: The importance of roads has been recognised in India since the ancient times.
Q: The Grand Trunk Road was built by Sher Shah Suri across the Indo-Gangetic plain from Chittagong (Bangladesh) to Peshawar (Pakistan).
R: Kings such as Ashoka and Akbar also improved the con- struction of roads and people.
S: Construction of goods continued as an important activity in the late medieval period.
questions.solution:
The four middle sentences must follow a chronological flow—ancient recognition, early imperial improvements, late‐medieval activity, then Sher Shah’s Grand Trunk Road—so the correct order is

S1 → P → R → S → Q → S6

Which corresponds to Option 4 (PRSQ).

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question:
S1: National highways connect one state will another and are of national importance.
S6: These road systems are also known as primary road systems and are laid and operated under the National Highway Authority of India.
P: There are important because while others are built to connect 75 per cent of the total road networks, they carry 40 per cent of the total road traffic.
Q: The infrastructure of the country is therefore crucial, and the development and maintenance is of critical importance.
R: This is due to the traffic because these roads connect long distances and pass through many cities and towns.
S: Since they cover the length and breadth of the country and link cities and towns, these highways are the primary facilitators of trade and connectivity.
questions.solution:
The sentences fit together most coherently in the order S → R → P → Q.

So the complete passage is:

S1: National highways connect one state to another and are of national importance.

S: Since they cover the length and breadth of the country and link cities and towns, these highways are the primary facilitators of trade and connectivity.

R: This is due to the traffic because these roads connect long distances and pass through many cities and towns.

P: They are important because, while other roads account for 75 % of the total network, national highways carry 40 % of the total road traffic.

Q: The infrastructure of the country is therefore crucial, and its development and maintenance are of critical importance.

S6: These road systems are also known as primary road systems and are laid and operated under the National Highway Authority of India.

Answer: Option 2 (SRPQ).

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question:
S1: The South African Constitution was inaugurated in December 1996.
S6: special constitutional court enforces the rights enshrined in the Constitution.
P: Its creation and promulgation took place at a time when South Africa was after the brutal era of the Warfield Government.
Q: The South African Constitution says that its "Bill of Rights is the cornerstone of democracy in South Africa."
R: Apropos, it forbids discrimination on the grounds of "race, gender, sexual orientation, status, ethnic or social origin, colour, age, disability, religion, conscience, belief, culture, language and birth."
S: The Bill of Rights grants perhaps the most extensive range of rights to its citizens.
questions.solution:
S1 introduces the South African Constitution and its inauguration date.

S6 talks about the special court that enforces the rights mentioned in the Constitution.

Now let's look at P, Q, R, and S:

P: Gives historical context for the constitution's creation (after the Warfield Government). This seems like a good follow-up to S1, setting the scene.

Q: Introduces the "Bill of Rights" and calls it the "cornerstone of democracy".

R: Lists specific grounds for forbidding discrimination, which are examples of rights.

S: Says the Bill of Rights grants extensive rights.

Let's try connecting them logically:

S1 sets the stage by mentioning the constitution's inauguration.

P then provides the crucial historical context in which it was created. (S1 -> P)

After introducing the constitution and its context, it makes sense to talk about a key part of it. Both Q and S introduce the Bill of Rights. Q calls it the "cornerstone", and S says it grants "extensive rights". Q stating its significance as a "cornerstone" seems like a good way to introduce its importance, which S can then elaborate on by saying how it's important – by granting extensive rights. (P -> Q -> S)

R then provides specific examples of these extensive rights, particularly the non-discrimination clauses. (S -> R)

Finally, S6 talks about the court that enforces these rights. This follows naturally after discussing the rights themselves.

So, the logical sequence appears to be P, Q, S, R.

Let's put it all together:

S1: The South African Constitution was inaugurated in December 1996.

P: Its creation and promulgation took place at a time when South Africa was after the brutal era of the Warfield Government.

Q: The South African Constitution says that its "Bill of Rights is the cornerstone of democracy in South Africa."

S: The Bill of Rights grants perhaps the most extensive range of rights to its citizens.

R: Apropos, it forbids discrimination on the grounds of "race, gender, sexual orientation, status, ethnic or social origin, colour, age, disability, religion, conscience, belief, culture, language and birth."

S6: special constitutional court enforces the rights enshrined in the Constitution.

This sequence PQSR makes a lot of sense. It starts with the constitution's inauguration, provides context, introduces the Bill of Rights and its significance (cornerstone), describes its scope (extensive rights), gives specific examples (non-discrimination), and ends by mentioning the enforcement mechanism.

Looking at the options provided:

Option 1: QSRP

Option 2: SPQR

Option 3: PQRS

Option 4: PQSR

Our derived sequence PQSR matches Option 4.

The final answer is $\boxed{PQSR}$.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question:
S1: Our Constitution reminds us of the necessity of representation in decision making.
S6: Elections have today become the most crucial symbol of the democratic process.
P: This is why elections become important.
Q: Our representatives are elected by the people.
R: Whenever we think of India as a democracy, our mind invariably turns to universal suffrage.
S: All citizens cannot participate in every decision.
questions.solution:
The most coherent ordering of the middle four sentences is S → P → Q → R, i.e. Option 2 (SPQR).

Reconstructed passage:

Our Constitution reminds us of the necessity of representation in decision making.

All citizens cannot participate in every decision.

This is why elections become important.

Our representatives are elected by the people.

Whenever we think of India as a democracy, our mind invariably turns to universal suffrage.

Elections have today become the most crucial symbol of the democratic process.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question:
participate in the function     many of the people who had come to
                       P                                     Q
the auditorium was over-crowded            could not find a seat because
                 R                                                                   S
questions.solution:
We need to figure out the best order for P, Q, R, and S.

Looking at the parts:

Q: "many of the people who had come to" - This looks like the beginning, introducing the subject.

P: "participate in the function" - This tells us why the people came. It fits well after "come to". So, QP makes sense: "many of the people who had come to participate in the function".

S: "could not find a seat because" - This tells us what happened to those people. It follows naturally after describing the people. So, QPS: "many of the people who had come to participate in the function could not find a seat because".

R: "the auditorium was over-crowded" - This gives the reason for why they couldn't find a seat. It fits perfectly after "because". So, QPSR: "many of the people who had come to participate in the function could not find a seat because the auditorium was over-crowded".

This order, QPSR, creates a complete and logical sentence.

Now let's check the options:

Option 1: PRQS - Doesn't fit.

Option 2: PQRS - Doesn't fit.

Option 3: QPSR - Yes, this matches our logical order.

Option 4: SPRQ - Doesn't fit.

So, the most appropriate arrangement is QPSR.

The correct option is Option 3.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question:
lay outside her existing life           she knew that the answer
               P                                                      Q 
somewhere in the back of her mind             to her questions about life
                      R                                                              S
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option (4) i.e. RQSP

Key Points

The sentence illustrates a realization occurring in someone's thoughts.

We begin with the adverbial phrase that identifies the location of this knowledge: "somewhere in the back of her mind" (R).

This is followed by the main clause: "she knew that the answer" (Q), thus forming RQ so far.

Next, we clarify what the answer pertained to: "to her questions about life" (S), extending the thought to RQS.

Finally, we describe where the answer was found: "lay outside her existing life" (P). This completes the sentence as RQSP.

Therefore, the correct rearrangement is RQSP.

Additional Information

Option 1 (PRQS): "lay outside her existing life somewhere in the back of her mind she knew that the answer to her questions about life" - This order results in a sentence that is grammatically incorrect and confusing.

Option 2 (QSPR): "she knew that the answer to her questions about life lay outside her existing life somewhere in the back of her mind" - Although grammatically possible, placing "somewhere in the back of her mind" at the end makes the sentence appear less natural and clear compared to starting with it.

Option 3 (PSRQ): "lay outside her existing life to her questions about life somewhere in the back of her mind she knew that the answer" - This sequence is grammatically fragmented and does not make sense.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question:
works of literature                 inside the pages of all good
                  P                                                     Q
lies the truth about some of                  the more challenging questions posed by humanity
                    R                                                                                 S
questions.solution:
The parts come together most smoothly in the order Q–P–R–S:

inside the pages of all good (Q)

works of literature (P)

lies the truth about some of (R)

the more challenging questions posed by humanity (S)

“inside the pages of all good works of literature lies the truth about some of the more challenging questions posed by humanity.”

Answer: Option 2 (QPRS)

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question:
takes on account of its economic successes           to address continuing social inequality.
                              P                                                                                   Q
for every stride of progress any nation                    it takes two back if it is unable
                               R                                                                S
questions.solution:
P: takes on account of its economic successes

Q: to address continuing social inequality.

R: for every stride of progress any nation

S: it takes two back if it is unable

We need to find the best sequence that makes a complete thought. Let's try building the sentence piece by piece.

Often, sentences start with a general idea or a subject. R seems like a good candidate for the beginning: "for every stride of progress any nation…"

What does a nation do? It "takes" a stride of progress. So, R could naturally be followed by P: "for every stride of progress any nation takes on account of its economic successes…"

Now, what happens "for every stride" it takes? The phrase "it takes two back" in S suggests a consequence or a setback despite progress. The "it" in S refers back to "any nation". So, S should likely follow R P: "for every stride of progress any nation takes on account of its economic successes it takes two back if it is unable…"

Finally, unable to do what? The last piece Q tells us: "…to address continuing social inequality." This fits perfectly after the "if it is unable" part of S.

So, putting it all together, the order R P S Q seems to form a coherent sentence:

"for every stride of progress any nation takes on account of its economic successes it takes two back if it is unable to address continuing social inequality."

Let's check this order against the options provided. The order R P S Q corresponds to Option 3.

The most appropriate arrangement is RPSQ.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question:
the book-value of a loan or                 an intangible asset over a set period of time
                  P                                                                     Q
used to periodically lower                      amortisation is an accounting technique
                  R                                                                      S
questions.solution:
The correct sequence of the sentence is Option (1): SRPQ.

Key Points

The sentence is providing a definition of "amortisation."

First, we identify the main subject and its initial description: "amortisation is an accounting technique" (S).

Next, we describe how this technique is "used to periodically lower" (R), forming SR.

We then specify what is being lowered: "the book-value of a loan or" (P), extending it to SRP.

Finally, we complete the definition by adding "an intangible asset over a set period of time" (Q), leading to the full sequence: SRPQ.

Therefore, the correct answer is SRPQ.

Additional Information

Option 2 (RSPQ): "used to periodically lower amortisation is an accounting technique the book-value of a loan or an intangible asset over a set period of time" – The sequence is incorrect and grammatically disjointed.

Option 3 (SRQP): "amortisation is an accounting technique used to periodically lower an intangible asset over a set period of time the book-value of a loan or" – The order of P and Q is swapped, resulting in incorrect phrasing.

Option 4 (RPSQ): "used to periodically lower the book-value of a loan or amortisation is an accounting technique an intangible asset over a set period of time" – This sequence is grammatically incorrect and does not form a coherent definition.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question:
Directions : In this section each item consists of six sentences of a passage. The first and sixth sentences are given in the beginning as S1 and S6. The middle four sentences in each have been jumbled up and labelled as P, Q, R and S. You are required to find the proper sequence of the four sentences and mark your response accordingly on the Answer Sheet. 

S1: Sufism is an English word coined in the Nineteenth Century 

S6 : It may also have been suffa, the platform outside a place of Islamic worship

P. Historians have understood this term in several ways

Q. According to some scholars, it is derived from suf, meaning wool 

R. Others derive it from safa, meaning purity 

S. The word used for Sufism in Islamic texts is tasawwuf
questions.solution:
The correct sequence is:

$$\boxed{\text{SPQR}}$$

Step-by-step:

  • $S1$ says: Sufism is an English word coined in the Nineteenth Century.

  • After this, sentence $S$ should come because it gives the corresponding Islamic term:

  • The word used for Sufism in Islamic texts is tasawwuf.

  • Then sentence $P$ fits:

  • Historians have understood this term in several ways.

  • Here, "this term" refers to tasawwuf.

  • After that, the different explanations come in order:

  • $Q$: According to some scholars, it is derived from suf, meaning wool

  • $R$: Others derive it from safa, meaning purity

  • Finally, $S6$ adds one more possible origin:

  • It may also have been suffa…

So the full order is:

$$S1 - S - P - Q - R - S6$$

Correct option:

$$\boxed{2}$$

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question:
Directions : In this section each item consists of six sentences of a passage. The first and sixth sentences are given in the beginning as S1 and S6. The middle four sentences in each have been jumbled up and labelled as P, Q, R and S. You are required to find the proper sequence of the four sentences and mark your response accordingly on the Answer Sheet. 

S1: Al-Biruni was born in 973, in Khwarizm in _ present-day Uzbekistan 

S6 : He arrived in Ghazni as a hostage, but gradually developed a liking for the city

P. In 1017, when Sultan Mahmud invaded Khwarizm, he _ took several scholars back to his capital, Ghazni : Al-Biruni was one among them

Q. He was well versed in several languages; Syriac, Arabic, Persian, Hebrew, and Sanskrit  

R. Khwarizm was an important centre of learning, and Al-Biruni received the best education available at the time  

S. However he did not know Greek, although he was familiar with the works of Plato and other Greek philosophers
questions.solution:
To find the correct sequence of the jumbled sentences, let us analyze the logical flow of the passage step-by-step:

  1. Connecting to S1:

Sentence S1 states that Al-Biruni was born in Khwarizm. Sentence R directly continues this topic by describing Khwarizm as a centre of learning where Al-Biruni received his education. This makes R the perfect continuation of S1.

  1. Forming a pair (Q and S):

Sentence Q mentions that Al-Biruni was well versed in several languages. Sentence S starts with the contrasting word "However" and states that he did not know Greek. Because S introduces an exception to the languages he knew in Q, Q and S form a mandatory pair (QS).

  1. Connecting to S6:

Sentence P tells us about the historical event in 1017 when Sultan Mahmud took scholars, including Al-Biruni, to Ghazni. Sentence S6 picks up exactly from here, stating how he arrived in Ghazni as a hostage and slowly developed a liking for the city. Therefore, P must be the sentence immediately preceding S6.

By putting these clues together, the logical sequence is R - Q - S - P.

Correct Sequence:

S1: Al-Biruni was born in 973, in Khwarizm in present-day Uzbekistan

R: Khwarizm was an important centre of learning, and Al-Biruni received the best education available at the time

Q: He was well versed in several languages; Syriac, Arabic, Persian, Hebrew, and Sanskrit

S: However he did not know Greek, although he was familiar with the works of Plato and other Greek philosophers

P: In 1017, when Sultan Mahmud invaded Khwarizm, he took several scholars back to his capital, Ghazni : Al-Biruni was one among them

S6: He arrived in Ghazni as a hostage, but gradually developed a liking for the city

The proper sequence is RQSP.

Correct Answer:

Option 2

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question:
Directions : In this section each item consists of six sentences of a passage. The first and sixth sentences are given in the beginning as S1 and S6. The middle four sentences in each have been jumbled up and labelled as P, Q, R and S. You are required to find the proper sequence of the four sentences and mark your response accordingly on the Answer Sheet. 

S1: Vijayanagara or ‘city of victory’ was the name of both a city and an empire 

S6 : They remembered it as Hampi, a name derived from that of the local mother goddess, Pampadevi

P. In its heyday it stretched from the river Krishna in the north to the extreme south of the Indian peninsula

Q. Although it fell into ruin in the seventeenth-eighteenth centuries, it continued to live in the memories of the people who inhabited the Krishna-Tungabhadra doab 

R. In 1565 the city was sacked and subsequently deserted

S. The Empire was founded in the fourteenth century
questions.solution:
The correct sequence is:

$$\boxed{\text{SPRQ}}$$

Step-by-step:

  • $S1$ introduces Vijayanagara as both a city and an empire.

  • $S$ should come first: it tells us when the empire was founded.

  • $P$ follows naturally: after introducing the empire, it describes its extent.

  • $R$ then tells what happened later: in $1565$, the city was sacked and deserted.

  • $Q$ comes after that because it begins with “Although it fell into ruin…”, which refers to the destruction mentioned in $R$.

  • Finally, $S6$ says that people remembered it as Hampi.

So the full order is:

$$S1 \rightarrow S \rightarrow P \rightarrow R \rightarrow Q \rightarrow S6$$

Hence, the answer is:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 1}}$$

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question:
Directions : In this section each item consists of six sentences of a passage. The first and sixth sentences are given in the beginning as S1 and S6. The middle four sentences in each have been jumbled up and labelled as P, Q, R and S. You are required to find the proper sequence of the four sentences and mark your response accordingly on the Answer Sheet. 

S1: Liberalisation and _ globalisation freed India’s economy from the low GDP trap that had impeded India’s progress 

S6 : Today India has transformed into one of the largest economies of the world

P. India’s 3 pe rate of growth and a set of emergent circumstances led to a balance of payment crisis in the early 1990s 

Q. Ease of business was ensured, and the requirement for import licences, and production quotas, were removed

R. Apart from these measures India also took a loan from the IMF to tide over the crisis, but after 1993, India has not taken another loan from the IMF

S. In a landmark budget in 1991, responding to the emergent crisis, India delicenced many industries and liberalised its economic policies
questions.solution:
The correct sequence is:

$$\boxed{\text{PSQR}}$$

So, the correct answer is:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 4}}$$

Step-by-step logic:

  • P should come first because it explains why reforms were needed:

  • India faced a balance of payment crisis in the early 1990s.

  • S comes next because it tells what India did in response:

  • In 1991, India delicensed industries and liberalised economic policies.

  • Q follows naturally because it gives further details of those reforms:

  • Ease of business was ensured.

  • Import licences and production quotas were removed.

  • R comes after that because “Apart from these measures” clearly refers to the reforms already mentioned in S and Q:

  • India also took a loan from the IMF.

Thus the full order is:

$$S1 \rightarrow P \rightarrow S \rightarrow Q \rightarrow R \rightarrow S6$$

Final answer:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 4: PSQR}}$$

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question:
Directions : In this section each item consists of six sentences of a passage. The first and sixth sentences are given in the beginning as S1 and S6. The middle four sentences in each have been jumbled up and labelled as P, Q, R and S. You are required to find the proper sequence of the four sentences and mark your response accordingly on the Answer Sheet. 

S1: In many countries, there are millions of people who are underprivileged and deprived 

S6 : In the past forced labour was imposed by landlords, moneylenders, and other wealthy persons

P. Both of these are prohibited under the Constitution of India  

Q. One such form of exploitation in our country has been begar or forced labour without payment 

R. The marginalised are often subjected to exploitation by their fellow human beings 

S. Another closely related form of exploitation is buying and selling of human beings and using them as slaves
questions.solution:
The correct sequence is:

$$\boxed{\text{RQSP}}$$

So, the correct answer is:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 2}}$$

Step-by-step:

  • $S1$ says that many people are underprivileged and deprived.

  • The next logical sentence is $R$:

    "The marginalised are often subjected to exploitation by their fellow human beings."

    This continues the idea of deprivation and exploitation.

  • Then $Q$:

    "One such form of exploitation in our country has been begar or forced labour without payment."

    Here, "one such form" refers to exploitation mentioned in $R$.

  • Then $S$:

    "Another closely related form of exploitation is buying and selling of human beings and using them as slaves."

    The word "Another" clearly follows after one form has already been mentioned in $Q$.

  • Then $P$:

    "Both of these are prohibited under the Constitution of India."

    "Both of these" refers to the two forms mentioned in $Q$ and $S$.

  • Finally, $S6$:

    "In the past forced labour was imposed by landlords, moneylenders, and other wealthy persons."

Hence the proper order is:

$$S1 \rightarrow R \rightarrow Q \rightarrow S \rightarrow P \rightarrow S6$$

Answer: $$\boxed{\text{RQSP}}$$

questions.title: BITSAT 2025
questions.question:
Balance the following redox reaction in acidic medium and determine the stoichiometric coefficient of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ in the final balanced equation.

$$ \mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}(a q)+\mathrm{Fe}^{2+}(a q) \longrightarrow \mathrm{Mn}^{2+}(a q)+\mathrm{Fe}^{3+}(a q) $$
questions.solution:
First, separate into two half-reactions:

Oxidation $\left(\mathrm{Fe}^{2+}\right.$ to $\left.\mathrm{Fe}^{3+}\right)$,

$$ \mathrm{Fe}^{2+} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Fe}^{3+}+1 e^{-} $$

Reduction ( $\mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}$to $\mathrm{Mn}^{2+}$ ),

$$ \mathrm{MnO}_4^{-} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Mn}^{2+} $$

Balance O by adding $4 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$,

$$ \mathrm{MnO}_4^{-} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Mn}^{2+}+4 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} $$

Balance H by adding $8 \mathrm{H}^{+}$,

$$ 8 \mathrm{H}^{+}+\mathrm{MnO}_4^{-} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Mn}^{2+}+4 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} $$

Add $5 e^{-}$to balance charge,

$$ 8 \mathrm{H}^{+}+\mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}+5 e^{-} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Mn}^{2+}+4 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} $$

Now, multiply oxidation half-reaction by 5 :

$$ 5 \mathrm{Fe}^{2+} \longrightarrow 5 \mathrm{Fe}^{3+}+5 e^{-} $$

Now add both:

$$ 8 \mathrm{H}^{+}+\mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}+5 \mathrm{Fe}^{2+} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Mn}^{2+}+5 \mathrm{Fe}^{3+}+4 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} $$

Thus, the stoichiometric coefficient of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}=4$

questions.title: BITSAT 2025
questions.question: Calculate the volume of $M / 10 \mathrm{KMnO}_4$ needed to react with 20 mL of $M / 2 \mathrm{FeSO}_4$ in acidic medium.
questions.solution:
Balanced reaction,

$$ \begin{aligned} & 5 \mathrm{Fe}^{2+}+\mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}+8 \mathrm{H}^{+} \longrightarrow 5 \mathrm{Fe}^{3+}+\mathrm{Mn}^{2+}+4 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \\ & \text { Using, molarity }=\frac{\text { Number of moles of solute }}{\text { Volume of solution }(\mathrm{L})} \\ & \begin{aligned} \text { or } \quad\left(\mathrm{KMnO}_4\right) & =\left(\mathrm{FeSO}_4\right) \\ \frac{M_1 V_1}{n_1} & =\frac{M_2 V_2}{n_2} \\ \mathrm{Fe}^{2+} \text { moles } & =0.02 \times 0.5=0.01 \mathrm{~mol} \end{aligned} \end{aligned} $$

5 moles of $\mathrm{Fe}^{2+}$ react with 1 mole of $\mathrm{KMnO}_4$. So,

$\mathrm{KMnO}_4$ required $=0.01 / 5=0.002 \mathrm{~mol}$

Volume $=0.002 / 0.1=0.02 \mathrm{~L}=20 \mathrm{~mL}$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2025 Paper 1 Online
questions.question: One of the products formed from the reaction of permanganate ion with iodide ion in neutral aqueous medium is
questions.solution:
Reaction of permanganate ion with iodide ion in neutral aqueous medium
In neutral medium, $\mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}$is reduced to $\mathrm{MnO}_2$ and io did ion oxidizes to $\mathrm{IO}_3^{-}$.
In the reaction of $\mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}$and $I^{-}, \mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}$is the oxidizing agent (it gets reduced) and $I^{-}$acts as the reducing agent (it get oxidized).
The products of the redux reaction between $\mathrm{MnO}_4^{-1}$ and $I^{-}$is determined by the reaction medium.
In acidic medium, $\mathrm{MnO}_4^{-} \rightarrow \mathrm{Mn}^{2+}$
In basic medium, $\mathrm{MnO}_4^{-} \rightarrow \mathrm{MnO}_4^{2-}$
In neutral medium, $\mathrm{MnO}_4^{-} \rightarrow \mathrm{MnO}_2$
Here, the reaction medium given is neutral and hence $\mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}$is reduced to $\mathrm{MnO}_2$. Also, the $\mathrm{I}^{-}$ion is oxidized to $\mathrm{IO}_3^{-}$in the neutral medium.
$$ \begin{aligned} & \mathrm{MnO}_4^{-} \rightarrow \mathrm{MnO}_2 \\ & \mathrm{I}^{-} \rightarrow \mathrm{IO}_3^{-} \end{aligned} $$
The balanced chemical equation can be written as
$$ \mathrm{2MnO}_4^{-}+\mathrm{I}^{-}+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \longrightarrow \mathrm{IMnO}_2+\mathrm{IO}_3^{-}+2 \mathrm{OH}^{-} $$
Answer: Option (B) $\mathrm{IO}_3^{-}$

questions.title: BITSAT 2025
questions.question: A man with a mass of 80 kg is standing on the rim to a circular platform with a mass of 200 kg . The circular platform is rotating at 12 revolutions per minute (rpm) about its axis. The man moves from the rim towards the centre of the platform. The new angular velocity of the system will be (Assuming that the man's moment of inertia at the centre of the plateform is negligible)
questions.solution:
According to the law of conservation of angular momentum.

Total initial angular momentum = Total final angular momentum

$$ \begin{aligned} \left(I+m R^2\right) \omega & =I \omega^{\prime} \\ \therefore \quad \omega^{\prime} & =\frac{I+m R^2}{I} \omega \Rightarrow \omega^{\prime}=\frac{\frac{M R^2}{2}+m R^2}{\frac{M R^2}{2}} \omega \\ \text { or } \quad \omega^{\prime} & =\frac{M+2 m}{M}=\frac{200+2 \times 80}{200} \times 12 \\ \omega^{\prime} & =\frac{360}{200} \times 12=21.6 \mathrm{rpm} \end{aligned} $$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2025 Paper 1 Online
questions.question: The center of a disk of radius $r$ and mass $m$ is attached to a spring of spring constant $k$, inside a ring of radius $R>r$ as shown in the figure. The other end of the spring is attached on the periphery of the ring. Both the ring and the disk are in the same vertical plane. The disk can only roll along the inside periphery of the ring, without slipping. The spring can only be stretched or compressed along the periphery of the ring, following the Hooke's law. In equilibrium, the disk is at the bottom of the ring. Assuming small displacement of the disc, the time period of oscillation of center of mass of the disk is written as $T=\frac{2 \pi}{\omega}$. The correct expression for $\omega$ is ( $g$ is the acceleration due to gravity):
questions.solution:
Let's disc rolls to $\phi$ angular displacement about its centre:

As the disc rolls without slipping. we can write,

$ x=r \phi=(R-r) \theta \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,...(i)$

Now, Let's apply $\tau=I \alpha$ about contact

$ \begin{aligned} & \text { point } \\ & \tau=m g \sin \theta \cdot r+k x r=\left(\frac{m r^2}{2}+m r^2\right) \alpha^{\prime} \\ & \Rightarrow m g \theta \cdot r+k(R-r) \theta r=\frac{3 m r^2}{2} \alpha^{\prime} \\ & \Rightarrow[m g+k(R-r)] \theta r=\frac{3}{2} m r^{\prime} \alpha^{\prime} \\ & \Rightarrow[m g+k(R-r)] \theta=\frac{3}{2} m r \alpha^{\prime}\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,...(ii) \end{aligned} $

here, $\alpha^{\prime}=\frac{d^2 \phi}{d t^2}$

from (1), $\phi=\left(\frac{R-r}{r}\right) 0$

$ \begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad \alpha^{\prime}=\frac{d^2 \phi}{d t^2}=\left(\frac{R-r}{r}\right) \frac{d^2 \theta}{d t^2} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \alpha^{\prime \prime}=\left(\frac{R-r}{r}\right) \alpha\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,...(iii) \end{aligned} $

Now, from (2) and (3),

$ \begin{aligned} & {[m g+k(R-r)] \theta=\frac{3}{2} m r\left(\frac{R-r}{r}\right) \alpha} \\ & \Rightarrow[m g+k(R-r)] \theta=\frac{3}{2} m(R-r) \alpha \end{aligned} $

Now, we know, for SHM,

$$ \begin{gathered} \alpha=\omega^2 \theta \\ \Rightarrow \quad\left[\frac{m g+k(R-r)}{\frac{3}{2} m(R-r)}\right] \phi=\omega^2 \phi \\ \Rightarrow \frac{2}{3} \frac{g}{(R-r)}+\frac{2}{3} \frac{k}{m}=\omega^2 \\ \Rightarrow \omega=\sqrt{\frac{2}{3}\left[\frac{g}{(R-r)}+\frac{k}{m}\right]} \end{gathered} $$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2025 Paper 1 Online
questions.question:
The correct match of the group reagents in List-I for precipitating the metal ion given in List-II from solutions, is

List–I List–II
(P) Passing $\mathrm{H_2S}$ in the presence of $\mathrm{NH_4OH}$ (1) $\mathrm{Cu^{2+}}$
(Q) $\mathrm{(NH_4)_2CO_3}$ in the presence of $\mathrm{NH_4OH}$ (2) $\mathrm{Al^{3+}}$
(R) $\mathrm{NH_4OH}$ in the presence of $\mathrm{NH_4Cl}$ (3) $\mathrm{Mn^{2+}}$
(S) Passing $\mathrm{H_2S}$ in the presence of dilute $\mathrm{HCl}$ (4) $\mathrm{Ba^{2+}}$
(5) $\mathrm{Mg^{2+}}$
questions.solution:
P) Passing $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}$ in the presence of $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{OH}$ It is fourth group reagent. The metal ions (cations) included in this group are $\mathrm{Mn}^{2+}, \mathrm{Zn}^{2+}, \mathrm{Ni}^{2+}$ and $\mathrm{Co}^{2 \mathrm{t}}$.
This group reagent is used for precipitating the cations $\mathrm{Mn}^{2+}, \mathrm{Zn}^{2+}, \mathrm{Ni}^{2+}$ and $\mathrm{C}_0{ }^{2+}$. They are precipitated as sulfides.
From the given cations, $\mathrm{Mn}^{2+}$ is precipitated by passing $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}$ in the presence of $\mathrm{NH}_4OH$ Precipitate of $\mathrm{Mn}^{2+}$ is MnS .
$$ P-3 $$
Q) $\left(\mathrm{NH}_4\right)_2 \mathrm{CO}_3$ in the presence of $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{OH}$ It is fifth group reagent. The cations, included in group $\bar{V}$ are $\mathrm{Ba}^{2+}, \mathrm{Ca}^{2+}$ and $\mathrm{Sr}^{2+}$. These cations are precipitated using the reagent $\left(\mathrm{NH}_4\right)_2 \mathrm{CO}_3$ in the presence of. $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{OH}$. These cations are precipitated as carbonates.
From the given cations, $\mathrm{Ba}^{2+}$ is precipitated by using $\left(\mathrm{NH}_4\right)_2 \mathrm{CO}_3$ in the presence of $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{OH}$. Precipitate of $\mathrm{Ba}^{2+}$ is $\mathrm{BaCO}_3$. $$ Q-4 $$
R) $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{OH} \,\,{\text {in }}$ the presence of $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{Cl}$ It is group III reagent. Group III cations are $\mathrm{Al}^{3+}, \mathrm{Cr}^{3+}$ and $\mathrm{Fe}^{3+}$. These cations get precipitated by using $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{OH}$ with $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{Cl}$. The cations are precipitated as hydroxides.
From the given cations, $\mathrm{Al}^{3+}$ is precipitated by using $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{OH}$ in the presence of $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{Cl}$. Precipitate of $\mathrm{Al}^{3+}$ is $\mathrm{Al}(\mathrm{OH})_3$ $$ R-2 $$
S) Passing $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}$ in the presence of dilute HCl It is group II reagent. Group II cations are $\mathrm{Cu}^{2+}, \mathrm{Cd}^{2+}, \mathrm{Hg}^{2+}, \mathrm{Bi}^{2+}, \mathrm{Pb}^{2+}, \mathrm{Sn}^{2+}, \mathrm{Sn}^{4+}, \mathrm{Sb}^{3+}, \mathrm{As}^{3+}$ and $A s^{5+}$. They are precipitated as sulfides that are insoluble in dilute HCl . From the given cations, $\mathrm{Cu}^{2+}$ is precipitated by passing $H_2 S$ in the presence of dilute HCl Precipitate of $\mathrm{Cu}^{2+}$ is Cus. $$ S-1 $$
$$ \begin{aligned} &\text { Answer: Option (A) }\\ &P \rightarrow 3, Q \rightarrow 4, \quad R \rightarrow 2, \quad S \rightarrow 1 \end{aligned} $$

questions.title: BITSAT 2025
questions.question: The equivalent resistance of the circuit across $A B$ is given by
questions.solution:
If $V_A>V_B$, both the junction diodes are forward biased and the given circuit diagram becomes a balanced Wheatstone bridge. The equivalent resistance in this case becomes $4 \Omega$.

If $V_A < V_B$, the diodes are reverse biased. In that case $4 \Omega, 5 \Omega$ and $4 \Omega$ are in series. So, equivalent resistance becomes $13 \Omega$.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: __________________ your transfer request is concerned, we'll be discussing it at the next meeting.
questions.solution:
Explanation:

The correct discourse marker for the sentence is "As far as."

Key Points:

The phrase "As far as…is concerned" is a widely accepted and grammatically correct discourse marker used to introduce a specific topic or aspect. It indicates that the subsequent statement pertains directly to that subject.

In the context of the sentence, it specifies that the transfer request will be addressed in the upcoming meeting.

Therefore, the correct answer is Option 1.

Complete Sentence:

As far as your transfer request is concerned, we'll be discussing it at the next meeting.

Additional Information:

Option 2: "To begin with" is employed to introduce the initial point or topic in a series, or to state an initial condition. It doesn't align with the structure "…is concerned."

Option 3: "By the way" is typically used to introduce information that is off-topic or tangential to the current discussion. It does not match the structure "…is concerned."

Option 4: "Talking about" introduces a related topic or a new subject. While it serves to introduce a topic, it doesn't fit the grammatical construct "…is concerned." Instead, you would say, "Talking about your transfer request, we'll discuss it…"

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: ___________________, did you know Ravi has a new job ?
questions.solution:
"_______, did you know Ravi has a new job?"

We need something that smoothly introduces a new topic, almost like a little detour in the conversation.

Think about the options:

For one thing: We usually use this when we're listing reasons or points about something. Like, "Why don't you like the movie? For one thing, the acting was terrible." It doesn't quite fit for just dropping some news.

By the way: Ah, this one is perfect for bringing up something new or slightly unrelated that just popped into your head during a conversation. Like, "We were talking about the party… by the way, did you know Ravi has a new job?" It shifts the focus naturally.

In the first place: This is another one we use for listing reasons, often for the initial point. Like, "Why didn't you go? In the first place, I was too busy." Not quite right here.

As a result: This shows cause and effect. "He studied hard; as a result, he passed the exam." Definitely doesn't fit introducing news.

So, the one that feels most natural for suddenly asking if someone knows about Ravi's new job is By the way. It signals a friendly shift to a different topic.

Option 2, By the way, is the best fit!

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: __________________ the wind, it was getting stronger, and I was getting colder.
questions.solution:
The sentence is talking about the wind and how it's affecting the speaker. We need a phrase to smoothly introduce the wind as the subject being discussed, leading into the description of it getting stronger and making the speaker colder.

Let's consider the choices:

To begin with: This is usually used to start a discussion or list the first point of something. It doesn't quite fit starting a sentence about an ongoing condition like the wind.

By the way: This is used to introduce a new topic or add extra information that isn't directly related to what was just said. The wind is the main subject here, not an aside.

First of all: Similar to "to begin with," it's for introducing the initial point or item. Not the right fit here.

Speaking of: Ah, this one is often used to transition to a topic that is currently relevant or has just been mentioned (even if implicitly by the situation). If you are out in the wind, and you want to comment on it, saying "Speaking of the wind…" works perfectly to introduce your observation.

Looking at the sentence, "__ the wind, it was getting stronger, and I was getting colder," option 4, "Speaking of," flows naturally. It introduces the wind as the topic you are about to discuss further.

So, the most appropriate discourse marker is "Speaking of".

The completed sentence would be:

Speaking of the wind, it was getting stronger, and I was getting colder.

This makes sense because you are bringing up the topic of the wind to explain why you are getting colder and noting its increasing strength.

So, the correct option is Option 4.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: _________________ what are you doing in my room ?
questions.solution:
The sentence "_ what are you doing in my room?" sounds like someone is a bit surprised or perhaps even slightly annoyed to find someone there. They need a way to grab attention or preface their direct question.

On the other hand: We use this when we're talking about a contrasting point. Like, "This cake is delicious; on the other hand, it's full of sugar." That doesn't quite fit here, does it?

Excuse me: People often say "excuse me" to get someone's attention, to interrupt politely, or even to express a bit of surprise or disbelief about something unexpected. If you walk into your room and find someone there, starting with "Excuse me" is a very common and natural way to lead into the question of why they're there.

Look there: This is used to point something out visually, like "Look there, a rainbow!" It doesn't work as an introduction to a question about someone's presence.

Eventually: This word talks about something happening after some time, like "Eventually, the sun set." That doesn't fit the immediate situation of finding someone in your room.

Looking at these, "Excuse me" feels like the perfect fit! It smoothly introduces the question and reflects that slight surprise or need to get their attention before asking the direct question.

So, the most appropriate discourse marker is Excuse me.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: _________________ an unhappy childhood leads to criminal behaviour.
questions.solution:
Key Points

The phrase "In some cases" is a discourse marker used to indicate that something is true in certain instances or situations, but not necessarily in all or most cases. It conveys a sense of partial truth or occasional occurrence.

The sentence suggests a correlation between an unhappy childhood and criminal behavior, but it's understood that this isn't a universal or absolute rule. "In some cases" perfectly captures this nuance, implying that while it can happen, it's not a definitive outcome for everyone with an unhappy childhood.

Therefore, the correct choice is Option 3.

Complete Sentence: "In some cases an unhappy childhood leads to criminal behaviour."

Additional Information

Option 1: "Still if" is grammatically awkward and doesn't convey the intended meaning of occasional occurrence.

Option 2: "Even somewhat" is also grammatically incorrect in this context and does not function as an appropriate discourse marker here.

Option 4: "In progression" implies a sequence or a development over time. Although an unhappy childhood might be part of a progression, "In progression an unhappy childhood leads…" is not a natural or idiomatic way to frame this statement.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: _________________ the problem is nothing but a wrong assumption about processes.
questions.solution:
The best choice is Option 4: “In other words.”

So the complete sentence reads:

“In other words, the problem is nothing but a wrong assumption about processes.”

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: Many, __________________ hesitate to publicly broach such question for fear of nurturing discrimination.
