Complete sentence:

Many, understandably, hesitate to publicly broach such questions for fear of nurturing discrimination.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: _________________ one notes that in the nineteenth century an important development of reflection and questioning gained momentum.
questions.solution:
The best choice is Option 2: Generally speaking.

“Generally speaking, one notes that in the nineteenth century an important development of reflection and questioning gained momentum.”

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: ____________________ the situation has completely transformed over the past few years.
questions.solution:
The best choice is Option 3: “Thus.”

Complete sentence:

“Thus, the situation has completely transformed over the past few years.”

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: ___________________ the committee is of the opinion that all academic matters will be discussed in the Executive Body Meeting.
questions.solution:
The most appropriate choice is Option 1: Hence.

So the sentence reads:

“Hence, the committee is of the opinion that all academic matters will be discussed in the Executive Body Meeting.”

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: There is more than ___________________ to get the job done.
questions.solution:
The most appropriate option to complete the sentence is "one way".

Here's why:

The structure "more than one" is usually followed by a singular noun, even though it implies plurality (more than one thing). It's a bit of a linguistic quirk, but that's the standard form in English.

Think of it like this:

"More than one person is here." (not "are here")

"More than one book was lost." (not "were lost")

So, following that pattern, it's "more than one way".

Let's look at why the others don't quite fit:

"one ways": This is grammatically incorrect because "one" needs a singular noun ("way").

"any ways" or "any way": While "any" can relate to options, the phrase "more than any way" or "more than any ways" isn't the standard way to express that there are multiple methods. The common idiom uses "more than one".

So, the completed sentence is: "There is more than one way to get the job done."

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: The class decided to organize__________
questions.solution:
When talking about organizing a single event like this for the first time, or just mentioning the act in general, we usually use the indefinite article "a" before the noun.

Think about it like this:

You'd say "I want to buy a book."

You'd say "They decided to build a house."

Similarly, for "picnic," if it's a single, unspecified one, you'd say "organize a picnic."

Using "picnic" alone sounds a bit off for a countable noun like this. "Picnics" would mean they decided to organize more than one. "The picnic" would imply there's a specific picnic everyone already knows about.

So, the most natural fit is Option 2: a picnic.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: The accused ___________________ before the judge.
questions.solution:
The sentence is describing what happens to someone accused of a crime when they are in court with a judge.

"tried" - While the accused is tried, the phrasing "The accused tried before the judge" isn't the standard way to say it in English. It would usually be passive, like "The accused was tried…"

"was trial" - "Trial" is a noun, so this doesn't work grammatically with "was".

"sat trial" - People do sit in court, but "sat trial" isn't a common or correct legal phrase.

"stood trial" - This is a very common and correct legal idiom! "To stand trial" means to undergo a trial in a court of law.

So, the most appropriate option to fill in the blank is stood trial.

The full sentence would be: The accused stood trial before the judge.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: The manner in which the demolitions have been carried out _______________ scrutiny by the HIgh court.
questions.solution:
"The manner in which the demolitions have been carried out ___ scrutiny by the High Court."

We need a phrase that shows the relationship between "The manner" and "scrutiny by the High Court". This relationship seems to be a current state – that the way something was done is presently being examined or is subject to examination by the court.

Let's think about the options:

was under: This would mean the scrutiny happened in the past, and perhaps it's finished now.

under: This word needs a verb before it to make sense in this structure (like "is under" or "was under"). It can't stand alone here.

is under: This means the scrutiny is happening right now. The way the demolitions were done is currently being examined by the High Court. This seems to fit the idea of a present legal situation.

however under: "However" is used to show contrast, and like just "under", it's not the main verb needed here.

Thinking about what the sentence likely means – that the High Court is currently looking into how the demolitions happened – the phrase that best expresses this ongoing state is "is under".

So, the most appropriate option to fill in the blank is Option 3: is under.

The complete sentence would be: "The manner in which the demolitions have been carried out is under scrutiny by the High Court."

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: I ___________________ consider the opinions of all stakeholders before taking a final decision.
questions.solution:
The sentence is: "I _______ consider the opinions of all stakeholders before taking a final decision."

We need a word that shows what you will do regarding the opinions. Let's look at the options:

will not: If you use "will not," the sentence becomes "I will not consider the opinions…", which means you won't think about what the stakeholders think. That changes the whole meaning!

have: If you use "have," the sentence becomes "I have consider the opinions…". This isn't grammatically correct. You'd need "have considered" for the past tense.

will: If you use "will," the sentence becomes "I will consider the opinions…". This is grammatically correct and shows your intention to consider the opinions in the future.

shall: If you use "shall," the sentence becomes "I shall consider the opinions…". This is also grammatically correct and shows your intention to consider the opinions. Often, "shall" with "I" or "we" suggests a strong intention, a promise, or a statement of what is intended to happen. It can sound a bit more formal or determined than "will".

Both "will" and "shall" work grammatically and convey a future action. However, in the context of making a formal decision involving stakeholders, stating "I shall consider…" often implies a stronger sense of determination or commitment to that process.

Given the options and the context, shall (Option 4) feels like the most appropriate choice, suggesting a clear and determined intention to follow this crucial step in the decision-making process.

So, the most appropriate option is shall.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question:
Directions : Given below are sentences that use discourse markers/expressions to make them complete. Identify the most appropriate discourse marker and mark your answer accordingly on the Answer Sheet. 

I can’t be bothered to tell him what I think;______ he would not listen to me.
questions.solution:
The correct discourse marker is Option 2: in any case.

So the sentence becomes:

I can’t be bothered to tell him what I think; in any case he would not listen to me.

Why?

  • In any case means anyway or even if I did, the result would be the same.

  • Here, the speaker says there is no point in telling him because he would not listen.

Why not the others?

  • meanwhile – used for something happening at the same time.

  • in the meantime – used for the period before something else happens.

  • somehow – means in some way, but it does not fit the meaning here.

Answer: Option 2 – in any case

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question:
Directions : Given below are sentences that use discourse markers/expressions to make them complete. Identify the most appropriate discourse marker and mark your answer accordingly on the Answer Sheet. 

______  if the referee points his fingers, this indicates that someone has done something wrong.
questions.solution:
The most appropriate discourse marker is Option 4: As regards.

Complete sentence:

As regards if the referee points his fingers, this indicates that someone has done something wrong.

Explanation:

  • As regards means with reference to or regarding.

  • The sentence is talking about a particular situation involving the referee’s signal.

Why others are incorrect:

  • On the contrary is used to show the opposite of what was said.

  • Whatever more is not suitable here.

  • Briefly means in a short way, which does not fit the sentence.

So, the correct answer is:

$$\text{Option 4: As regards}$$

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question:
Directions : Given below are sentences that use discourse markers/expressions to make them complete. Identify the most appropriate discourse marker and mark your answer accordingly on the Answer Sheet. 

________ have you been to’ the local library yet ?
questions.solution:
The most appropriate discourse marker is:

Option 4: Incidentally

So the complete sentence is:

Incidentally, have you been to the local library yet?

Why?

“Incidentally” is used when we introduce a new point casually during conversation.

The other options do not fit:

  • Sort of – shows vagueness

  • Frankly – means speaking honestly or directly

  • All the same – means nevertheless

Answer: Option 4 — Incidentally

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question:
Directions : Given below are sentences that use discourse markers/expressions to make them complete. Identify the most appropriate discourse marker and mark your answer accordingly on the Answer Sheet. 

________  women are healthier and are living longer than ever before: indeed on an average, they can expect to live five years longer than men.
questions.solution:
The most appropriate discourse marker is:

Option 3: As far as

So the complete sentence is:

As far as women are concerned, they are healthier and are living longer than ever before; indeed, on an average, they can expect to live five years longer than men.

Why?

  • As far as is used to introduce a topic or say something about a particular group.

  • Here, the sentence is specifically talking about women, so “As far as women are concerned” fits naturally.

Why not the others?

  • To a large extent shows degree, not topic introduction.

  • As regards can also introduce a topic, but “As far as women are concerned” is the more natural and commonly used expression here.

  • Turning now is usually used when shifting to a new point in speech or writing, and it does not fit smoothly here.

Answer: Option 3

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question:
Directions : Given below are sentences that use discourse markers/expressions to make them complete. Identify the most appropriate discourse marker and mark your answer accordingly on the Answer Sheet. 

The difference is that I have been fortunate to find a career that I love and, ______, | am getting paid reasonably for it.
questions.solution:
The most appropriate discourse marker is Option 3: what is more.

Complete sentence:

The difference is that I have been fortunate to find a career that I love and, what is more, I am getting paid reasonably for it.

Why?

The phrase “what is more” is used to add another positive point or give extra information.

Here, the speaker says:

  1. They love their career.

  2. In addition, they are getting paid reasonably.

So, “what is more” fits perfectly.

Why the others are incorrect?

  • briefly – means in a short way; does not fit the meaning.

  • broadly speaking – means generally; not suitable here.

  • in conclusion – used at the end of a discussion; not appropriate in this sentence.

Correct Answer:

Option 3

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question:
Directions : Given below are sentences that use discourse markers/expressions to make them complete. Identify the most appropriate discourse marker and mark your answer accordingly on the Answer Sheet. 

________ the Government of India has followed the policy of trying to improve relations with its neighbours.
questions.solution:
The most appropriate discourse marker is:

Option 4: In general

So the complete sentence is:

In general, the Government of India has followed the policy of trying to improve relations with its neighbours.

Why?

  • In general is used to talk about something that is true in most cases or broadly.

  • The sentence is making a broad statement about the policy of the Government of India.

Why the others are incorrect?

  • Particular → incorrect usage here; it should be in particular if used as a discourse marker.

  • Knowingly → means deliberately, which does not fit the meaning.

  • Otherwise → shows an alternative or consequence, so it does not fit here.

Answer: Option 4 — In general

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question:
Directions : Given below are sentences that use discourse markers/expressions to make them complete. Identify the most appropriate discourse marker and mark your answer accordingly on the Answer Sheet. 

________ this issue is likely to be discussed when the Council meets next.
questions.solution:
The most appropriate discourse marker is Option 1: As per reports.

So the complete sentence is:

As per reports, this issue is likely to be discussed when the Council meets next.

Reason:

  • As per reports means according to reports, which fits the meaning of the sentence.

  • Accordingly shows result or consequence.

  • Likewise shows similarity.

  • Briefly means in a short manner.

Hence, the correct answer is Option 1.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question:
Directions : Given below are sentences that use discourse markers/expressions to make them complete. Identify the most appropriate discourse marker and mark your answer accordingly on the Answer Sheet. 

________ you needed to consult a physician to have a clear idea of what the disease is about.
questions.solution:
The most appropriate discourse marker is Option 1: In that case.

Complete sentence:

In that case, you needed to consult a physician to have a clear idea of what the disease is about.

Why?

  • In that case is used when referring to a particular situation or condition.

  • The other options do not fit the meaning:

  • In these case → grammatically incorrect; it should be “in these cases”

  • Likewise → means “similarly”

  • Henceforth → means “from now on”

Answer: Option 1 — In that case

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question:
Directions : Given below are sentences that use discourse markers/expressions to make them complete. Identify the most appropriate discourse marker and mark your answer accordingly on the Answer Sheet. 

________ in the year-ago period the current account deficit stood at $1-3 billion or 0-2% of the GDP.
questions.solution:
The most appropriate discourse marker is Notably.

So, the correct sentence is:

Notably, in the year-ago period the current account deficit stood at $1.3$ billion or $0.2\%$ of the GDP.

Correct Answer:

Option 2 — Notably

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question:
Directions : Given below are sentences that use discourse markers/expressions to make them complete. Identify the most appropriate discourse marker and mark your answer accordingly on the Answer Sheet. 

________ the records have been completely undermined by shoddy book-keeping.
questions.solution:
The most appropriate discourse marker is Option 2: As such.

Complete sentence:

As such, the records have been completely undermined by shoddy book-keeping.

Why?

  • As such means therefore / in that way / consequently, and it fits naturally at the beginning of this sentence.

  • Because of needs a noun phrase after it, so it cannot stand alone here.

  • Forever does not make sense in this context.

  • Hence usually shows a result, but As such is more suitable and natural here.

Answer: Option 2 — As such

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question:
Directions : Each of the following sentences in this section has a blank space, followed by four options. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank and mark your response on the Answer Sheet accordingly. 

It does not look like he is going ________ in his studies than what was predicted by his school principal.
questions.solution:
The most appropriate option is Option 1: any further.

Sentence:

It does not look like he is going $ \text{any further} $ in his studies than what was predicted by his school principal.

Reason:

  • Further is used for progress, degree, or advancement, especially in abstract things like studies.

  • Farther is generally used for physical distance.

  • In a negative sentence like does not look like he is going…, any further is the natural expression.

So, the correct answer is:

$$\boxed{\text{any further}}$$

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question:
Directions : Each of the following sentences in this section has a blank space, followed by four options. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank and mark your response on the Answer Sheet accordingly. 

________ all the confusion the thief made a quiet getaway.
questions.solution:
The correct option is Option 4: Amid.

Sentence:

Amid all the confusion, the thief made a quiet getaway.

Explanation:

  • Amid means in the middle of or surrounded by.

  • Here, the sentence means that while there was confusion all around, the thief escaped quietly.

Why not the others?

  • Among / Amongst are generally used for people or things in a group, not for a situation like confusion.

  • Amidst is also grammatically correct, but amid is the more standard and natural choice here.

Answer: Amid

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question:
Directions : Each of the following sentences in this section has a blank space, followed by four options. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank and mark your response on the Answer Sheet accordingly. 

The intricate operation on the patient required the expertise of a/an ______ surgeon.
questions.solution:
The correct option is Option 3: specialist.

Correct sentence:

The intricate operation on the patient required the expertise of a/an specialist surgeon.

But in proper English usage, we say:

The intricate operation on the patient required the expertise of a specialist surgeon.

Reason:

  • special = unusual or important

  • especial = used for emphasis, meaning particularly

  • specialist = a person who is an expert in a particular field

  • specialised = trained for a particular purpose, but it does not fit best here before surgeon

Since the sentence talks about the expertise of an expert surgeon, specialist is the most appropriate word.

There are ______ thirty students inside the classroom at any time.
questions.solution:
The correct option is Option 2: fewer than.

So the sentence becomes:

There are fewer than thirty students inside the classroom at any time.

Step by step:

  • Students are countable noun.

  • For countable nouns, we use fewer than.

  • Less than is generally used with uncountable nouns.

  • Then is incorrect here; the correct word is than.

Hence, the correct answer is fewer than.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question: The sum of the first k terms of a series S is 3k2+5k. Which one of the following is correct?
options: The terms of S form an arithmetic progression with common difference 14.
The terms of S form an arithmetic progression with common difference 6.
The terms of S form a geometric progression with common ratio 10/7.
The terms of S form a geometric progression with common ratio 11/4.
questions.solution:
1. Given the sum of the first $k$ terms of the series $S$ as

    $$S_k \;=\;3k^2 \;+\;5k.$$

  2. The $k$-th term $t_k$ is

    $$t_k \;=\;S_k \;-\;S_{\,k-1}.$$

  3. Compute $S_{\,k-1}$:

    $ S_{\,k-1} =3(k-1)^2+5(k-1) =3\bigl(k^2-2k+1\bigr)+5k-5 =3k^2-6k+3+5k-5 =3k^2 - k -2. $

  4. Hence

    $ t_k =(3k^2+5k)\;-\;(3k^2 - k -2) =3k^2+5k-3k^2 +k +2 =6k +2. $

  5. The difference between consecutive terms is

    $ t_{k+1}-t_k =[6(k+1)+2] - (6k+2) =6. $

  6. Therefore the terms form an arithmetic progression with common difference $6$.

Answer: Option B.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question: The sum of the first 8 terms of a GP is five times the sum of its first 4 terms. If r≠1 is the common ratio, then what is the number of possible real values of r?
questions.solution:
Step 1: Understanding the Problem

The question says the sum of the first 8 terms in a geometric progression (GP) is equal to 5 times the sum of its first 4 terms.

Step 2: Using the GP Sum Formula

The sum of the first $ n $ terms of a GP is: $ S_n = a \frac{1 - r^n}{1 - r} $ where $ a $ is the first term and $ r $ is the common ratio (with $ r \neq 1 $).

Step 3: Setting Up the Equation

The sum of 8 terms is: $ S_8 = a \frac{1 - r^8}{1 - r} $ and the sum of 4 terms is: $ S_4 = a \frac{1 - r^4}{1 - r} $

The question says $ S_8 = 5 \times S_4 $, so: $ a \frac{1 - r^8}{1 - r} = 5 \times a \frac{1 - r^4}{1 - r} $

Step 4: Simplifying the Equation

We can divide both sides by $ a $ (assuming $ a \neq 0 $), and also cancel $ 1 - r $ (as $ r \neq 1 $): $ \frac{1 - r^8}{1 - r^4} = 5 $

Step 5: Rearranging for a Quadratic Equation

Multiply both sides by $ 1 - r^4 $ to remove the fraction: $ 1 - r^8 = 5(1 - r^4) $

Expand and rearrange: $ 1 - r^8 = 5 - 5r^4 $

$ -r^8 + 5r^4 - 4 = 0 $ or $ r^8 - 5r^4 + 4 = 0 $

Step 6: Letting $ x = r^4 $

Let $ x = r^4 $, turn the equation into: $ x^2 - 5x + 4 = 0 $

Step 7: Solving the Quadratic

Factor or use the quadratic formula to get: $ x = 4 $ or $ x = 1 $

Step 8: Solving for $ r $

Since $ x = r^4 $, set $ r^4 = 4 $ or $ r^4 = 1 $.

So, $ r^4 = 4 \Rightarrow r = \pm\sqrt{2} $

$ r^4 = 1 \Rightarrow r = \pm 1 $

Step 9: Considering Valid Values

The question says $ r \neq 1 $, so ignore $ r = 1 $. But $ r = -1 $ is allowed.

The possible real values of $ r $ are $ \sqrt{2} $, $ -\sqrt{2} $, and $ -1 $.

Step 10: Final Answer

So there are three possible real values for $ r $: $ \sqrt{2},\ -\sqrt{2}, -1 $.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question: If 5th, 7th and 13th terms of an AP are in GP, then what is the ratio of its first term to its common difference?
questions.solution:
1. Let the first term be $a$ and the common difference be $d$.

    Then

    t₅ = $a + 4d$,

    t₇ = $a + 6d$,

    t₁₃ = $a + 12d$.

  2. Since t₅, t₇, t₁₃ are in GP,

    $$(\text{middle term})^2 = (\text{first term})\times(\text{third term})$$

    $$\bigl(a + 6d\bigr)^2 = \bigl(a + 4d\bigr)\bigl(a + 12d\bigr).$$

  3. Expand both sides:

    Left: $a^2 + 12ad + 36d^2$.

    Right: $a^2 + 16ad + 48d^2$.

  4. Equate and simplify:

    $$a^2 + 12ad + 36d^2 = a^2 + 16ad + 48d^2$$

    $$12ad + 36d^2 = 16ad + 48d^2$$

    $$0 = 4ad + 12d^2 = 4d(a + 3d).$$

  5. Since $d\neq0$, we get $a + 3d = 0\implies a = -3d$.

    Therefore,

    $$\frac{a}{d} = -3.$$

Answer: Option A (-3).

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question:
If p, 1, q are in AP and p, 2, q are in GP, then which of the following statements is/are correct?

I. p, 4, q are in HP.

II. (1/p), (1/4), (1/q) are in AP.

Select the answer using the code given below.
questions.solution:
We are given:

  • $p, 1, q$ are in A.P.

  • $p, 2, q$ are in G.P.

We need to check whether statements I and II are correct.

Step 1: Condition for A.P.

For three numbers in A.P., the middle term is the average of the other two:

$ 1 = \frac{p + q}{2} $

$ p + q = 2 \quad ...(1) $

Step 2: Condition for G.P.

For three numbers in G.P., the square of the middle term is the product of the other two:

$ 2^2 = p \cdot q $

$ pq = 4 \quad ...(2) $

Step 3: Solve equations (1) and (2)

We know:

$ p+q = 2, \quad pq = 4 $

This represents a quadratic equation in $x$:

$ x^2 - (p+q)x + pq = 0 $

$ x^2 - 2x + 4 = 0 $

Discriminant $= (-2)^2 - 4(1)(4) = 4 - 16 = -12 < 0$

This means $p$ and $q$ are non-real complex numbers.

So, the situation is consistent only for complex $p, q$.

Step 4: Check Statement I

We need to see if $p, 4, q$ are in H.P.

Condition for H.P.: The reciprocals $\dfrac{1}{p}, \dfrac{1}{4}, \dfrac{1}{q}$ must be in A.P.

That means:

$ \frac{2}{4} = \frac{1}{p} + \frac{1}{q} $

$ \frac{1}{2} = \frac{p+q}{pq} $

From (1) and (2):

$ \frac{p+q}{pq} = \frac{2}{4} = \frac{1}{2} $

So true.

Hence, Statement I is correct.

Step 5: Check Statement II

We need to see if $\dfrac{1}{p}, \dfrac{1}{4}, \dfrac{1}{q}$ are in A.P.

Condition: middle term is the average of other two.

$ \frac{1}{4} \stackrel{?}{=} \frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{1}{p} + \frac{1}{q}\right) $

$ \frac{1}{4} \stackrel{?}{=} \frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{p+q}{pq} $

From (1), (2):

$ \frac{p+q}{pq} = \frac{2}{4} = \frac{1}{2} $

So RHS $= \frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{1}{2} = \frac{1}{4}$

Thus, equality holds.

Hence, Statement II is also correct.

Final Answer:

Both statements are correct.

$ \boxed{\text{Option C: Both I and II}} $

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14th September 2025
questions.question: What is the relation between m and n?

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14th September 2025
questions.question: How many values of m are possible?
questions.solution:
The left side is an arithmetic progression:

$$6,\;10,\;14,\;\dots$$

Here,

  • first term $a = 6$

  • common difference $d = 4$

  • number of terms $= m$

So its sum is

$$S_m = \frac{m}{2}\left[2a + (m-1)d\right]$$

$$S_m = \frac{m}{2}\left[12 + 4(m-1)\right]$$

$$S_m = \frac{m}{2}(12 + 4m - 4)$$

$$S_m = \frac{m}{2}(4m+8)$$

$$S_m = 2m(m+2)$$

Now the right side is

$$1 + 3 + 5 + 7 + \dots \text{ up to } n \text{ terms}$$

This is the sum of first $n$ odd numbers, which is

$$n^2$$

So given,

$$2m(m+2) = n^2$$

That is,

$$2m^2 + 4m = n^2$$

We need $m < 25$, $n < 25$.

So we test values of $m$ and check when $2m(m+2)$ is a perfect square.

$$n^2 = 2m(m+2)$$

Let us try small values of $m$:

  • $m=1$: $2(1)(3)=6$, not a square

  • $m=2$: $2(2)(4)=16 = 4^2$

  • $m=3$: $2(3)(5)=30$, not a square

  • $m=4$: $2(4)(6)=48$, not a square

  • $m=5$: $2(5)(7)=70$, not a square

  • $m=6$: $2(6)(8)=96$, not a square

  • $m=7$: $2(7)(9)=126$, not a square

  • $m=8$: $2(8)(10)=160$, not a square

  • $m=9$: $2(9)(11)=198$, not a square

  • $m=10$: $2(10)(12)=240$, not a square

  • $m=11$: $2(11)(13)=286$, not a square

  • $m=12$: $2(12)(14)=336$, not a square

Since $n<25$, we must have

$$n^2 < 25^2 = 625$$

So,

$$2m(m+2) < 625$$

Checking further:

  • $m=13$: $390$, not a square

  • $m=14$: $448$, not a square

  • $m=15$: $510$, not a square

  • $m=16$: $576 = 24^2$

This gives $n=24$, valid since $n<25$.

Now for $m=17$,

$$2(17)(19)=646 > 625$$

So no larger $m$ can work.

Hence possible values of $m$ are:

$$m=2,\;16$$

So the number of possible values of $m$ is:

$$2$$

Therefore, the correct option is:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 3: Two}}$$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14th September 2025
questions.question: What is the sum of the binary numbers $(10110110) _2$ and $( 10001 1) _2$
questions.solution:
First, convert $ (10110110)_2 $ into decimal:

$ (10110110)_2 = 1 \times 2^7 + 0 \times 2^6 + 1 \times 2^5 + 1 \times 2^4 + 0 \times 2^3 + 1 \times 2^2 + 1 \times 2^1 + 0 \times 2^0 $

$ = 128 + 0 + 32 + 16 + 0 + 4 + 2 + 0 = 182 $

Now convert $ (100011)_2 $ to decimal:

$ (100011)_2 = 1 \times 2^5 + 0 \times 2^4 + 0 \times 2^3 + 0 \times 2^2 + 1 \times 2^1 + 1 \times 2^0 $

$ = 32 + 0 + 0 + 0 + 2 + 1 = 35 $

Next, add the decimal values:

$ 182 + 35 = 217 $

Then convert the result back to binary.

To change $ 217 $ into binary, divide it repeatedly by 2 and record the remainders:

$ 217 \div 2 = 108 \, \text{remainder} \, 1 $

$ 108 \div 2 = 54 \, \text{remainder} \, 0 $

$ 54 \div 2 = 27 \, \text{remainder} \, 0 $

$ 27 \div 2 = 13 \, \text{remainder} \, 1 $

$ 13 \div 2 = 6 \, \text{remainder} \, 1 $

$ 6 \div 2 = 3 \, \text{remainder} \, 0 $

$ 3 \div 2 = 1 \, \text{remainder} \, 1 $

$ 1 \div 2 = 0 \, \text{remainder} \, 1 $

Reading the remainders from bottom to top gives $ 217_{10} = (11011001)_2 $.

So, the sum of $ (10110110)_2 $ and $ (100011)_2 $ is $ (11011001)_2 $.

Therefore, the correct option is 1.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14th September 2025
questions.question: What is the harmonic mean of the numbers C(10,3) C(10,4) C(10,5) C(10,6) and C(10,7) ?
questions.solution:
For the numbers

$$\binom{10}{3},\ \binom{10}{4},\ \binom{10}{5},\ \binom{10}{6},\ \binom{10}{7},$$

the harmonic mean is

$$ H=\frac{n}{\sum \frac{1}{x_i}} $$

Here, $n=5$.

First find the values:

$$ \binom{10}{3}=120,\quad \binom{10}{4}=210,\quad \binom{10}{5}=252 $$

Using symmetry of binomial coefficients,

$$ \binom{10}{6}=\binom{10}{4}=210,\quad \binom{10}{7}=\binom{10}{3}=120 $$

So the numbers are

$$ 120,\ 210,\ 252,\ 210,\ 120 $$

Now,

$$ \frac{1}{120}+\frac{1}{210}+\frac{1}{252}+\frac{1}{210}+\frac{1}{120} = 2\left(\frac{1}{120}\right)+2\left(\frac{1}{210}\right)+\frac{1}{252} $$

Take LCM $=2520$:

$$ \frac{1}{120}=\frac{21}{2520},\quad \frac{1}{210}=\frac{12}{2520},\quad \frac{1}{252}=\frac{10}{2520} $$

Therefore,

$$ 2\cdot\frac{21}{2520}+2\cdot\frac{12}{2520}+\frac{10}{2520} = \frac{42+24+10}{2520} = \frac{76}{2520} = \frac{19}{630} $$

Hence,

$$ H=\frac{5}{19/630} =5\cdot\frac{630}{19} =\frac{3150}{19} $$

So the correct answer is

$$ \boxed{\frac{3150}{19}} $$

Thus, the correct option is Option 1.

questions.title: BITSAT 2025
questions.question: The coefficient of $x^n$ in the expansion of $\frac{1-a x-x^2}{e^x}$ is
questions.solution:
Taylor series expansion of $e^{-x}$ is

$$ e^{-x}=1-\frac{x}{1!}+\frac{x^2}{2!}-\frac{x^3}{3!}+\ldots=\sum_{k=0}^{\infty} \frac{(-1)^k x^k}{k!} $$

Now, $\frac{1-a x-x^2}{e^x}=\left(1-a x-x^2\right) e^{-x}$

                           $$ =e^{-x}-(a x) e^{-x}-\left(x^2\right) e^{-x} $$

From $e^{-x}$, the coefficient of $x^n$ in $e^{-x}$ is $\frac{(-1)^n}{n!}$

From $-a x e^{-x}$, the coefficient of $x^n$ in $-a x e^{-x}$ is given by the coefficient of $x^{n-1}$ in $e^{-x}$, multiplied by $-a$

$$ -a \cdot \frac{(-1)^{n-1}}{(n-1)!} $$

From $-x^2 e^{-x}$, the coefficient of $x^n$ in $-x^2 e^{-x}$ is given by the coefficient of $x^{n-2}$ in $e^{-x}$, multiplied by -1 :

$$ \frac{-(-1)^{n-2}}{(n-2)!} $$

Now, combine the coefficient from all three terms

$$ \begin{aligned} & \text { coefficient of } x^n=\frac{(-1)^n}{n!}-a \frac{(-1)^{n-1}}{(n-1)!}-\frac{(-1)^{n-2}}{(n-2)} \\ & =(-1)^{n-2}\left[\frac{(-1)^2}{n!}-a \cdot \frac{(-1)}{(n-1)!}-1 \cdot \frac{1}{(n-2)!}\right] \\ & =(-1)^{n-2}\left[\frac{1}{n!}+\frac{a}{(n-1)!}-\frac{1}{(n-2)!}\right] \\ & =\frac{(-1)^n}{n!}\left[1+(a+1) n-n^2\right] \end{aligned} $$

questions.question: If $a, b, c, d$ be four positive unequal quantities and $s=a+b+c+d$, then $(s-a)(s-b)(s-c) (s-d)>k a b c d$. Then, value of $k$ is
questions.solution:
Using $A M>G M$, we have

$$ \begin{aligned} & \frac{a+b+c}{3}>(a b c)^{1 / 3} \\ \Rightarrow & a+b+c>3(a b c)^{1 / 3} \Rightarrow(s-d)>3(a b c)^{1 / 3} \end{aligned} $$

Similarly, $(s-a)>3(b c d)^{1 / 3},(s-b)>3(a c d)^{1 / 3}$ and

$$ \begin{aligned} & (s-c)>3(a b d)^{1 / 3} \\ & \therefore(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)(s-d)> \\ & \quad 3^4(a b c)^{\frac{1}{3}}(b c d)^{\frac{1}{3}}(a c d)^{\frac{1}{3}}(a b d)^{\frac{1}{3}} \\ & \quad=81 a b c d \end{aligned} $$

Thus, $k=81$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question:
Consider the following statements:
I. f is one-one function.
II. f is onto function if the codomain is the set of natural numbers.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Given:

$$ f = \{(1, 1), (2, 4), (3, 7), (4, 10)\} $$

This means:

$f(1) = 1$

$f(2) = 4$

$f(3) = 7$

$f(4) = 10$

Statement I: f is one-one function.

A function is one-one (or injective) if no two different elements in the domain map to the same output (image).

Let's check:

$f(1) = 1$

$f(2) = 4$

$f(3) = 7$

$f(4) = 10$

Each input gives a different output. No two inputs have the same output.

So, statement I is correct.

Statement II: f is onto function if the codomain is the set of natural numbers.

A function is onto (surjective) if every element of the codomain has at least one pre-image in the domain.

Here, the codomain is the set of natural numbers (i.e., $\mathbb{N} = \{1, 2, 3, \ldots\}$).

The outputs of $f$ are $\{1, 4, 7, 10\}$.

Are all natural numbers covered by $f$?

No. For example, $2$ is not an output value, nor is $5$ or $6$, etc.

Thus, not every natural number is an output of $f$.

So, statement II is incorrect.

Summary:

Statement I is correct.

Statement II is not correct.

The correct answer is:

Option A: I only

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14th September 2025
questions.question: In a class of 45 students, 34 like to play cricket and 26 like to play football. Further, each student likes to play at least one of the two games. How many students like to play exactly one game?
questions.solution:
Let:

  • Number of students who like cricket $= 34$

  • Number of students who like football $= 26$

  • Total number of students $= 45$

Since each student likes at least one of the two games,

$$ n(C \cup F) = 45 $$

Using the formula,

$$ n(C \cup F) = n(C) + n(F) - n(C \cap F) $$

So,

$$ 45 = 34 + 26 - n(C \cap F) $$

$$ 45 = 60 - n(C \cap F) $$

$$ n(C \cap F) = 15 $$

Now, students who like exactly one game

$$ = \text{only cricket} + \text{only football} $$

$$ = (34 - 15) + (26 - 15) $$

$$ = 19 + 11 $$

$$ = 30 $$

So, the correct answer is:

$$ \boxed{30} $$

Option 2

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14th September 2025
questions.question: Set X contains 3n elements and set Y contains 2n elements, and they have n elements in common. How many elements does (X - Y) × (Y-X) have?
questions.solution:
We use the formula for set difference:

$$ n(X - Y) = n(X) - n(X \cap Y) $$

Given:

$$ n(X) = 3n,\quad n(Y) = 2n,\quad n(X \cap Y) = n $$

So,

$$ n(X - Y) = 3n - n = 2n $$

Similarly,

$$ n(Y - X) = 2n - n = n $$

Now, the number of elements in the Cartesian product $(X-Y)\times(Y-X)$ is:

$$ n\big((X-Y)\times(Y-X)\big) = n(X-Y)\cdot n(Y-X) $$

$$ = 2n \cdot n = 2n^2 $$

So the correct answer is:

$$ \boxed{2n^2} $$

Option 4.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14th September 2025
questions.question: Let A = {- 3, - 2, - 1, 0, 1, 2, 3} and B = {0, 1, 4, 9} How many elements does the subset of AB corresponding to the relation R = {(x, y) : |x| < y} have, where x $\in$ A and y $\in$ B?
questions.solution:
We have

$$ A = \{-3,-2,-1,0,1,2,3\}, \qquad B = \{0,1,4,9\} $$

The relation is

$$ R = \{(x,y): |x| < y,\; x \in A,\; y \in B\} $$

We have to count how many ordered pairs $(x,y)$ satisfy $|x|

First, find possible values of $|x|$ for $x \in A$:

  • if $x = 0$, then $|x|=0$

  • if $x = \pm 1$, then $|x|=1$

  • if $x = \pm 2$, then $|x|=2$

  • if $x = \pm 3$, then $|x|=3$

Now check each value of $y \in B$.

For $y=0$

Condition:

$$ |x|<0 $$

This is not possible, since $|x| \ge 0$ always.

So, number of pairs $=0$.

For $y=1$

Condition:

$$ |x|<1 $$

This is true only for

$$ x=0 $$

So, number of pairs $=1$.

For $y=4$

Condition:

$$ |x|<4 $$

All elements of $A$ satisfy this, because maximum value of $|x|$ is $3$.

So, number of pairs $=7$.

For $y=9$

Condition:

$$ |x|<9 $$

Again, all elements of $A$ satisfy this.

So, number of pairs $=7$.

Now total number of ordered pairs is

$$ 0+1+7+7=15 $$

Hence, the required number of elements is

$$ \boxed{15} $$

So, the correct option is:

$$ \boxed{\text{Option 3}} $$

questions.title: BITSAT 2025
questions.question: The frequency of oscillation of the spring mass system is
questions.solution:
$$ \begin{aligned} &\text { For the given spring mass system, }\\ &k_{\mathrm{eq}}=k+2 k+k=4 k \end{aligned} $$

$$ \text { ∴ Frequency, } \eta=\frac{1}{2 \pi} \sqrt{\frac{k_{\text {eq }}}{m}}=\frac{1}{2 \pi} \sqrt{\frac{4 k}{m}} $$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2025 Paper 1 Online
questions.question: A person sitting inside an elevator performs a weighing experiment with an object of mass 50 kg . Suppose that the variation of the height $y$ (in m ) of the elevator, from the ground, with time $t$ (in s) is given by $y=8\left[1+\sin \left(\frac{2 \pi t}{T}\right)\right]$, where $T=40 \pi \mathrm{~s}$. Taking acceleration due to gravity, $g=10$ $\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}^2$, the maximum variation of the object's weight (in N ) as observed in the experiment is ___________.
questions.solution:
Here, we will use the formula for apparent weight in elevator as the elevator has some acceleration.

The normal force (apparent weight) adjusts for the elevations acceleration

relative to gravity.

Apparent weight: $w=m(g+a)$, where $a$ is the elevator's acceleration.

Here, elevator's position, $y$ is given so we can find elevator's acceleration using

$$ a=\frac{d^2 y}{d t^2} $$

Given, $m=50 \mathrm{~kg}$

$$ \begin{aligned} & \text { and } y=8\left[1+\sin \left(\frac{2 \pi t}{T}\right)\right] \\ & T=40 \pi \mathrm{~s} . \\ & \Rightarrow y=8(1+\sin (\omega t)) \quad\left(as, \omega=\frac{2 \pi}{T}\right) \\ & \omega=\frac{2 \pi}{40 \pi}=\frac{1}{20} \\ & a=\frac{d^2 y}{d t^2}=-8 \omega^2 \sin (\omega t) \\ & \left|a_{\text {max }}\right|=8 \omega^2=8\left(\frac{1}{20}\right)^2=8 \times \frac{1}{400} \\ & \left|a_{\text {max }}\right|=\frac{1}{50} \end{aligned} $$

$W_{\text {max }}=m\left(g+a_{\text {max }}\right)$ (when lift goes up)

and. $W_{\text {min }}=m\left(g-a_{\text {max }}\right)$ (when lift goes down)

so

$$ \begin{aligned} \Delta w & =w_{\text {max }}-w_{\text {min }}=2 m a_{\text {max }} \\ & =2 \times 50 \times \frac{1}{50} \Rightarrow \Delta w=2 \mathrm{~N} \end{aligned} $$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2025 Paper 2 Online
questions.question: As shown in the figures, a uniform rod OO' of length l is hinged at the point O and held in place vertically between two walls using two massless springs of same spring constant. The springs are connected at the midpoint and at the top-end (O') of the rod, as shown in Fig. 1 and the rod is made to oscillate by a small angular displacement. The frequency of oscillation of the rod is f₁. On the other hand, if both the springs are connected at the midpoint of the rod, as shown in Fig. 2 and the rod is made to oscillate by a small angular displacement, then the frequency of oscillation is f₂. Ignoring gravity and assuming motion only in the plane of the diagram, the value of $ \frac{f_1}{f_2} $ is:
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{\mathrm{ML}^2}{3} \ddot{\theta}+\mathrm{K} \cdot \frac{\ell}{2} \theta \cdot \frac{\ell}{2}+\mathrm{K} \cdot \ell \cdot \theta \cdot \ell=0 \\ & \ddot{\theta}+\frac{15}{4} \frac{\mathrm{k}}{\mathrm{M}} \theta=0 \\ & \ddot{\theta}=-\left(\frac{15}{4} \frac{\mathrm{k}}{\mathrm{M}}\right) \theta \\ & \omega_1=\sqrt{\frac{15 \mathrm{~K}}{4 \mathrm{M}}}\end{aligned}$

$\frac{1}{3} \mathrm{ML}^2 \ddot{\theta}+2 \mathrm{~K} \frac{\mathrm{~L}}{2} \theta \cdot \frac{\mathrm{~L}}{2}=0$

$\begin{aligned} & \ddot{\theta}+\frac{3}{2} \frac{K}{M} \theta=0 \\ & \ddot{\theta}=-\frac{3}{2} \frac{K}{M} \theta \\ & \omega_2=\sqrt{\frac{3}{2} \frac{K}{M}} \\ & \frac{\omega_1}{\omega_2}=\sqrt{\frac{15}{4} \times \frac{2}{3}}=\sqrt{\frac{5}{2}}\end{aligned}$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2025 Paper 2 Online
questions.question:
The density (in $\mathrm{g} \mathrm{cm}^{-3}$ ) of the metal which forms a cubic close packed (ccp) lattice with an axial distance (edge length) equal to 400 pm is ___________.

Use: Atomic mass of metal $=105.6 \mathrm{amu}$ and Avogadro's constant $=6 \times 10^{23} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$
questions.solution:
• In a ccp (fcc) unit cell, number of atoms per cell, n = 4

• Edge length

$$a=400\text{ pm}=400\times10^{-12}\text{ m}=4.0\times10^{-8}\text{ cm}$$

• Volume of cell

$$V=a^3=(4.0\times10^{-8}\text{ cm})^3=6.4\times10^{-23}\text{ cm}^3$$

• Mass of one atom

$$m_{\rm atom}=\frac{M}{N_A}=\frac{105.6\text{ g/mol}}{6.0\times10^{23}\text{ mol}^{-1}} =1.76\times10^{-22}\text{ g}$$

• Mass of unit cell

$$m_{\rm cell}=n\;m_{\rm atom}=4\times1.76\times10^{-22}=7.04\times10^{-22}\text{ g}$$

• Density

$$\rho=\frac{m_{\rm cell}}{V} =\frac{7.04\times10^{-22}}{6.4\times10^{-23}} \approx11\;\text{g/cm}^3$$

Answer: 11 g·cm⁻³.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2025 Paper 2 Online
questions.question:
At 300 K , an ideal dilute solution of a macromolecule exerts osmotic pressure that is expressed in terms of the height $(h)$ of the solution (density $=1.00 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~cm}^{-3}$ ) where $h$ is equal to 2.00 cm . If the concentration of the dilute solution of the macromolecule is $2.00 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{dm}^{-3}$, the molar mass of the macromolecule is calculated to be $\boldsymbol{X} \times 10^4 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$. The value of $\boldsymbol{X}$ is __________.

Use: Universal gas constant $(R)=8.3 \mathrm{~J} \mathrm{~K}^{-1} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ and acceleration due to gravity $(g)=10 \mathrm{~m} \mathrm{~s}^{-2}$
questions.solution:
Density of the solution:

$ \text{Density} = 1.00 \, \text{g cm}^{-3} = 1000 \, \text{kg m}^{-3} $

Height of the solution column ($h$):

$ h = 2 \, \text{cm} = 2 \times 10^{-2} \, \text{m} $

Acceleration due to gravity ($g$):

$ g = 10 \, \text{m s}^{-2} $

Calculating osmotic pressure ($\pi$):

Osmotic pressure is given by the equation:

$ \pi = h \cdot \rho \cdot g $

Substituting the known values:

$ \pi = 2 \times 10^{-2} \times 1000 \times 10 = 200 \, \text{N m}^{-2} $

Relating osmotic pressure to concentration and molar mass:

The equation for osmotic pressure in terms of concentration and molar mass is:

$ \pi = cRT $

where $c = \frac{2000}{M}$ as the concentration in g/dm$^3$ needs to be converted to mol/dm$^3$ by dividing by the molar mass $M$. Incorporating the given temperature and universal gas constant, we have:

$ 200 = \left(\frac{2000}{M}\right) \cdot 8.3 \cdot 300 $

Solving for the molar mass $M$:

Rearrange and solve the equation to find $M$:

$ M = 24900 \, \text{g mol}^{-1} = 2.49 \times 10^4 \, \text{g mol}^{-1} $

Thus, the value of $X$ is $2.49$.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2025 Paper 1 Online
questions.question:
Consider the following frequency distribution:

Value458961211
Frequency5$f_1$$f_2$2113

Suppose that the sum of the frequencies is 19 and the median of this frequency distribution is 6.

For the given frequency distribution, let $\alpha$ denote the mean deviation about the mean, $\beta$ denote the mean deviation about the median, and $\sigma^2$ denote the variance.

Match each entry in List-I to the correct entry in List-II and choose the correct option.

List – I List – II
(P) 7 f1 + 9 f2 is equal to (1) 146
(Q) 19 α is equal to (2) 47
(R) 19 β is equal to (3) 48
(S) 19 σ2 is equal to (4) 145
(5) 55
questions.solution:
Given $12+f_1+f_2=19$

Median = 6

$\Rightarrow \quad f_1+f_2=7$

Median $=\left(\frac{N+1}{2}\right)^{\text {th }}$ term $=\left(\frac{19+1}{2}\right)^{\text {th }}$ term $=10^{\text {th }}$ term

$$ \begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad 6+f_1=10 \Rightarrow f_1=4 \text { and } f_2=3 \\ & \therefore \quad 7 f_1+9 f_2=7 \times 4+9 \times 3 \\ & =55 \Rightarrow P \rightarrow 5 \end{aligned} $$

Mean $(\bar{X})=\frac{\Sigma f_i x_i}{\Sigma f_i}=\frac{133}{19}=7$

$$ \begin{aligned} & \text { Mean deviation about mean }(\alpha)=\frac{\Sigma f_i\left|x_i-\bar{x}\right|}{\Sigma f_i} \\ & =\frac{5(3)+4(2)+1(1)+3(1)+2(2)+3(4)+1(5)}{19}=\frac{48}{19} \\ & \Rightarrow 19 \alpha=48 \quad Q \rightarrow 3 \end{aligned} $$

Mean deviation about median $(\beta)$

$$ \begin{aligned} & =\frac{\Sigma f_i\left|x_i-M\right|}{\Sigma f_i} \\ & =\frac{5(2)+4(1)+1(0)+3(2)+2(3)+3(5)+1(6)}{19} \\ & =\frac{47}{19} \\ & 19 \beta=47 \quad R \rightarrow 2 \end{aligned} $$

$\begin{aligned} & \text { Variance }\left(\sigma^2\right)=\frac{\Sigma f_i f_i^2}{\Sigma f_i}-(\bar{X})^2 \\ & =\frac{5 \times 16+4 \times 25+1 \times 36+3 \times 64+2 \times 81+3 \times 121+1 \times 144}{19}-(7)^2 \\ & =\frac{1077}{19}-49=\frac{146}{19} \\ & \Rightarrow 19 \sigma^2=146 \\ & S \rightarrow 1 \\ & P \rightarrow 5, Q \rightarrow 3, R \rightarrow 2, S \rightarrow 1\end{aligned}$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question: What is the total number of students whose height is less than or equal to 165 cm?
questions.solution:
To find out how many students have a height of 165 cm or less, we need to add up the numbers from certain height categories.

First, look at the height group 160-162 cm. There are 12 students in this group.

Next, check the 162-164 cm category. This group has 15 students.

After that, add the students from the 164-166 cm group. This group includes 24 students.

Now, add these numbers together to get the total number of students: 12 (from 160-162 cm) + 15 (from 162-164 cm) + 24 (from 164-166 cm).

The sum is 12 + 15 + 24 = 51.

Thus, the correct option is Option C: 51.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question: What is the height of the class?
questions.solution:
Total number of students

$$N=12+15+24+13=64$$

Since $N$ is even,

$$\frac{N}{2}=\frac{64}{2}=32$$

Find the cumulative frequencies (cf) class‐wise:

• 160–162 : cf = 12

• 162–164 : cf = 12+15 = 27

• 164–166 : cf = 27+24 = 51

• 166–168 : cf = 51+13 = 64

Identify the median class:

We look for the class in which the 32nd observation lies.

• 27 < 32 ≤ 51, so the median class is 164–166.

Note down for the median class:

l = lower boundary = 164 cm

cf ₀ = cumulative frequency before median class = 27

fₘ = frequency of median class = 24

h = class-width = 166 − 164 = 2 cm

Apply the NCERT formula for median of grouped data:

$$ \text{Median} = l \;+\;\frac{\bigl(\tfrac{N}{2}-\text{cf}_{0}\bigr)}{f_{m}}\;\times h $$

Substituting,

$$ \text{Median} =164+\frac{(32-27)}{24}\times2 =164+\frac{5}{24}\times2 =164+\frac{10}{24} =164+0.4167 \approx164.42\text{ cm} $$

Hence the required height of the class is approximately

164.41 cm.

Answer: Option C.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question: The height which occurs most frequently in the class is
questions.solution:
The given distribution is

Height (cm) Number of students

160–162  12

162–164  15

164–166  24 ← modal class

166–168  13

Modal class is the class with the highest frequency, i.e. 164–166.

In grouped data, Mode is given by the formula

$$\text{Mode}=l+\frac{f_1-f_0}{2f_1-(f_0+f_2)}\times h$$

where

• $l$ = lower limit of modal class = 164

• $h$ = class–width = 166–164 = 2

• $f_1$ = frequency of modal class = 24

• $f_0$ = frequency of the class before modal class = 15

• $f_2$ = frequency of the class after modal class = 13

Substitute the values:

$$\text{Mode}=164+\frac{24-15}{2\cdot24-(15+13)}\times2 =164+\frac{9}{48-28}\times2 =164+\frac{9}{20}\times2 =164+0.9 =164.9\text{ cm}$$

Therefore, the height which occurs most frequently is 164.9 cm.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question: The most appropriate graphical representation of the given frequency distribution is
questions.solution:
Data on heights are quantitative and given in continuous class‐intervals. For such data, NCERT recommends using a histogram, where:

The horizontal axis (x‐axis) represents the class‐intervals (e.g. 160–162, 162–164, …).

The vertical axis (y‐axis) represents the frequency of students in each class.

Bars are drawn adjacent to each other (no gaps) with heights equal to the frequencies.

Hence, the most appropriate graphical representation is a histogram (Option C).

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question: Which one of the following is correct?
options: Cannot be determined from the given data
questions.solution:
Let the number of observations be $n=50$.

Compute the arithmetic means

$ \bar X = \frac{\sum X}{n} = \frac{200}{50} = 4,\qquad \bar Y = \frac{\sum Y}{n} = \frac{250}{50} = 5. $

Recall the formula for (population) variance:

$ \sigma^2_X = \frac{\sum X^2}{n} - (\bar X)^2,\quad \sigma^2_Y = \frac{\sum Y^2}{n} - (\bar Y)^2. $

Calculate $\sigma^2_X$:

$ \sigma^2_X = \frac{900}{50} - 4^2 = 18 - 16 = 2. $

Calculate $\sigma^2_Y$:

$ \sigma^2_Y = \frac{1400}{50} - 5^2 = 28 - 25 = 3. $

Compare variances:

$\sigma^2_X = 2$ and $\sigma^2_Y = 3$, so

$ \sigma^2_X < \sigma^2_Y. $

Answer: Option B (Variance $(X)$ < Variance $(Y)$).

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question: Which one of the following statements is correct?
options: Coefficient of variation of X is strictly more than coefficient of variation of Y.
Coefficient of variation of X is strictly less than coefficient of variation of Y
Coefficient of variation of X is same as coefficient of variation of Y.
Coefficient of variation cannot be determined from the given data.
questions.solution:
The Coefficient of Variation (C.V.) tells us how much variation there is in the data compared to the average. It is calculated using the formula:

$ C.V.(X) = \frac{\sigma_X}{M_X} \times 100 $

Now, let's use the given values:

The computation for $ \text{C.V.}(X) $ is:

$ C.V.(X) = \frac{\sqrt{2}}{50} \times 100 = 2\sqrt{2} $

And the Coefficient of Variation for Y is:

$ C.V.(Y) = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{50} \times 100 = 2\sqrt{3} $

This means $ \text{C.V.}(X) $ is less than $ \text{C.V.}(Y) $.

Hence, the correct answer is Option B.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question: The arithmetic mean of 100 observations is 50. If 5 is subtracted from each observation and then divided by 20, then what is the new arithmetic mean?
questions.solution:
Compute the total sum of the original observations.

Since the arithmetic mean of 100 observations $x_i$ is 50,

$$\sum_{i=1}^{100} x_i = 100 \times 50 = 5000.$$

Define the transformed observations.

For each $i$,

$$y_i = \frac{x_i - 5}{20}.$$

Find the new arithmetic mean:

$$\bar y = \frac{1}{100}\sum_{i=1}^{100} y_i = \frac{1}{100}\sum_{i=1}^{100}\frac{x_i - 5}{20} = \frac{1}{20}\Bigl(\frac{1}{100}\sum_{i=1}^{100}x_i\Bigr) - \frac{5}{20}.$$

Substitute $\tfrac{1}{100}\sum x_i = 50$:

$$\bar y = \frac{50}{20} - \frac{5}{20} = 2.5 - 0.25 = 2.25.$$

Answer: 2.25 (Option A)

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question: The standard deviation of 100 observations is 10. If 5 is added to each observation and then divided by 20, then what will be the new standard deviation?
questions.solution:
A linear transformation of the form

$$Y_i = \frac{X_i + 5}{20}$$

can be seen as

$$Y_i = \frac{1}{20}X_i \;+\;\frac{5}{20}\,. $$

Adding a constant ($+5/20$) shifts all values but does not change the spread (standard deviation).

Multiplying by $\tfrac{1}{20}$ scales the standard deviation by $|\tfrac{1}{20}|$.

Original standard deviation is 10, so new standard deviation is

$$10 \times \frac{1}{20} = 0.5.$$

Answer: Option B (0.5)

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 13 April 2025
questions.question: What is the arithmetic mean of 82,92,102,...,152?
questions.solution:
To find the arithmetic mean (average) of the numbers $8^2, 9^2, 10^2, ..., 15^2$, follow these steps:

1. List all the numbers:

The numbers are the squares of integers from $8$ to $15$.

There are $15 - 8 + 1 = 8$ numbers: $8^2, 9^2, 10^2, 11^2, 12^2, 13^2, 14^2, 15^2$.

2. Find the sum of these numbers:

Use the formula for the sum of squares of the first $n$ natural numbers:

$$ 1^2 + 2^2 + \ldots + n^2 = \frac{n(n+1)(2n+1)}{6} $$

To get the sum from $8^2$ to $15^2$, compute the sum up to $15^2$ and subtract the sum up to $7^2$:

$$ S = (8^2 + 9^2 + \ldots + 15^2) = (1^2 + 2^2 + \ldots + 15^2) - (1^2 + 2^2 + \ldots + 7^2) $$

3. Compute each part:

Sum up to $15^2$:

$$ S_{15} = \frac{15 \times 16 \times 31}{6} = \frac{7440}{6} = 1240 $$

Sum up to $7^2$:

$$ S_7 = \frac{7 \times 8 \times 15}{6} = \frac{840}{6} = 140 $$

So,

$$ S = 1240 - 140 = 1100 $$

4. Find the arithmetic mean:

Divide the sum by the number of terms ($8$):

$$ \text{Mean} = \frac{1100}{8} = 137.5 $$

5. Answer:

The arithmetic mean is 137.5.

Option C is correct.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14th September 2025
questions.question: The standard deviation of 100 observations is 10. If 20 is added to each observation, then what will be the new standard deviation?
questions.solution:
If the same number is added to each observation, the standard deviation does not change.

Here, $20$ is added to each of the $100$ observations.

So, the new standard deviation will remain:

$$10$$

Therefore, the correct answer is:

$$\boxed{10}$$

Option 1

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14th September 2025
questions.question:
The frequency distribution of the marks obtained by students in a Science examination is given below:
 
Marks 5-15
 		 15-25
 		 25-35
 		 35-45
 
Number of
students		 20 30 30 20
What is the arithmetic mean?
questions.solution:
To find the arithmetic mean of grouped data, we use the formula

$$ \bar{x} = \frac{\sum f_i x_i}{\sum f_i} $$

where:

  • $f_i$ = frequency

  • $x_i$ = class mark of each class interval

Step 1: Find the class marks

Class mark $x_i = \frac{\text{lower limit} + \text{upper limit}}{2}$

So,

  • For $5-15$: $x_1 = \frac{5+15}{2} = 10$

  • For $15-25$: $x_2 = \frac{15+25}{2} = 20$

  • For $25-35$: $x_3 = \frac{25+35}{2} = 30$

  • For $35-45$: $x_4 = \frac{35+45}{2} = 40$

Step 2: Multiply frequency by class mark

$ \begin{array}{|c|c|c|} \hline \text{Class Interval} & f_i & f_i x_i \\ \hline 5-15 & 20 & 20 \times 10 = 200 \\ 15-25 & 30 & 30 \times 20 = 600 \\ 25-35 & 30 & 30 \times 30 = 900 \\ 35-45 & 20 & 20 \times 40 = 800 \\ \hline \end{array} $

Now,

$$ \sum f_i = 20+30+30+20 = 100 $$

and

$$ \sum f_i x_i = 200+600+900+800 = 2500 $$

Step 3: Find the mean

$$ \bar{x} = \frac{2500}{100} = 25 $$

So, the arithmetic mean is

$$ \boxed{25} $$

Therefore, the correct option is Option 2.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 14th September 2025
questions.question:
Consider the following statements:

I. Mean and variance have the same unit of measurement.

II. Mean deviation and standard deviation have the same unit of measurement.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Analysis of Statements

Evaluating Statement I: "Mean and variance share the same unit of measurement."

  • The unit of the mean is identical to the unit used in the original dataset.
  • Because the calculation of variance involves squaring the deviations from the mean, its unit is the square of the original data's unit.
  • Since these units differ, Statement I is false.

Evaluating Statement II: "Mean deviation and standard deviation share the same unit of measurement."

  • The mean deviation is calculated using the absolute values of deviations, meaning its unit remains the same as the data.
  • The standard deviation is derived by taking the square root of the variance, which reverts the unit back to the original scale of the data.
  • Because both metrics share the same unit as the original dataset, Statement II is true.

Conclusion: Based on the evaluation above, the correct choice is Option 2.

questions.title: BITSAT 2025
questions.question: 1. If $p, q, r$ are in GP and the line $p x+q y+r=0$ forms a triangle with the coordinate axes, what is the area of the triangle if $p=2, q=4$ and $r=8$ ?
questions.solution:
The $x$-intercept is $-\frac{r}{p}=-\frac{8}{2}=-4$. and the $y$-intercept is $\frac{-r}{q}=\frac{-8}{4}=-2$

$$ \begin{aligned} \text { Thus, the area of the triangle } & =\frac{1}{2} \times x_{\text {intercept }} \times y_{\text {intercept }} \\ & =\frac{1}{2} \times 4 \times 2=4 \end{aligned} $$

questions.title: BITSAT 2025
questions.question: If the equation $2 h x y+2 g x+2 f y+c=0$ represents two straight lines, then the area of rectangle formed with the coordinates axes is
questions.solution:
The equation $2 h x y+2 g x+2 f y+c=0$ will represents a pair of straight lines if

$$ \begin{aligned} & 0+2 f g h-0 \times f^2-0 \times g^2-c h^2=0 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad 2 f g=c h \\ & \Rightarrow \quad c=\frac{2 f g}{h} \\ & \quad 2 h x y+2 g x+2 f y+\frac{2 f g}{h}=0 \\ & \Rightarrow h^2 x y+g h x+f h y+f g=0 \\ & \Rightarrow h y(h x+f)+g(h x+f)=0 \end{aligned} $$

$$ \Rightarrow(h x+f)(h y+g)=0 $$

Thus, the equations of lines represented by the given equation are $h x+f=0$ and $h y+g=0$.

These two lines and the coordinate axes form a rectangle $O A B C$.

∴ Area of rectangle $O A B C=O A \times O C$

$$ =\left|\frac{-f}{h}\right| \times\left|\frac{-g}{h}\right|=\frac{|f g|}{h^2} $$

questions.title: BITSAT 2025
questions.question: If four lines $a x \pm b y \pm c=0$ encloses a rhombus, then the area is
options:
$\frac{1}{a b c}$ sq. units

$2 a b c$ sq. units

$\frac{2 c^2}{a b}$ sq. units

None of these
questions.solution:
$$ \begin{aligned} &\text { The equations of the four lines are }\\ &\begin{aligned} & a x+b y+c=0 \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,...(i)\\ & a x-b y+c=0 \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,...(ii)\\ & a x+b y-c=0 \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,...(iii)\\ & a x-b y-c=0\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,...(iv) \end{aligned} \end{aligned} $$

Clearly, lines (i) and (iii) are parallel. Also, lines (ii) and (iv) are also parallel.

So, the given lines form a parallelogram whose vertices are $A\left(\frac{c}{a}, 0\right), B\left(0, \frac{c}{b}\right), C\left(\frac{-c}{a}, 0\right)$ and $D\left(0, \frac{-c}{b}\right)$.

Clearly, diagonal $A C$ and $B D$ of this 11 gm are at right angle to each other. So, the given lines form a rhombus $A B C D$.

$$ \begin{aligned} \therefore \text { Area of rhombus } A B C D & =\frac{1}{2}(A C \times B D) \\ & =\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{2 c}{a} \times \frac{2 c}{b}\right)=\frac{2 c^2}{a b} \text { sq. units } \end{aligned} $$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2025 Paper 2 Online
questions.question:
Let S denote the locus of the point of intersection of the pair of lines

$4x - 3y = 12\alpha$,

$4\alpha x + 3\alpha y = 12$,

where $\alpha$ varies over the set of non-zero real numbers. Let T be the tangent to S passing through the points $(p, 0)$ and $(0, q)$, $q > 0$, and parallel to the line $4x - \frac{3}{\sqrt{2}} y = 0$.

Then the value of $pq$ is :
questions.solution:
$\left.\begin{array}{c}4 x-3 y=12 \alpha \\ 4 x+3 y=\frac{12}{\alpha}\end{array}\right\} \rightarrow 16 x^2-9 y^2=144$

$\begin{aligned} & \frac{x^2}{9}-\frac{y^2}{16}=1 \rightarrow \text { Curve :S } \\ & T: y=m x \pm \sqrt{9 m^2-16} \\ & m=\frac{4 \sqrt{2}}{3} \\ & y=\frac{4 \sqrt{2} x}{3} \pm \sqrt{32-16} \\ & 3 y=4 \sqrt{2} x \pm 12 \\ & \text { as } q>0 \\ & 3 y=4 \sqrt{2} x+12\end{aligned}$

$$ \mathrm{p}=-\frac{3}{\sqrt{2}} \quad \& \quad \mathrm{q}=4 $$

$$ \mathrm{pq}=-6 \sqrt{2} $$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2025 Paper 2 Online
questions.question:
Adsorption of phenol from its aqueous solution on to fly ash obeys Freundlich isotherm. At a given temperature, from $10 \mathrm{mg} \mathrm{g}^{-1}$ and $16 \mathrm{mg} \mathrm{g}^{-1}$ aqueous phenol solutions, the concentrations of adsorbed phenol are measured to be $4 \mathrm{mg} \mathrm{g}^{-1}$ and $10 \mathrm{mg} \mathrm{g}^{-1}$, respectively. At this temperature, the concentration (in $\mathrm{mg} \mathrm{g}^{-1}$ ) of adsorbed phenol from $20 \mathrm{mg} \mathrm{g}^{-1}$ aqueous solution of phenol will be ______________.

Use: $\log _{10} 2=0.3$
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{x}{m}=K(C)^{\frac{1}{n}} \\\\ & 4=K(10)^{\frac{1}{n}}........(i) \\\\ & 10=K(16)^{\frac{1}{n}}.......(ii)\end{aligned}$

From (ii) and (i)

$$ \begin{aligned} & \log 4-\log 10=\frac{1}{n}(\log 10-\log 16) \\ & 2 \log 2-1=\frac{1}{n}(1-4 \log 2) \\ & 0.6-1=\frac{1}{x}(1-1.2) \\ & -0.4=\frac{-0.2}{n} \\ & n=\frac{2}{4}=\left(\frac{1}{2}\right) \end{aligned} $$

$\begin{aligned} \text { Now } 10 & =K(16)^2 \\ K & =\frac{10}{256} \\ \text { So, } \frac{x}{m} & =K(20)^2 \\ \frac{x}{m} & =\frac{10}{256} \times 400 \\ \frac{x}{m} & =15.625\end{aligned}$

or

$$ \begin{aligned} & 4=K(10)^2 \\ & K=\frac{4}{100} \\ & \frac{x}{m}=K(20)^2 \\ & \frac{x}{m}=\frac{4}{100} \times 400 \\ & \frac{x}{m}=16 \end{aligned} $$

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: Confident and Confidant
options: Confident means certain and confidant means close friend
Confidant means certain and confident means close friend
Confident means belief and confidant means an emissary
Confidant means assurance and confidant means intimate person
questions.solution:
Confident (with a 't' at the end) is usually an adjective. It describes a feeling of certainty about something or about yourself. Like, you might feel confident about passing an exam, or you might be a confident public speaker.

Confidant (with a 't' at the end for a male, or sometimes 'confidante' with 'te' for a female, but 'confidant' is often used for both) is a noun. It refers to a person that you trust deeply, often someone you share secrets or private matters with. They are your trusted companion or close friend.

Now let's look at the options:

Option 1: Confident means certain and confidant means close friend

Yes, "Confident" means certain or sure of yourself.

Yes, a "Confidant" is someone you're close to and trust with secrets, like a close friend. This option seems spot on.

Option 2: Confidant means certain and confident means close friend

No, "Confidant" is a person, not the feeling of certainty.

No, "Confident" describes a state of mind or attitude, not a person.

Option 3: Confident means belief and confidant means an emissary

"Confident" does involve belief (in oneself or something), but "certain" is a more direct description.

"Confidant" is definitely not an emissary (a messenger or representative).

Option 4: Confidant means assurance and confidant means intimate person

This option has a typo, using "confidant" twice. Even if we interpret the first part as "Confident means assurance", while related, "certain" is better. The second part, "confidant means intimate person", is a good description, but the option structure is flawed, and the first part isn't the best fit anyway.

Based on the standard meanings, Option 1 captures the distinction perfectly.

So, the most appropriate description is:

Option 1: Confident means certain and confidant means close friend

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: Broke and brook
options: Broke means abundance and brook means a flowing water body
Broke means bankrupt and brook means a stream
Broke means fractured and brook means nonsense
Broke means negotiate and brook means a stream
questions.solution:
Broke: This word usually means having no money, being bankrupt, or something that is fractured or no longer whole.

Brook: This word most commonly refers to a small stream of water. It can also be used as a verb meaning to tolerate or allow something.

Looking at the options provided:

Option 1: "Broke means abundance" is incorrect.

Option 3: "Brook means nonsense" is incorrect.

Option 4: "Broke means negotiate" is incorrect.

Option 2 fits best:

Broke can definitely mean bankrupt (having no money).

Brook means a stream (a small flowing water body).

So, the option that most appropriately describes the meaning of both words is Option 2.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: Accept and Except
options: Accept means apart from and except means recognise
Accept means recognise and except means include
Accept means consent and except means apart from
Accept means allude and except means apart from
questions.solution:
Accept: This word is a verb. It means to receive something willingly, to agree to something, or to consider something as true or suitable. Think of it like saying "yes" to receiving something or agreeing with something. A common meaning is 'to consent'.

Except: This word is usually a preposition or conjunction. It means "apart from," "excluding," or "but." It's used to indicate what is not included in a general statement.

Looking at the options provided:

Accept means apart from (No, that's 'except') and except means recognise (No, that's 'accept').

Accept means recognise (Could be, but not the primary or best fit here) and except means include (Definitely not, 'except' means exclude).

Accept means consent (Yes, that's a good fit) and except means apart from (Yes, that's a perfect fit).

Accept means allude (No, 'allude' means to hint at) and except means apart from (Yes, but 'accept' is wrong).

Based on the meanings, Option 3 describes both words most appropriately.

So, the option that correctly describes the meaning of both words is:

Option 3: Accept means consent and except means apart from

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: Accord and Accrued
options: Accord means collected and accrued means agreement
Accord means agreement and accrued means arrived
Accord means accepted and accrued means received
Accord means agreement and accrued means accumulated
questions.solution:
Accord: This word generally means agreement or harmony. Think of reaching an accord on a peace treaty – everyone agrees.

Accrued: This is the past tense of "accrue." It means something has accumulated or gathered over time, often like interest adding up on a loan or benefits building up.

Looking at your options:

Accord means collected and accrued means agreement - Not quite right.

Accord means agreement and accrued means arrived - Accrued isn't about arriving.

Accord means accepted and accrued means received - Accepted is close for accord, but accrued is better described as accumulated.

Accord means agreement and accrued means accumulated - This matches our understanding perfectly!

So, the option that most appropriately describes the meaning of both words is Option 4.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: Guarantee and Warranty
options: Guarantee is an agreement and warranty is a service contract
Guarantee is a business agreement and warranty is a service contract
Guarantee is a promise and warranty is a service contract
Guarantee is acceptance and warranty is a service contract
questions.solution:
Think about how "guarantee" and "warranty" are usually used when you buy something.

A guarantee is often a more general assurance or promise from the seller. It's a strong declaration of quality or performance. Like a "money-back guarantee" – that's a straightforward promise. So, "Guarantee is a promise" feels right.

A warranty is typically a specific written agreement that comes with a product. It outlines what the seller will do if the product is defective within a certain time period – often involving repairs or replacement, which are forms of "service." So, thinking of a "warranty" as a type of "service contract" makes sense in this context, as it's a contract promising a remedy (like service) under specific conditions.

Looking at the options:

"Guarantee is an agreement and warranty is a service contract" - Both are agreements, so this isn't very distinguishing for the guarantee.

"Guarantee is a business agreement and warranty is a service contract" - Similar issue as option 1.

"Guarantee is a promise and warranty is a service contract" - This fits well with the common understanding. A guarantee is a strong promise, and a warranty is a contractual promise for potential future service (repair/replacement).

"Guarantee is acceptance and warranty is a service contract" - "Acceptance" doesn't describe what a guarantee is.

Based on how these words are commonly used and legally understood, Option 3 provides the most appropriate description for both terms out of the choices given.

So, the option that best fits is:

$$ \text{Guarantee is a promise and warranty is a service contract} $$

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: The Constitution of India ensures proportionate representation from all regions.
questions.solution:
Key Points

The term 'proportionate' refers to something that is corresponding in size or amount to something else, forming a proportion. In Hindi, it is termed as "आनुपातिक".

Example: The punishments were proportionate to the crimes committed.

'Balanced' means something that is stable and in fair proportion, suggesting that elements are properly or appropriately distributed. In Hindi, it is "संतुलित".

Example: A balanced diet includes a variety of food groups.

Therefore, the correct answer is "balanced".

Additional Information

Here are explanations for the other options, along with their meanings in Hindi and example sentences:

Partial (आंशिक): Exists only in part; incomplete.

Example: She made a partial payment on her loan.

Unlikely (असंभव): Improbable or not likely to happen.

Example: It is unlikely that he will arrive before noon.

Suffragette (मताधिकार समर्थक महिला): A woman advocating for the right to vote through organized protest.

Example: The suffragettes fought bravely for women's voting rights.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: There is a feeling of disenchantment among the members of the group.
questions.solution:
That sentence talks about a feeling of disenchantment among the group members.

Let's think about what that word means. When you're "enchanted," you feel charmed or really excited about something, almost like it's magical. So, "disenchantment" is the opposite – it's that feeling when the magic wears off, and you feel let down because things weren't as great or exciting as you thought they would be.

Looking at the options:

delight: That's feeling really happy or pleased, which is the opposite of disenchantment.

disappointment: This is a feeling of sadness or displeasure because your hopes or expectations weren't met. This fits perfectly!

idealism: This is about having high hopes or believing in perfect ideas, but it's not the feeling of being let down.

unrelenting: This means not stopping or easing up, which doesn't relate to a feeling about expectations.

So, the word that's nearest in meaning to disenchantment is disappointment.

The correct option is Option 2.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: She believed that it was imminent that he would be chosen as the leader of the group.
questions.solution:
The sentence is: "She believed that it was imminent that he would be chosen as the leader of the group."

The word imminent usually means something that is about to happen very soon. It often carries a sense of being close at hand, perhaps even unavoidable.

Now let's look at the options given:

timely: This means happening at the right or most suitable time. It doesn't quite capture the idea of happening very soon.

distant: This means far away in time or space. This is actually the opposite of imminent.

unlikely: This means not likely to happen. Imminent suggests it is going to happen, and soon, so this is also incorrect.

inevitable: This means certain to happen; unavoidable.

When someone believes something is imminent, especially something like being chosen for a role, they often feel it's not just happening soon, but that it's practically guaranteed or unavoidable at that time. Among the options, "inevitable" is the word that best captures this sense of impending certainty that is often part of what makes something seem "imminent". It suggests that the event is so sure to happen that it's right around the corner.

So, the most appropriate option nearest in meaning to imminent in this context is inevitable.

The correct option is Option 4.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: The brave soldiers left an indelible impression on the people of the land.
questions.solution:
Let's think about what "indelible" usually means. It's often used for things like ink that won't wash out, or memories that you can't forget. So, it means something that cannot be removed, washed away, or erased.

Now let's look at the options:

permanent: This means lasting forever or for a very long time, something that stays.

fleeting: This means lasting for a very short time, quickly passing.

hilarious: This means very funny.

eradicable: This means capable of being removed or destroyed.

Comparing "indelible" to these options, the word that is nearest in meaning, especially when talking about an impression that won't go away, is permanent.

So, the most appropriate option is permanent.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
questions.question: The manager always provides instantaneous replies to all queries.
questions.solution:
The word "instantaneous" means happening right away, without any delay.

Looking at the options:

Option 2: delayed - This means happening later.

Option 3: deliberate - This means done intentionally or planned.

Option 4: unwanted - This means not desired.

Comparing these to "instantaneous," the word that is nearest in meaning is immediate.

So, the most appropriate option is Option 1.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
questions.question:
Directions :In the following items, a pair of words is provided. You are required to select the option that most appropriately describes the meaning of both the words and mark your response on the Answer Sheet accordingly 

Overview and Review
options: Overview means assessment and review means general idea
Overview means generai idea and review means assessment
Overview means general analysis and review means go through
Overview means summary and review means evaluation
questions.solution:
To find the correct option, let us understand the exact meanings of both words step by step.

Step 1: Understand the meaning of "Overview"

The word 'Overview' refers to a broad or general view of a subject. It provides the main points without going into deep details. For example, before starting a new chapter, a teacher might give an overview of the topic. Therefore, an overview simply gives a general idea of something.

Step 2: Understand the meaning of "Review"

The word 'Review' means to examine or study something carefully, usually to make a judgment, find mistakes, or suggest improvements. For example, a teacher might review your test paper to check your performance. Therefore, a review involves a formal and detailed assessment.

Step 3: Analyze the given options

  • Option 1: States that overview is an assessment and review is a general idea. This is incorrect because it completely reverses the meanings of the two words.

  • Option 2: States that overview means a general idea and review means assessment. This perfectly matches the distinct definitions we discussed.

  • Option 3: Uses the terms "general analysis" and "go through". An overview is not an analysis (which requires detailed study), making this incorrect.

  • Option 4: Uses "summary" and "evaluation". While close, Option 2 is the most precise and standard definition used in English grammar to contrast the broadness of an overview (general idea) with the critical examination of a review (assessment).

Therefore, the option that most appropriately describes both words is Option 2.

Correct Answer:

Option 2: Overview means general idea and review means assessment
